title: perf: parallel tree-sitter parsing via rayon + in-memory SQLite
description:
cmd_parse walks files sequentially and writes to disk SQLite throughout. Two changes:

1. **Rayon parallel parse**: Split project_file into a pure function returning ParsedFile struct (nodes, ast_entries, refs). Parse files with rayon par_iter. Batch-insert results sequentially into SQLite in one transaction.

2. **In-memory SQLite**: For fresh parses (non-incremental), build the .db in :memory: and serialize to disk at end. Avoids WAL overhead during indexing.

The ExtractedRef + extract_refs factory (already merged) makes this possible — extraction is pure data, no DB access needed during parallel phase.

Expected: 4-8x speedup on multi-core (sequential parse ~80s → ~10-20s for 190 Go files).

title: perf: parallel tree-sitter parsing via rayon
description:
cmd_parse walks files sequentially — one file at a time through tree-sitter. For 190 files this takes ~80s.

Fix: use rayon par_iter to parse files in parallel. Each file is independent (own AST, own source). Collect results, then batch-insert into SQLite in a single transaction.

codebase-memory-mcp uses a C worker pool (pass_parallel.c + worker_pool.c) for this. Rayon is the Rust equivalent.

Expected: 4-8x speedup on multi-core machines (80s → 10-20s).

title: perf: don't store _source BLOBs — use file path + byte range references instead
description:
The _source table stores full file content as BLOBs. For mache's 190 Go files this is 352MB — 99% of the .db size. The arena has to be 700MB+ to fit it.

Fix: store (source_id, path, size, hash) in _source instead of (source_id, language, content). Consumers read the actual file from disk when they need content. The _ast table already has byte ranges (start_byte, end_byte) that reference into the source file.

Expected impact: .db drops from ~352MB to ~20MB. Arena fits in 64MB default. Parse time drops (no large BLOB writes).

codebase-memory-mcp uses the same approach — their graph doesn't store source content, only references.

title: Daemon: default --control and --arena paths to ~/.mache/ when not specified
description: Currently the daemon creates leyline.ctrl and leyline.arena in the current directory. Mache's control package has a security check that only allows paths under ~/.mache/ or /tmp. Default to ~/.mache/default.ctrl and ~/.mache/default.arena when --control and --arena are not explicitly specified.

title: fix: auto-size arena after parse — respect ADR-001 tiered strategy
description:
## Problem

The daemon's `--arena-size-mib` defaults to 64MB. For large projects (e.g., mache with 200+ Go files), the parsed .db exceeds the 32MB buffer capacity (64MB arena = 4KB header + two 32MB buffers). Users have to manually guess `--arena-size-mib`.

## Root cause

ADR-001 (memory arena design) describes a tiered sizing strategy: linear for <2MB, demand-paged for large. The current implementation uses a fixed default with no auto-sizing.

## Fix

After `cmd_parse` produces the .db, check its size. If it exceeds the arena's buffer capacity, grow the arena to fit:

```rust
let db_size = std::fs::metadata(&db_path)?.len();
let min_arena = ArenaHeader::HEADER_SIZE + db_size * 2 + (1024 * 1024); // header + 2 buffers + 1MB margin
let arena_bytes = arena_bytes.max(min_arena);
```

This respects the 64MB default for small projects but auto-grows for large ones. The arena file is sparse on APFS/ext4, so oversizing costs no physical storage until written.

## Why not change the default

The 64MB default is intentional per ADR-001 — it fits in a single huge page (2MB) boundary region and works for most single-file projections. Changing it would affect the network transport path (ley-line private) which sizes arenas to match sender expectations.

title: Git-aware incremental reparse: use git status/diff to determine what changed
description:
The daemon should use git to determine what needs reparsing:

1. On startup: `git status --porcelain` → untracked + modified files → parse those first
2. On file change: `git diff --name-only HEAD` → only reparse changed files
3. On branch switch: `git diff --name-only HEAD@{1}..HEAD` → bulk reparse the delta
4. Store last-parsed commit SHA in the .db metadata → skip files unchanged since then

Categories:
- new (untracked): parse from scratch, add to .db
- modified: reparse, update _ast + node_refs + node_defs for that file
- deleted: remove from .db
- unchanged: skip

The git SHA becomes the cache key. If the .db was built at commit abc123 and HEAD is now abc456, only files in the diff need reparsing. This makes subsequent daemon starts near-instant for clean repos.

comments.text: Subsumed by decade ley-line-open-eb0252 (living daemon). Git-aware reparse is step 3 of the daemon lifecycle.
comments.author: rsry-mcp

title: Readiness signal: daemon notifies mache when initial parse + arena load is complete
description:
mache starts before daemon finishes parsing → gets empty graph. Need a signal.

Options:
1. UDS op: `{"op": "status"}` → `{"state": "ready", "generation": 3, "files_parsed": 190}`
2. Control block field: `ready` flag alongside generation
3. Arena convention: generation 0 = not ready, generation > 0 = has data

mache's HotSwapGraph poller already detects generation bumps. The simplest fix: daemon doesn't bump generation until first parse is complete. mache waits (already has 30s timeout in buildControlGraph). But mache should surface "still loading" to MCP clients instead of returning empty results.

comments.text: Subsumed by decade ley-line-open-eb0252 (living daemon). Readiness signal is step 1 of the daemon lifecycle.
comments.author: rsry-mcp

title: feat: open daemon with UDS protocol + DaemonExt trait
description:
Add a `daemon` subcommand to leyline-cli-lib that runs the full coordination daemon: arena + mount + UDS socket + event router. Base ops (status, flush, load, query, reparse, subscribe) use only open crates. ley-line (private) extends via a `DaemonExt` trait to add sheaf, embed, network ops.

This makes the daemon an LLO concern, not an LL concern. Mache talks to the socket regardless of which tier is behind it. LL becomes "more ops on the same socket" rather than "a different architecture."

See spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-10-open-daemon-design.md

title: epic: LLO as composable base layer — LL enriches, mache consumes
description:
## Problem

ley-line-open (LLO) and ley-line (LL) have no defined composition model. LLO produces base .db files (nodes + _ast + _source). LL has additional capabilities (LSP enrichment, embeddings, sheaf cache) that should layer on top. Mache should be ignorant of which produced the .db — it just checks for table presence.

## Design Principle

The .db file IS the interface contract. Tables are the API:
- **Base (LLO):** nodes, _ast, _source — always present
- **LSP enrichment (LL or LLO lsp crate):** _lsp, _lsp_hover, _lsp_defs, _lsp_refs — optional
- **Cross-refs (LL or mache build):** node_refs, node_defs — optional
- **Embeddings (LL only):** vector index sidecar — optional
- **Sheaf (LL only):** cache/invalidation metadata — optional

Mache never imports LL or LLO. It queries sqlite_master for table presence and activates features accordingly. This is already how useNodesTable works — extend the pattern.

## Decomposition

1. [DONE] `ll-open parse` — base .db producer (bead ley-line-open-6dc367)
2. `ll-open enrich-lsp` — LSP enrichment subcommand using the lsp/ crate
3. Cross-ref extraction — populate node_refs/node_defs from _ast queries
4. Composition interface — define how LL layers its private enrichments on an LLO base .db without LLO needing to know about LL
5. Pipeline orchestration — who calls what in what order (ll-open parse → ll-open enrich-lsp → leyline enrich-embed → mache mount)

title: CLI binary: ll-open parse <source-dir> → output.db with _ast + _lsp* tables
description:
## What
Add a `ll-open` CLI binary that pre-bakes source code into a `.db` file with `_ast` tables (tree-sitter) and optionally `_lsp*` tables (LSP enrichment). This is the missing entry point for the pre-bake pipeline.

## Why
Currently mache ingests raw source via tree-sitter at mount time — 10.7s for 248 Go files. If ley-line-open pre-bakes the `.db` once, mache mounts it instantly via SQLiteGraph (nodes-table fast path). The library code exists (`lsp::project_lsp`, `ts` crate, `schema::create_schema`) but there's no CLI to invoke it.

## Desired workflow
```
ll-open parse ~/project --lang go --output project.db
  # produces project.db with:
  #   nodes table (id, parent_id, name, kind, size, mtime, record)
  #   _ast table (tree-sitter AST nodes)
  #   _lsp_defs, _lsp_refs, _lsp_hover (optional, if --lsp flag + running LSP server)
  #   node_refs (cross-reference index)

mache serve project.db
  # instant — SQLiteGraph nodes-table fast path, no tree-sitter, no CGO
```

## Architecture
- New `rs/ll-open/cli/` crate with `[[bin]]` target `ll-open`
- Subcommands: `parse` (source → db), maybe `enrich` (add LSP to existing db)
- `parse` walks source tree, runs tree-sitter via `ts` crate, writes `_ast` rows
- Schema projection via `schema::create_schema` + `schema::insert_node`
- Optional `--lsp` flag for LSP enrichment

## Connection to mache
- mache's `SelectWalker` auto-selects ASTWalker when `_ast` table present
- mache's `OpenSQLiteGraph` detects `nodes` table → fast path
- mache's `--control` reads from ley-line arena → hot-swap on rebuild

## Open questions
- Should the CLI also support incremental rebuilds (only re-parse changed files)?
- Should it produce a ley-line arena file directly, or a plain `.db` that mache can `leyline load`?
- Does the `ts` crate already produce `_ast` table format that ASTWalker expects?

comments.text:
Implemented ll-open CLI binary with `parse` subcommand. Files: rs/ll-open/cli/Cargo.toml, rs/ll-open/cli/src/main.rs.

- Walks source directory, detects language per file via TsLanguage::from_extension
- Multi-file support: node IDs scoped under file paths (e.g. `main.go/function_declaration_0/identifier`)
- Creates directory hierarchy nodes for path components
- Writes nodes + _ast + _source tables — mache's SQLiteGraph fast path compatible
- Tested on mache codebase: 191 Go files, 286K nodes, 0 errors
- Clippy clean, all existing tests pass
comments.author: rsry-mcp

Scoped crate (gearhash + leyline-core only) so the base dataplane pulls no CDC unless it opts in.

title: Research: Turso (MVCC SQLite-in-Rust) as the arena storage layer — solve the page/concurrency problem while keeping LLO's pieces
description:
User idea 2026-07-17: Turso's MVCC (multi-version concurrency control) likely solves "our page problem" — integrate it as the arena/storage substrate while retaining LLO's own layers (merkle CAS, _ast/_source/_lsp projection, sheaf, extraction epochs).

## The thesis to validate
Turso (turso.tech — the Rust rewrite of SQLite, successor lineage to libSQL) implements MVCC: concurrent readers + writers without SQLite's single-writer file lock, versioned pages instead of in-place mutation. LLO's arena today is a SQLite db (zero-copy via sqlite3_deserialize on the arena payload; mache reads it read-only mode=ro). The hypothesized "page problem" MVCC solves — CLARIFY with the user which of these it is:
- daemon write/read contention on the shared arena (the reference_leyline_daemon_pollution jitter class)?
- page-level lock contention during incremental re-extraction (extraction epochs re-derive facts — MVCC could make that concurrent-safe)?
- the frozen-_ast-until-re-parse gap the mache serve/mount + testfixtures leyline path documents (mache-37ae8b) — MVCC snapshots could give consistent reads during a live re-parse?
- something else.

## The constraint: "keep our pieces"
Turso would be the STORAGE/concurrency engine only. LLO must retain: the merkle node_hash CAS (content dedup), the projection schema (_ast/node_defs/node_refs/node_content/node_child/_source/_lsp*/_cfg), the sheaf substrate, extraction epochs, the validate/emit_ast daemon ops, the capnp wire (wire_format_major=1). Turso must be a drop-in for the SQLite page/txn layer WITHOUT changing the projected table contract mache consumes (mache reads via modernc.org/sqlite pure-Go driver + tableHasColumn probes — that contract is load-bearing across the ecosystem).

## Open questions
- Does Turso support sqlite3_deserialize / in-memory arena zero-copy, or does its storage model differ? (This is the crux — the arena zero-copy is central to LLO's design.)
- Wire/on-disk format compatibility: can mache's modernc.org/sqlite driver still open a Turso-produced db, or does Turso diverge from the SQLite file format? If it diverges, the whole consumer contract breaks.
- MVCC's cost vs benefit for LLO's mostly-read, batch-write workload.
- Turso maturity/production-readiness as of 2026-07.

## Deliverable
A design spike: benchmark the concurrency scenario ("page problem") on current SQLite-arena vs a Turso prototype; assess file-format compat with mache's pure-Go reader; recommend integrate / partial / no. Relates to the analysis-substrate storage layer + reference_leyline_daemon_pollution.

title: Git-replay stress test for restriction superset-correctness (rung-2 analog) [was f3a81e]
description: ADR-0031 caveat #3. The GO rests on 9 hand-picked fixtures + a structural sound-superset argument. A git-replay over real edits stresses superset-CORRECTNESS harder: find real edits where the review result changes but the naive restriction does NOT (false-skip = superset gap). Mirror the ADR-0030 rung-2 harness (rs/ll-open/sheaf/benches/git_replay_invalidation.rs): replay LLO's rs/ history, per edit compute (restriction_hash changed?, review_result changed?) via real extract_rust facts (reuse the restriction_cache module from the re-key bead), assert restriction false_skip_rate == 0 over N>=400 real region-edits. If any false-skip appears, report the exact edit + the dependency the restriction missed. Recreated after beads.db reset lost id f3a81e. Depends on the re-key bead (reusable module).

comments.text:
GO — git-replay superset-correctness stress complete. restriction false_skip == 0 over 1786 real region-edits.

## What was built
New bench `rs/ll-open/sheaf/benches/restriction_git_replay.rs` (harness=false), mirroring the ADR-0030 rung-2 replay pattern (`git_replay_invalidation.rs`) but over the reusable `restriction_cache` module (PR #242) + `tests/common` extraction of the REAL `extract_rust` facts. Registered in `sheaf/Cargo.toml`. Extraction support added to `tests/common/mod.rs`: public `review_targets()` + `stable_container_id()` (container_id's Stable arm refactored onto it, DRY) so a driver resolves a specific function region to the Stable container id its refs are grouped under — the name-keyed `container_by_fn` collides across a multi-file corpus.

## Method
Replay N commits touching rs/. Each commit's changed .rs files (that parse on BOTH parent and commit) form a MULTI-FILE corpus, so `review_call_targets` resolves call targets ACROSS files — exercising the cross-object dependency the fixture `corpus-def-rename` proved load-bearing. Build FactSubstrate before/after under ContainerKeying::Stable; for every function_item region present on both sides whose OWN bytes changed (a real region-edit), compute restriction-changed? (`restriction_for_call_target`) vs review-changed? (`review_call_targets`, the oracle). Same-signature targets (Stable-id collision, refs merge) excluded so each unit is exactly one function.

## Corpus + N (measured, RESTR_N_COMMITS=600)
- 577 commits touching rs/ scanned; 510 multi-file commit-corpora measured
- **1786 real region-edits** (>> the N>=400 bar)
- 1423 same-signature targets excluded (Stable body-invariant id collision — symmetric for restriction and review, so exclusion hides no gap)
- review CHANGED (oracle): 1034 | review UNCHANGED: 752

## Confusion matrix (restriction hash vs review oracle, Stable keying)
```
                        | review UNCHANGED  review CHANGED
  restriction UNCHANGED |            682             0     <- false_skip = 0
  restriction CHANGED   |             70          1034
```
- **restriction false_skip = 0** (false_skip_rate = 0/1034 = 0.0000)
- restriction true_skip = 682 (true_skip_rate = 682/752 = 90.7% of review-unchanged edits)
- wasted_inval (over-invalidation, sound) = 70
- true_inval = 1034
- **WholeObject true_skip = 0** by construction (the corpus always holds a changed file) — restriction recovers 682 sound skips WholeObject cannot, at 0 false-skips.

## Outcome
Zero false-skips over 1786 real region-edits => `restriction-unchanged ⇒ review-output-unchanged` SURVIVES replay. The ADR-0031 positive claim (rung-2 analog) holds on real edits, not just the 9 fixtures. No missed dependency found; nothing engineered away.

Scope note (honest): the unit is a region-edit (F's own bytes changed), over a per-commit multi-file corpus. The pure cross-file-def-rename class (F byte-identical, only a callee def in another file renamed) is out of this replay's region-edit population and remains fixture-covered (corpus-def-rename). Sweep the finding with RESTR_N_COMMITS higher if a broader corpus is wanted.

task ci: GREEN (exit 0) — fmt:check, clippy (-D warnings), check, test, blake3 lint, compat/server-json gates all pass. cargo fmt --all applied.

title: Re-key the review restriction on node_hash / stable container identity (deployment precondition) [was f38a86]
description:
ADR-0031 caveat #1 / deployment precondition. The restriction-addressed review-cache experiment (rs/ll-open/sheaf/tests/restriction_review_real.rs, on main via #240) keys review restrictions by container NAME (fn:score), not the daemon's positional node_id. A positional container id shifts on any line change above F → restriction degenerates to whole-file sensitivity (stays sound, loses true-skips). Fix: key on stable, reflow-invariant identity — ADR-0027 node_hash. Also graduate the restriction/review/oracle logic from the test into a reusable sheaf module so the git-replay + second-family beads build on it. Proof fixture: an edit inserting lines ABOVE F (F byte-identical) must NOT invalidate F's call-target restriction. Keep the 9 original fixtures green. Honest outcome allowed: if node_hash keying needs cmd_parse/daemon integration a sheaf test can't reach, report the exact interface. IN PROGRESS (agent bead-f38a86 running; recreated after beads.db reset lost id f38a86).

Verification: cargo test -p leyline-sheaf --test restriction_review_real (above-F fixture + 9 originals green); task ci.

comments.text:
Executed (was f38a86). Branch `worktree-agent-a7903e770a63df777`, commit `da5b41d`. NOT pushed, no PR. task ci green (fmt first), EXIT=0.

## Stable-identity approach used
Local stable node_hash-style STAND-IN, not the real cmd_parse fold (which is not reachable from a sheaf test — see integration gap below). The container identity is a bottom-up merkle fold in ADR-0027's exact shape (hash_leaf/hash_internal: domain ‖ tag ‖ uvarint(len(kind)) ‖ kind ‖ token/child-hashes, source order, spans OUT) computed over F's SIGNATURE subtree (function_item minus its `body` block).

Two things had to be true, and only signature-scoping gives both:
- reflow/position-invariant (node_hash property) → survives insert-above;
- BODY-invariant → the whole-function node_hash changes on ANY body edit, which would kill the headline body-only true-skips (local-rename, body-arith). Excluding the body block keeps those skips while still invalidating on a signature change. This is a real finding: the container key must be the node_hash of the function's DECLARATION/signature node, NOT the whole function_item.

## Above-F fixture: RED → GREEN (measured)
New test `stable_identity_survives_insert_above`: insert a new import + comment + whole new fn ABOVE `score`, `score` byte-identical.
- RED (temporarily forced Stable→positional id): FAILED at "stable node_hash keying must survive insert-above and skip soundly" — positional container id shifts function_item_0 → function_item_1, restriction hash changes, cache wastefully recomputes (ADR-0031 caveat-#1 whole-file degeneration).
- GREEN (real signature node_hash): PASS. Oracle confirms score's resolved call graph unchanged, so the skip is sound. Positional keying is asserted RED in the same test (`!restriction_unchanged(Positional)`).

## 9 original fixtures stay green under Stable keying
Verdict now runs under ContainerKeying::Stable (the deployment-sound identity), reproducing ADR-0031 exactly:
```
policy          false_skip_rate  true_skip_rate  recompute_saved
WholeObject                0/4             0/5                 0
AstShape                   4/4             4/5                 4  (ADR-0030 blind spot)
Restriction                0/4             5/5                 5
```
local-rename + body-arith: sound SKIP (restriction unchanged, review confirmed unchanged) while WholeObject recomputes. callee-swap / import-path / qualifier-swap / corpus-def-rename: correctly recompute. Cost test green (restriction 6 rows vs review 400→20k; cheaper wall-time at scale).

## Module surface exposed for f3a81e / f463aa
`leyline_sheaf::restriction_cache` (pub, parser-INDEPENDENT, ships in production — zero leyline-ts dep):
- rows: DefRow, RefRow, ImportRow
- FactSubstrate::from_rows(defs, refs, imports, container_by_fn) + container_for_fn(name)
- ContainerKeying { Name, Positional, Stable }
- restriction_for_call_target(sub, container, &mut rows_touched) -> [u8;32]
- review_call_targets(sub, container, &mut rows_touched) -> BTreeSet<ResolvedEdge>; ResolvedEdge
- whole_object_hash(corpus) -> [u8;32]
- Policy, FixtureResult, PolicyStats, stats
Parser-dependent extraction lives in tests/common/mod.rs (dev/bench shared): build_substrate(corpus, keying), ast_shape_hash, the node_hash fold. f3a81e (git-replay bench) and f463aa (second review family) reuse the src module + build_substrate without duplication.

## Integration gap (precise, for the follow-up)
The real ADR-0027 node_hash is computed by cmd_parse's `fold_children` (cli-lib), which depends on leyline-sheaf — so a sheaf test CANNOT call the production node_hash without a dependency inversion. The fold also produces node_hash per NAMED NODE; the container key needs the node_hash of the function's DECLARATION node specifically (whole function_item is body-sensitive). Two documented stand-in deviations in restriction_cache.rs / tests/common:
1. SHA-256 (sheaf's hash) not BLAKE3 — the container id is an opaque string; only reflow/body invariance matters for the experiment.
2. raw tree-sitter node.kind() not TsLanguage::canonical_kind (κ canonicalization is in leyline-ts).

What the daemon-side deploy needs (f38a86-follow-up interface): the fold already emits a node_hash for every named node onto _ast/node_content. To re-key the live restriction, expose the node_hash of the function's declaration/signature node (or add a signature-scoped node_hash to the fold) and store it as the node_refs container identity in place of the positional container_node_id. No new hashing primitive is required — hash_leaf/hash_internal already exist in cmd_parse; the change is (a) hash the declaration subtree not the whole function_item, and (b) thread that hash as the container key. Leaving bead OPEN.
comments.author: rsry-mcp

title: ADR-0031: restriction-addressed derived-view caching over CAS — positive construction after ADR-0030 NO-GO
description: Umbrella for the restriction-addressed-caching thread (context-substrate/restriction-addressed-caching). ADR-0030 killed the APPROXIMATE embedding-distance skip gate; this is the EXACT restriction-addressed variant that works: key an expensive derived-view (code-review fact) on the exact hash of the consumer's input closure over the real substrate (node_refs/node_defs/qualifier/container_node_id/_imports). Verified experiment landed on main (#240). ADR-0030 amended + ADR-0031 written (#241). Thesis: CAS gives exact identity for stored objects; restriction-addressed caching gives exact identity for consumer-observable derived views. Claim: restriction maps earned rent, cohomology did not. NOTE: recreated after a beads.db git-reset lost the original id ley-line-open-f62327 (referenced in ADR-0031 as the umbrella). Children: the re-key, git-replay, and second-family beads (also recreated).

title: Rung 3 — safety: node_hash floor sits under the δ⁰ skip decision; a wrong skip degrades to revalidation, never stale-served
description: ADR-0030 (d4b72e) proof ladder, rung 3 — the invariant that makes a lossy semantic optimizer safe. Not an experiment. Establish + test that node_hash (exact, content-addressed) is checked UNDER the sheaf δ⁰ skip decision, so a false negative (δ⁰ says 'close, skip' but the region genuinely changed) degrades to 'unnecessary revalidation that catches it', never 'stale facts served to a consumer'. Verify by injecting a deliberate δ⁰ false-negative in a test and asserting the consumer still receives correct facts. This is what makes rung 2's FN rate a performance cost rather than a correctness bug — and it is exactly the floor the extraction epoch (bead 20988a) + node_hash already provide. Can proceed in parallel with rung 1/2 (it's about the guard, not the optimizer).

title: Rung 2 — value: git-replay experiment, re-derivation as free oracle, measure true-skip vs uncaught-FN over EPS
description: ADR-0030 (d4b72e) proof ladder, rung 2 — the real proof. Gated on rung 1 (embedding-stalk-divergence) passing. Free ground-truth oracle: define 'meaningful change to a region' = 'the derived facts (node_defs/node_refs/CFG) actually changed', computable by re-derivation. Protocol: replay N real edits from git history; per touched region record (a) the δ⁰-tolerance skip/invalidate decision under HV stalks and (b) the oracle (did re-derived facts change?). Sweep EPS; tabulate confusion matrix. True-skip (δ⁰ skip, facts unchanged) = work saved = the payoff number. False-negative (δ⁰ skip, facts changed) = would-serve-stale = must be ~0 at a useful EPS (and is caught by the hash floor, rung 3). Baseline SHA gate: 0 FN, 0 true-skips. Success = an EPS band with meaningful true-skip and near-zero FN → math pays rent, with a number. Falsification: ROC is diagonal (skip decision uncorrelated with fact-stability) → surface-embedding distance does NOT predict derived-fact stability → decoration confirmed, ship the honest BFS. THIS is the crux question the whole ADR hinges on.

title: Rung 1 — divergence: HV/SimHash stalk makes δ⁰ diverge from the hash-gated BFS on a cosmetic edit (kill-switch)
description: ADR-0030 (d4b72e) proof ladder, rung 1. Cheapest real signal + kill switch. In a test harness, replace the SHA-256 stalk with an HDC hypervector (or Charikar SimHash) for a region; construct one cosmetic edit (rename a local, reflow whitespace) that preserves region meaning. Assert δ⁰(HV) < EPS ('close, skip') while hash(bytes) differs ('changed') — the two decisions DIVERGE, proving the math is no longer degenerate. Contrapositive is the kill switch: if no such divergence can be constructed even with HV stalks, the thesis is dead — stop, ship the honest BFS (bead 716c69 reframe), do NOT build rung 2. Binary outcome, ~1 day. Depends on 71b20c (rung 0 establishes the degenerate baseline).

title: ADR-0030: sheaf-over-embeddings — make δ⁰ load-bearing by swapping cryptographic stalks for locality-preserving ones (semantic incremental invalidation)
description:
Design + decision doc (docs/adr/0030). The necessity audit (716c69) proved the sheaf's δ⁰ cascade reduces to a hash-gated reverse-dep BFS on all OSS-live inputs, because live stalks are SHA-256 (avalanche → locality-destroying; only 'identical' vs 'randomly far', no sub-EPS continuum) and live restriction maps are static axis-aligned column masks.

Thesis: the substrate already contains the missing ingredient — rs/ll-open/hdc produces locality-PRESERVING representations (Charikar signed-projection = SimHash; HDC hypervectors via bytes_to_hv, similarity-preserving by cosine). Wiring those in as sheaf stalks (replacing merkle.rs SHA-256) makes δ⁰ measure genuine semantic disagreement between overlapping regions — enabling semantic incremental invalidation ('this edit didn't meaningfully change the region → skip re-derivation'), which no content-hash system can do. hdc/src/schema.rs already carries the comment 'algebra doesn't pay rent and we'd ship il+LSH instead' — the fork was already seen.

Correctness architecture: the semantic optimizer can be WRONG (call two different regions close, skip, serve stale). So node_hash (exact) + extraction epoch (bead 20988a, this session) remain the correctness FLOOR; sheaf-over-embeddings rides on top as a lossy performance layer with the hash as the safety net. Today's epoch work is precisely what makes a lossy optimizer safe.

This bead = the ADR writeup (the artifact to poke before code) + the go/no-go decision. Proof ladder tracked as child beads: rung 0 (71b20c, degeneracy tripwire — exists), rung 1 (divergence with HV stalks), rung 2 (git-replay value experiment, re-derivation as free oracle), rung 3 (safety invariant). Open empirical question the whole thing hinges on: does surface-embedding distance predict derived-fact stability for code regions? Unknown; rung 2 settles it.

Related: 716c69, 71b20c, 20988a, a3f764, decade merkle-cas-substrate, decade hdc-translation-layer (a660ab).

title: Add discriminating test: δ⁰-mode on_change output must equal a hash-gated reverse-dep BFS on hash-derived stalks (tripwire for non-axis-aligned restriction maps)
description:
Follow-up to the necessity audit (bead 716c69). The audit's claim — "the δ⁰ cascade is decoration over a hash-gated reverse-dep BFS on all live inputs" — is currently unfalsified by any test. The falsifiability_gates suite proves the cascade reaches a fixed point and respects EPS, but no test compares the δ⁰-mode invalidation set against the naive hash-gated BFS it reduces to.

Add a gate test in rs/ll-open/sheaf/tests/ that:
1. Builds a δ⁰-mode SheafCache (project_dim_range maps, identical both endpoints — the ONLY shape any live consumer produces) over a small chain/diamond.
2. Implements a reference `reverse_dep_bfs(changed, |u,v| hash(P·x_u) != hash(P·x_v))` — plain adjacency BFS gated by an axis-masked content-hash diff.
3. Asserts the two invalidation sets are BYTE-IDENTICAL across three input classes: (a) whole-stalk change, (b) private-dim-only change (dims ≥ agreement_dim), (c) agreement-dim change with hash-scale (non-sub-EPS) movement.

Value: this becomes a TRIPWIRE. The one input that separates δ⁰ from the hash-gated BFS is a sub-EPS *continuous* wiggle in an agreement coordinate — reachable only with NON-hash, NON-axis-aligned stalks/maps. If a future consumer ever installs a rotated/weighted RestrictionMap (making δ⁰ genuinely non-reducible and the cohomology load-bearing), this test fails and documents exactly where the math starts earning its keep. Until then it pins the reduction the audit found, so the "sheaves as the moat" framing can't silently regrow.

Close condition: cargo test -p leyline-sheaf --test <new_gate> passes and the identity assertions hold for classes (a),(b),(c).

Cross-refs: 716c69 (the audit), 4eef8d (cascade fixed-point gate), 4f3f6e (δ⁰ EPS shape).

title: Reframe sheaf invalidation: δ⁰ cascade is a masked-hash-gated reverse-dep BFS on all OSS-live inputs; moat is the label index, not the cochain math
description:
Adversarial necessity audit (math-friend, 2026-07-15, _agent_log/theoretical-foundations-analyst_2026-07-15_agent_log.md, SESSION 2).

FINDING (verified in code, read-only):
On every OSS-live input the δ⁰ cascade produces the SAME invalidation set a reverse-dep BFS gated by "shared-agreement hash changed" would produce. Three facts collapse the math to a masked hash comparison over a graph traversal:

1. Live restriction maps are ALWAYS `RestrictionMap::project_dim_range` — an axis-aligned coordinate slice, IDENTICAL on both endpoints (sheaf_ops.rs:475 op_sheaf_set_topology, :879 op_sheaf_update_topology; both pass `f.clone(), f`). No consumer installs a rotated/weighted/asymmetric map; compose/weighted/project_dim/RestrictionMap::new have zero non-test callers outside complex.rs. So for edge (u,v): δ⁰_edge = ‖P·(x_v − x_u)‖² = a coordinate-masked squared Euclidean distance.

2. mache's live stalks are hash bytes: data[0..agreement_dim] = SHA-256 of the region's cross-community boundary; data[agreement_dim..] = private dims (mache/internal/leyline/sheaf.go:23-26). So the δ⁰ agreement coordinates ARE a hash; "δ⁰ moved past EPS" ⇔ "shared-boundary SHA-256 changed". A hash over data[0..agreement_dim] reproduces δ⁰ exactly. The one capability δ⁰ has over a hash — a numerical tolerance on continuous stalk movement — is unexercised because the stalks are discrete hash bytes.

3. "per-edge fixed point" is a misnomer: SheafCache::on_change (cache.rs:464-524) is a single-pass VecDeque BFS terminated by the `visited` set (O(V+E)); each edge is evaluated once via check_boundary_changed, no convergence iteration. topology.rs::RestrictionGraph::bfs is the plain adjacency BFS the tests already diff against.

Separately: the "91×" moat number (docs/research/sheaf-ablation-study.md) measures regions_touching_files = label-prefix STRING matching (sheaf_ops.rs:221-264), which — per the study's own math-friend audit, bead 50c21d — never calls on_change, never runs the BFS, never evaluates a δ⁰ threshold. The precision moat is a filename→region reverse index (2 labels/file), not cohomology.

"holonomy" has zero occurrences in rs/ (only docs/decades + a CLI spec) — pure vocabulary.

CORRECTNESS: the sheaf carries no correctness content-addressing+epoch don't. Its output is an advisory eviction set; over-evict=slow, under-evict=staleness (a correctness issue only if a consumer trusts the cache without node_hash revalidation). Confirms the floor is node_hash + epoch (bead 20988a). Stalks are derived from the same content node_hash covers.

WHAT TO DO (this bead):
- Reframe the ADR / "sheaves as the moat" language so the moat claim is gated on the label reverse-index, not the δ⁰ cascade.
- Mark δ¹/H¹/faces/holonomy machinery as not-exercised-by-invalidation.
- Fix the "per-edge fixed point" naming on the cascade → "hash-gated reverse-dep BFS with a coordinate-masked edge predicate".
- Keep the Σ‖δ⁰‖² health metric but stop implying it feeds eviction.
The in-crate docstrings (lib.rs, cache.rs) are already candid — this is about the ADR/marketing layer + the misnomer, not the module comments.

Cross-refs: ae7a35 (sheaf lift, "math is the moat" framing), 50c21d (ablation reframing), 20988a (extraction epoch = the real coarse backstop), a3f764 (RESOLVED wiring audit).

title: smell-baseline.json is stale — renamed-tool path + pre-drawdown inflated ceilings grandfather ~200 phantom unwrap sites
description:
docs/smell-baseline.json (last written 2026-07-14) is stale on two axes:

1. Renamed path: it grandfathers `rs/tools/cargo-toml-projector/src/main.rs`, but that tool was renamed to smell-projector in PR #211 — the entry can never match a live source again.
2. Inflated ceilings: it predates the unwrap drawdown (PRs #206/#208, 196→0 bare unwraps) + today's refactors. It lists ~200 unwrap_without_expect sites across 23 files (cert_chain.rs 34, graph.rs 29, staging.rs 16, sheaf/complex.rs 16, cmd_daemon.rs 16, vcs 13...) while `smell-projector --dogfood` now reports zero findings. The gate passes vacuously (0 ≤ inflated ceiling) and would permit ~200 NEW bare unwraps to reappear without failing.

Fix: regenerate via `task smells:baseline`, verify the diff removes the cargo-toml-projector entry + drops the phantom ceilings to current reality, commit. Tightens the gate — a fresh bare unwrap or unannotated unsafe then fails as intended.

Verification: after regen, `cargo run -p smell-projector -- --repo-root ..` still exits 0, and re-introducing one bare .unwrap() in a prod file makes it fail.

comments.text: Downstream of ley-line-open-50d7c0 (unwrap_sites projection yields 0 rows → gate vacuous). The baseline's inflated ceilings + `--write-baseline`-emits-0 are BOTH symptoms of the broken projection, not just calendar staleness. The renamed cargo-toml-projector→smell-projector path entry is genuinely stale independent of that. Do NOT rebaseline until 50d7c0 fixes the projection — regenerating now would write an empty baseline that cements the vacuous gate as clean. Sequence: fix 50d7c0 → then this bead's rebaseline reflects real unwraps.
comments.author: rsry-mcp

title: schema-bridge tooldefs: close the field-name + nested/free-form-object + numeric-bounds gaps blocking rosary's MCP registry
description:
Follow-up from rosary-08a278 (ADR-0006 revival). The `tooldefs` emitter (PR #232, ley-line-open-beb8bb) reproduces flat single-word-field tools, but 5 gaps block reproducing the rest of rosary's 41-tool MCP surface through the REAL `capnp compile → capnpc-schema-bridge-tooldefs` pipeline:

1. **snake_case field names (load-bearing).** `capnp compile` errors on underscores in field names ("declaration names ... must not contain underscores"), and the tooldefs emitter uses the capnp field name verbatim as the JSON property name — no camelCase→snake_case conversion, and it does not honor `$Json.name` (parse_field_annotations rejects it as UnmappedConstruct). So every rosary tool with a `repo_path`/`issue_type`/`test_files`/`comment_id`/`bead_id`/… field (nearly all of them, incl. `rsry_bead_create`) cannot be authored in a real .capnp. NOTE: the existing `bead_create_tooldefs_matches_live_tools_rs` fixture passes only because it pokes raw field-name bytes into a hand-built CodeGeneratorRequest, bypassing the capnp frontend — it does NOT prove bead_create round-trips through the plugin. Fix options: honor capnp `$Json.name("repo_path")` in the tooldefs/jsonschema emitters, OR add a `$name`/`$wireName` trait to _traits.capnp, OR apply a deterministic camelCase→snake_case conversion. Whichever lands must be proven by a fixture that goes through actual `capnp compile` (compile a real .capnp fixture, not poked bytes).
2. **Inline nested objects** (rosary `session_ref: {type:object, properties:{provider,id}, required:[…]}`). Struct-typed fields emit `"$ref":"#/$defs/…"`, and tooldefs has no `$defs` bag → dangling ref. Need inline object expansion for tool inputSchemas.
3. **Free-form objects** (`payload: {type:object, additionalProperties:true}`). capnp has no any-object type; needs a trait (e.g. `$JsonObject`) the emitter lowers to an open object.
4. **Array-of-object** (`rsry_bead_import.beads`: List of inline object).
5. **Numeric bounds** (`rsry_bead_search.limit: minimum:1, maximum:50`). No `$min`/`$max` trait; emitter only emits `minimum:0` for UInt.

Acceptance: `cargo test -p leyline-schema-bridge` gains fixtures — compiled through real `capnp compile` — that reproduce rsry_bead_create (snake_case fields), rsry_dispatch_record (nested session_ref), rsry_agent_run_event_record (payload free-form object), rsry_bead_import (array-of-object), and rsry_bead_search (numeric bounds) structurally against their live tools.rs entries.

title: Arena-resident query blobs: hash-allowlisted overrides for compiled-in .scm defaults
description:
Final phase of queries-as-data (analysis on ley-line-open-e5addb + _agent_log/theoretical-foundations-analyst_2026-07-15_agent_log.md §§1-2). .scm query files become content-addressed arena data: compiled-in defaults seed, arena-resident blobs override behind an operator-controlled BLAKE3 allowlist.

Requirements:
1. Bootstrap: query_set(lang) = arena blob (present AND trusted) else compiled default; version skew surfaces via extraction/injection epoch (query bytes already epoch inputs).
2. Trust: untrusted blob IGNORED with one loud stderr line; extraction falls back to compiled default, never silently to nothing.
3. Emission-ABI gate: blob declaring capture names unknown to the engine is a HARD ERROR at load; vocabulary versioned via emission_abi_version declaration, documented in query_engine.rs.
4. Resource bounds: QueryCursor match limits/timeouts so a pathological blob degrades to "no facts for this file" + stderr, not a hung daemon.
5. Provenance: active query-set source (compiled vs arena hash) observable via introspection; no per-row provenance columns.

Verification: cargo test -p ll-open-ts && task ci

comments.text:
STEP 2 — storage decision (pre-implementation).

DECISION: in-arena tables, following the content-addressed blob-table + pointer-table pattern the arena already uses twice:
- `query_blobs (blob_hash BLOB PRIMARY KEY, blob_bytes BLOB NOT NULL)` — same shape as ADR-0028 `source_blobs` and ADR-0026 `capnp_blobs`, keyed on BLAKE3(blob_bytes), verified at load (a row whose bytes do not hash to its key is treated as untrusted: one stderr line, compiled fallback).
- `_queries (lang TEXT, kind TEXT DEFAULT 'tags', blob_hash BLOB REFERENCES query_blobs, PRIMARY KEY (lang, kind))` — the pointer, same FK-shaped-pointer discipline as `_source.content_hash → source_blobs.blob_hash` and `_ast_pointer.blob_hash → capnp_blobs.blob_hash`.

PRECEDENT REJECTED: sidecar file next to the arena. The only sidecar today is the transient flock `<arena>.lock` (arena_lock.rs) — admission control, not data. Everything durable lives inside the one SQLite file: blobs (source_blobs/capnp_blobs), provenance (_meta.extraction_epoch/_meta.injection_epoch — the f6/f7 gates), facts. An arena is moved/backed up as a single .db; a sidecar would silently detach from it.

TRUST BOUNDARY: the allowlist does NOT live in the arena — whoever writes the arena must not be able to self-trust a blob. Operator control via env `LLO_TRUSTED_QUERY_HASHES` (comma-separated hex BLAKE3-32), matching the established env-knob surface (LLO_EMBEDDER; seams LLO_EXTRACTION_EPOCH, LLO_INJECTIONS_SCM).

WIRING PLAN:
- Resolution once per parse pass in `parse_into_conn` (leyline_ts::query_engine::QuerySet::resolve(conn)): override present + hash-verified + allowlisted → engine built from blob via ABI-gated constructor; otherwise compiled default + one stderr line. Unknown capture name or bad emission-abi declaration in a TRUSTED blob → hard Err out of parse_into_conn.
- Engines threaded per-file (ExtractCtx { queries: &QuerySet, budget }) through fold_children/fold_injected into extract_refs — no process-global override state, so per-arena resolution stays correct under the daemon.
- Epoch: `_meta.query_set_epoch` = σ composite over (lang, source-tag compiled|arena:<hex>, active scm bytes) per Tier-3 language, ANDed into the existing f6/f7 unchanged-skip gate in parse_into_conn. Compiled .scm edits now self-invalidate (the refs.rs EXTRACTION_EPOCH doc anticipated exactly this). Provenance: `_meta.query_source:<lang>` = `arena:<hex>` rows written only for active overrides (absence = compiled) — the smallest honest introspection surface.
- Resource bounds (tree-sitter 0.26.8): `QueryCursor::set_match_limit` + `did_exceed_match_limit` on every engine run; `matches_with_options` progress-callback deadline (per-file budget) on arena-blob engines, whose patterns are the untrusted input. Trip → facts for that file dropped, one stderr line, parse completes.
comments.author: rsry-mcp

comments.text:
STEP 2 (impl agent) — confirming scoping plan (comment 221) + two load-bearing refinements decided BEFORE implementing.

FOLLOWING the plan verbatim on storage: in-arena `query_blobs (blob_hash BLOB PK, blob_bytes BLOB NOT NULL)` + `_queries (lang TEXT, kind TEXT DEFAULT 'tags', blob_hash BLOB, PK(lang,kind))`. Precedent = source_blobs (ADR-0028) + capnp_blobs (ADR-0026): content-addressed blob table + FK-shaped pointer, all durable state inside the one .db. Trust allowlist OUTSIDE the arena via env `LLO_TRUSTED_QUERY_HASHES` (comma-sep hex blake3-32) so the arena writer can't self-trust. Provenance via `_meta.query_source:<lang> = arena:<hex>` (present only for active overrides; absence = compiled). Epoch via `_meta.query_set_epoch` ANDed into the existing f6/f7 unchanged-skip gate.

REFINEMENT 1 (blast-radius): `extract_refs(node,source,node_id,source_id,language,container)` stays UNCHANGED (7 call sites incl. 5 refs.rs test callers) — the compiled path is byte-identical. Overrides ride a NEW `extract_refs_resolved(..., queries: &QuerySet, bounds: &Cell<bool>)` called only from cmd_parse's fold. `parse_into_conn(conn,source,lang,scope)` signature UNCHANGED (60+ callers) — the QuerySet is resolved INSIDE it from conn + env allowlist. Resolution returns `(QuerySet, Vec<String> warnings)`; parse_into_conn prints one eprintln per warning (this is how "exactly one stderr line" is unit-testable — resolve is called directly in test b).

REFINEMENT 2 (override semantics): an arena override REPLACES the compiled QueryEngine's `.scm`-driven emission for that language. The query-inexpressible imperative arms (Rust use-list flattening, Python from-import joins, qualified Class.method defs, JS/TS κ fixups) are NOT re-run under an override — an operator shipping an override owns the complete emission for that language via the `.scm`. For the pure-delegate languages (Go/C/SQL/Bash) this is a lossless swap; documented in query_engine.rs. Demo/tests use Go (pure delegate).

ABI GATE (req 3): override blob MUST declare a leading `; emission-abi-version: N` comment. Missing, or N != EMISSION_ABI_VERSION(=1) → hard load error. After Query::new, every capture name not in {def,ref,import,name,qualifier,path,alias} and not `_`-prefixed (predicate helpers, e.g. bash `@_cmd`) → hard load error. Applies to TRUSTED blobs only (untrusted are ignored before the gate).

RESOURCE BOUNDS (req 4, tree-sitter 0.26.8): override engines run `QueryCursor::set_match_limit` + `matches_with_options` with a progress-callback tick budget; on `did_exceed_match_limit` or budget break, that file's extracted facts are dropped, one stderr line, parse completes. Compiled engines stay unbounded (trusted, fixture-pinned). Limits env-tunable (`LLO_QUERY_MATCH_LIMIT`).

Verification: `cargo test -p leyline-cli-lib` + `task ci` (run from rs/).
comments.author: rsry-mcp

comments.text:
GREEN — implemented, committed, task ci exit 0. Bead stays OPEN.

TDD RED (captured by stubbing extract_refs_resolved to ignore overrides, then restoring):
- a_allowlisted_override_replaces_compiled_default — FAILED: "allowlisted override must emit the distinguishable package-name def; got [Alpha, Beta, Gadget]"
- d_pathological_override_is_bounded_and_file_skipped — FAILED: "a bounded override run must drop this file's extracted facts; got [Alpha, Beta, Gadget]"
- e_swapping_active_blob_bumps_epoch_and_rederives — FAILED: "OVERRIDE_PKG must emit only the package-name def; left [Alpha, Beta, Gadget] right [widget]"
- b (untrusted-ignored+warning) and c (ABI hard error) test resolve_query_set directly, so they were green pre-wiring — the resolver builds/rejects override engines independent of the fold. Result under the stub: 2 passed, 3 failed.

GREEN (feature restored):

- full `cargo test -p leyline-cli-lib`: every suite ok (f6/f7 epoch gates, injection, source_blobs, pointer_store, integration, ir_producer, sheaf — 0 failures across ~40 test binaries).
- `cargo test -p leyline-ts --lib`: 67 passed.

task ci (from worktree root): exit 0 — fmt:check, clippy -D warnings, full test, smells (no new findings), lint:blake3 (no direct blake3::hash; all hashing via leyline_core::ContentAddressed), compat:check + gen:server-json:check both up to date.

Files:
- rs/ll-open/ts/src/schema.rs — query_blobs/_queries DDL + create_query_blob_tables + read_query_overrides
- rs/ll-open/ts/src/query_engine.rs — EMISSION_ABI_VERSION, new_override (ABI gate), extract_bounded (BoundsExceeded), QuerySet, resolve_query_set, query_set_epoch; module-header docs for the ABI + override semantics
- rs/ll-open/ts/src/refs.rs — extract_refs_resolved wrapper (extract_refs unchanged)
- rs/ll-open/cli-lib/src/cmd_parse.rs — create tables, resolve+warn, epoch gate AND + stamp, provenance rows, ExtractCtx threaded through fold_children/fold_injected/fold_injected_node, per-file bounds drop+stderr
- rs/ll-open/cli-lib/tests/arena_query_blobs_test.rs (new)

Resource-bound mechanism: QueryCursor::set_match_limit + matches_with_options progress-callback tick budget (tree-sitter 0.26.8); LLO_QUERY_MATCH_LIMIT / LLO_QUERY_PROGRESS_BUDGET operator knobs. Test d trips it deterministically with a two-unbounded-wildcard pattern at LLO_QUERY_MATCH_LIMIT=8.

title: Injections: injections.scm embedded-language extraction with own-CA-root injected subtrees
description:
Phase after engine+epoch in the queries-as-data design (bead e5addb analysis + _agent_log/theoretical-foundations-analyst_2026-07-15_agent_log.md). Falsification-shaped MVP (EXP2): go host → sql injected, driven by rs/ll-open/ts/queries/go/injections.scm using upstream tree-sitter conventions (@injection.content + #set! injection.language).

Hard requirements:
(a) Injected subtrees get their OWN content-addressed root — host file structural hash must NOT depend on injected grammar version (bumping tree-sitter-sql must not re-hash Go files containing SQL).
(b) Extraction epoch becomes COMPOSITE — injected facts depend on host injections.scm + injected-language tags.scm + both grammar versions. Composite = hash over {EXTRACTION_EPOCH, injections.scm content, injected tags.scm content}.

Acceptance (EXP2):
- SQL CREATE TABLE name inside a Go string literal lands in node_defs for that Go file (source_id = host file, container_node_id = enclosing Go function).
- Test proving host file structural node_hash values byte-identical with injection pass on vs off.
- f7 epoch gate: change injections.scm content (test override), assert re-derivation; f6 tests keep passing.

Verification:
  cd rs && cargo test -p ll-open-ts
  cd rs && cargo test -p ll-open-cli-lib
  cd rs && cargo test --test f6_extraction_epoch_invalidation
  task ci

comments.text:
SCOPE (pre-implementation, EXP2 falsification-shaped MVP; empirical probes done against tree-sitter 0.26 + tree-sitter-go 0.25 + tree-sitter-sequel 0.3 in a scratch crate — Go string content nodes are NAMED (raw_string_literal_content / interpreted_string_literal_content), the Rust binding's QueryCursor::matches evaluates #match? and exposes #set! via property_settings, and Parser::set_included_ranges reparses a subrange keeping HOST byte offsets).

1. DETECTION — queries/go/injections.scm, upstream conventions: pattern root = string-literal node, capture @injection.content on the content child, (#set! injection.language "sql"), (#match? …) heuristic requiring a statement-shaped leading keyword (create table/view/…, select, insert into, update…set, delete from, with…as() — kills "update the docs"/"delete this file" prose). Heuristic is per-pattern DATA in the .scm, not engine code. Both raw and interpreted string literals; escape-sequence-split interpreted strings inject only the leading content segment (documented limitation; @injection.combined is follow-up).

2. ENGINE — ts/src/injections.rs: InjectionEngine (Query compiled once per host language via OnceLock, Go-only for now), sites(node, source) anchored via set_max_start_depth(0) exactly like QueryEngine::extract; returns (target TsLanguage resolved via from_name — unresolvable feature-off targets skipped, Range of the content node). cli-lib's fold_children calls it per named node; on hit, fold_injected (cmd_parse.rs) reparses the range under the target grammar via set_included_ranges and mini-folds the injected tree. Injected parse failures degrade to zero facts, never fail the host parse.

3. INJECTED NODE_ID SCHEME (pinned by tests) — injected root = "{host_literal_node_id}#inj{k}" (k = site ordinal at that host node, 0-based; always 0 for go→sql). Descendants follow the host fold's naming exactly: "{parent}/{kind}", "_{idx}" suffix only when >1 named siblings share a kind. '#' cannot occur in host node_ids (path + grammar-kind derived), so no collision. Injected facts land in node_defs/node_refs with source_id = HOST file, container_node_id = host enclosing Go function at the literal (fold's container threading passes through; κ containers inside the injected tree thread normally).

4. OWN-CA-ROOT / JOIN SHAPE — host fold is UNTOUCHED: injected nodes get NO _ast/nodes/_ast_pointer/capnp rows, so host node_hash values are byte-identical with the pass on or off (requirement (a) holds by construction; proven by test via LLO_DISABLE_INJECTIONS=1 seam). fold_injected computes merkle node_hash with the SAME hash_leaf/hash_internal fold over the injected tree and emits deduped node_content/node_child rows (lang = "sql") — the injected program root's content row IS the own-CA root, and a standalone .sql file with identical statement bytes dedups to the SAME hashes (span-free fold; pinned by test). Injected fact rows carry their injected node_hash — required anyway by the node_defs/node_refs.node_hash FK → node_content (schema.rs create_ir_tables). ParsedFile gains injected_hashes: Vec<(node_id, [u8;32])> merged into the hash_by_id attachment map. No dedup-continuity risk: zero existing node_hash preimages change (hash_leaf/hash_internal untouched; injection adds NEW hashes in the injected grammar's kind-space only). No wire/arena format break — everything additive; no STOP needed.

5. COMPOSITE EPOCH — new _meta key "injection_epoch" ANDed with extraction_epoch in the epoch_current gate; extraction_epoch storage/semantics UNCHANGED (f6 asserts the stored value equals the numeric override, so the composite must be a second key, not a reshaped first one). Value = hex σ-hash (leyline-core ContentAddressed, lint:blake3-compliant) over domain "llo/injection-epoch/v1" ‖ current_extraction_epoch() ‖ per (host,target) pair: injections.scm bytes ‖ target tags.scm bytes ‖ grammar fingerprints (abi_version + node_kind_count + field_count of BOTH grammars — the available in-process proxy for grammar version; a grammar bump that changes neither kinds nor fields but changes parse shapes still needs a manual EXTRACTION_EPOCH bump, documented). Computed in ts/src/injections.rs (ts already deps leyline-core). Env seam LLO_INJECTIONS_SCM substitutes the injections.scm bytes in the preimage — same convention as LLO_EXTRACTION_EPOCH (test seam changes the epoch input, not the emission). Stamped only on full-tree passes, same as extraction_epoch. Pre-injection arenas lack the key → treated stale → ONE forced re-derive on upgrade, which is also what delivers the new injected facts — so NO EXTRACTION_EPOCH bump (4 stays; the missing-key staleness is the invalidation).

6. TESTS (RED first): cli-lib/tests/injection_extraction_test.rs — (a) SQL CREATE TABLE def token in node_defs for the host .go file w/ container + canonical_kind "type"; (b) host _ast (node_id→node_hash) map byte-identical with LLO_DISABLE_INJECTIONS=1 vs off; node_id scheme pin; own-CA-root pin (injected def node_hash == the node_hash the same statement produces in a standalone .sql parse, and resolves in node_content lang='sql'). cli-lib/tests/f7_injection_epoch_invalidation.rs — epoch recorded; LLO_INJECTIONS_SCM content change forces unchanged=0 full re-derivation; same-epoch preserves incremental skip. ts injections.rs unit tests — sites() detection + prose rejection + anchoring. f6 must pass unmodified.

OUT OF SCOPE: any host language beyond go, any target beyond sql (data-only follow-up once the pass exists); @injection.combined; injected _ast occurrence rows; arena-resident query blobs.
comments.author: rsry-mcp

title: schema-bridge: $doc/$optional/$default annotation vocabulary + $op-driven MCP tool-definitions output mode (unblocks rosary-08a278)
description:
The capnpc-schema-bridge-jsonschema emitter (ley-line-open-6585aa, PR #228) named rosary's MCP tool registry (rosary-08a278) as its first consumer, but as shipped it CANNOT reproduce rosary's live MCP surface (src/serve/tools.rs, 41 tools, 840 lines) without regression. Four load-bearing gaps, measured against tools.rs:

1. DESCRIPTIONS DROPPED — 206 property-level + 41 tool-level descriptions, all load-bearing MCP UX (e.g. bead_create.files: "CRITICAL: these scope parallel dispatch — has_file_overlap() blocks concurrent beads…"). The emitter's own header documents it emits none: "capnp's CodeGeneratorRequest carries no doc comments (capnp discards comments at parse) … descriptions arrive later via an annotation vocabulary, not invented here." WORSE: enum values are encoded IN descriptions (0 JSON `enum` arrays in tools.rs — issue_type/work_mode list valid values as prose), so descriptions are doubly load-bearing.

2. OPTIONALITY INVERTED — emitter marks ALL fields required + additionalProperties:false (capnp has no optional fields). rosary curates required SUBSETS: bead_create has 14 properties, only `title` required; many tools are `"required":[]` (all optional). Generating makes every argument mandatory — a breaking change to every tool contract.

3. DEFAULTS DROPPED — 21 `"default"` values in tools.rs; emitter emits no JSON `default`.

4. WRONG OUTPUT SHAPE — emitter produces a `$defs` bag of TYPE schemas (one entry per struct). rosary's tool_definitions() returns the MCP tools/list ARRAY of {name, description, inputSchema} objects. The `$op`/OpInfo annotation in _traits.capnp exists to declare "this struct is an operation (input/output/errors)" but the jsonschema emitter consumes NO annotations yet — it is a pure type→schema shape mapper.

SCOPE OF THIS BEAD:
- Add to rs/ll-core/schema-spec/_traits.capnp: a `$doc(text)` field+struct annotation (→ JSON Schema `description`; struct-level → the tool description), an `$optional` field annotation (→ curated `required` subset instead of all-required), and optionally `$default(text)` + explicit enum support. Follow the file's naming + append-only ordinal rules (range @0xd3b652fd6a4debe7..ff).
- Teach outputs/json_schema.rs to lower these annotations, and add an `$op`-driven output mode that emits the MCP tools/list array ({name, description, inputSchema} per `$op` struct) rather than only the `$defs` bag.
- Keep the existing $defs/type-schema mode (other consumers) working; the tool-definitions mode is additive.

CONSUMER CONTRACT (the acceptance bar): a schema-bridge tool-definitions run over rosary's registry capnp must be byte-reproducible into rosary's committed tool_definitions() JSON — i.e. rosary-08a278's drift gate (generated == committed live tools.rs surface) must be able to pass. Descriptions, curated required-sets, defaults, and the tools/list shape must all round-trip. Coordinate the exact JSON shape with rosary-08a278.

BLOCKS: rosary-08a278 (rosary's generated MCP tool registry).

comments.text:
## ORIENT complete — annotation plumbing VERIFIED usable (no STOP)

Studied schema-bridge (IR / inputs::capnp / json_schema+zod+go emitters / cross-emitter agreement gate / integration.rs) + `_traits.capnp` + live rosary `src/serve/tools.rs` (rsry_bead_create). Confirmed capnp CodeGeneratorRequest exposes annotation VALUES usably: `annotation::Reader::get_value() -> value::Reader` (capnp-0.21.7 schema_capnp.rs:3457), builder `init_value().set_text()/set_void()`; the existing `decode_value` already reads `value::Which::Text`. STOP condition does not trigger.

Key drift facts measured against tools.rs `rsry_bead_create` (13 props, `required:["title"]`):
- rosary inputSchemas carry NO `additionalProperties:false` → tooldefs mode MUST omit it (the `$defs` emitter keeps it).
- `priority` is plain `{"type":"integer"}` (no `minimum:0`) → fixture models it `:Int32`, not `:UInt8`.
- defaults present: description="" , priority=2, issue_type="task", force=false.
- enum values (issue_type/work_mode) are prose INSIDE the field description, not capnp enums → fixture models them as `:Text` + `$doc` prose. No JSON `enum` array needed for the drift gate to hold.

## DESIGN
1. `_traits.capnp`: append `doc @0xd3b652fd6a4debee (field,struct,enum,enumerant) :Text`, `optional @0xd3b652fd6a4debef (field) :Void`, `default @0xd3b652fd6a4debf0 (field) :Text` (JSON-encoded value verbatim). Append `name @3 :Text` to `OpInfo` (explicit MCP tool name — drift-proof vs a fragile CamelCase→snake heuristic).
2. IR: `StructField{doc,optional,default}`, `Struct{doc,op:Option<OpInfo>}`, `Enum{doc}`, new `ir::OpInfo{name,input,output,errors}`.
3. inputs/capnp.rs: recognize the 4 `_traits` ids (doc/optional/default/op), lower to IR; unknown annotations still hard-error (json.* fail-fast tests stay green). Enumerant-level `$doc` recognized (valid per vocabulary) but not lowered — JSON Schema draft 2020-12 has no per-enum-value description slot; documented, not silent.
4. json_schema emitter: `$doc`→`description`, `$optional`→drop from `required`, `$default`→`default`. `$defs` mode keeps `additionalProperties:false`.
5. New output mode: `OutputFormat::ToolDefs`, bin `capnpc-schema-bridge-tooldefs`, suffix `tools.json` (follows the crate's argv[0] one-bin-per-format convention). Emits a JSON ARRAY of `{name,description,inputSchema}` — one per `$op` struct, IR declaration order (deterministic), inputSchema WITHOUT additionalProperties — "pluckable" into rosary's `json!({"tools": <array>})`.
6. Cross-emitter agreement gate: honor `$optional` (required = non-optional fields); zod/go don't express optionality/default/description → explicitly exempted with a comment (they still surface the field NAME, which is what the gate checks). New annotated fixture added.
7. Acceptance fixture: `BeadCreateInput` mirroring rsry_bead_create; tooldefs output parsed to serde_json::Value and asserted structurally == the tools.rs entry → proves rosary's drift gate CAN pass.

## RED-first
Writing tests referencing the new IR fields + `OutputFormat::ToolDefs` + `outputs::tool_defs::emit` + annotated fixtures FIRST (compile-fail / assertion-fail = RED), then implement to GREEN. RED list to follow in next comment.
comments.author: rsry-mcp

comments.text:
## SHIPPED (committed, not pushed) — leaving bead OPEN per instructions

Branch `feat/schema-bridge-annotations-beb8bb` @ **68dd05d44d951fb381ddc7133449cd130f148b29** (base = main f38049b).

### Vocabulary shipped (`_traits.capnp`, compiles under capnp 1.3.0)
- `annotation doc @0xd3b652fd6a4debee (field, struct, enum, enumerant) :Text`
- `annotation optional @0xd3b652fd6a4debef (field) :Void`
- `annotation default @0xd3b652fd6a4debf0 (field) :Text` (JSON-encoded value, injected verbatim)
- `OpInfo.name @3 :Text` (append-only) — explicit MCP tool name (drift-proof vs CamelCase→snake heuristic)
- `_traits.md` catalog updated to match.

### Output-mode design
`OutputFormat::ToolDefs` + bin `capnpc-schema-bridge-tooldefs` (suffix `tools.json`), following the crate's argv[0] one-bin-per-format convention (documented choice, not a flag). Emits a bare MCP `tools/list` JSON ARRAY `[{name, description, inputSchema}]` — one entry per `$Op` struct, IR declaration order (deterministic). `name` = `OpInfo.name`; tool `description` = struct `$Doc`; inputSchema struct = `OpInfo.input` (or the annotated struct when empty). inputSchema carries NO `additionalProperties` (MCP inputSchemas are open — the live rosary registry omits it; the `$defs` emitter keeps it). Field-shape (type/description/default) is shared verbatim with `json_schema::render_property`, so the two emitters can't drift.

### Annotation plumbing (verified usable — no STOP)
`inputs/capnp.rs` reads annotation VALUES off CodeGeneratorRequest: Text via `annotation::get_value()→value::Which::Text`; `$Op(OpInfo)` struct value decoded by peeling the any_pointer (`StructPeek`) and reading pointer slots 0=input/1=output/2=errors/3=name (OpInfo is all-pointer; slot index == ordinal, frozen by the append-only rule). Unknown annotations still hard-error via the existing `annotation_kind` path.

### bead_create fixture proof
`bead_create_tooldefs_matches_live_tools_rs`: a `BeadCreateInput` `$Op` fixture (13 props, `required:["title"]`, defaults description=""/priority=2/issue_type="task"/force=false, enum-values-as-prose in descriptions, `priority :Int32` → plain `{"type":"integer"}`) → tooldefs output parsed to serde_json::Value equals an INDEPENDENTLY serde_json-built expected entry copied from rosary `src/serve/tools.rs`. Two implementations (string-emit vs Value-build) meet in the middle + literal spot-checks (name, required, prop count 13, each default, no `enum` array, no `additionalProperties`). Proves rosary's generated-vs-committed drift gate CAN pass.

### RED→GREEN
Tests authored first against not-yet-existing API/behavior → compile-RED (crate wouldn't build: `OutputFormat::ToolDefs`, `outputs::tool_defs::emit`, `StructField{doc,optional,default}`, `Struct{doc,op}`, `Enum.doc`, `OpInfo` all absent). Behavioral RED asserts that drove implementation, all now GREEN:
- `field_annotations_lower_to_jsonschema` (description + optional→required-subset + default)
- `struct_and_enum_doc_lower_to_description`
- `enumerant_doc_is_accepted_but_not_lowered` (vocabulary complete, no silent gap)
- `tooldefs_emits_tools_array_without_additional_properties`
- `tooldefs_empty_when_no_op_structs`
- `tooldefs_dispatch_routes_to_tool_defs_emitter`
- `tooldefs_format_parses` / `_suffix_is_tools_json` / `_from_binary_name`
- `bead_create_tooldefs_matches_live_tools_rs`
- `cross_emitter_agreement_annotated_tool` (gate extended: required = non-optional; zod/go optionality/default/description exempted with comment, still assert field-name presence)
Regression-preserved GREEN: `json_flatten_annotation_fails_fast`, `unknown_annotation_fails_fast_with_hex_id`, `jsonschema_struct_scalars_golden` (byte-exact — un-annotated fixtures unchanged).

### Test counts + CI
schema-bridge integration: **60 passed, 0 failed, 2 ignored** (pre-existing #[ignore] stubs). `cargo fmt --all` clean. **`task ci` GREEN (EXIT=0)** — full monorepo gate: fmt:check, check, clippy, smells, all crate tests, build:fs-*, sign:host, compat:check, gen:server-json:check. New `capnpc-schema-bridge-tooldefs` bin builds + runs.

Files: `rs/ll-core/schema-spec/_traits.capnp` + `_traits.md`; `rs/ll-open/schema-bridge/{Cargo.toml, src/lib.rs, src/inputs/capnp.rs, src/ir/mod.rs, src/outputs/{mod.rs,json_schema.rs,tool_defs.rs (new)}, tests/integration.rs}`.

Not pushed, no PR. Bead left open per dispatch. Unblocks rosary-08a278: the tooldefs emitter now reproduces the live tool_definitions() shape.

title: task ci: cheap textual gates run before compile-heavy gates — fail fast on fmt
description:
Observed 2026-07-15: three of six worktree agents burned a full check+clippy pass (minutes) before dying at fmt:check (sub-second), then reran the entire gate — fmt:check sits third in the ci task, behind two compile-heavy gates (Taskfile.yml:495-497).

Fix: reorder `task ci` so sub-second textual gates run first (fmt:check, then lint:blake3 and readme:version-check where they are compile-free), before check/clippy/test. Failure cost for a formatting slip drops from minutes×2 to sub-second + one clean pass.

Verification: task ci still exits 0 on clean tree; introduce a deliberate fmt violation and confirm task ci fails in under ~5s.

title: Tier 3 partial algebra for sql/bash: sql DDL defs + relation/invocation/trigger refs; bash function defs + command refs + static source imports
description: Author query-native Tier 3 extraction for sql and bash following PR #223's pattern (.scm + engine-delegate fn + dispatch arm + κ mappings + fixture tests + fidelity test). Scope is the PARTIAL algebra assessed on parent bead ley-line-open-e5addb (2026-07-15 assessment comment): sql emits create_table/create_view/create_materialized_view/create_function/create_schema defs (schema-qualified dual-emit) and relation/insert/delete-from/invocation/trigger-edge refs, no imports; bash emits function_definition defs, statically-named command refs, and source/. imports with static paths. Rejected emissions (index/trigger defs, sql columns, DROP/ALTER refs, CTEs, bash variables/expansions/aliases) are documented in the .scm headers with reasons. EXTRACTION_EPOCH 2→3. Text predicates (#any-of?/#not-any-of?) verified native to the Rust binding — pure query data, no engine change.

comments.text:

Assessment (full text on parent bead ley-line-open-e5addb, 2026-07-15 ASSESSMENT comment): both languages carry a well-posed PARTIAL algebra. Grammar shapes verified empirically via scratch AST dumps against tree-sitter-sequel 0.3 and tree-sitter-bash 0.25 before authoring; #any-of?/#not-any-of? text predicates verified natively evaluated by the Rust binding's QueryCursor::matches (no engine change — predicates are per-pattern query data).

Artifacts:
- rs/ll-open/ts/queries/sql/tags.scm (115 lines): 5 def patterns (create_table incl. TEMPORARY, create_view, create_materialized_view, create_function incl. OR REPLACE, create_schema; schema-qualifier dual-emit), 6 ref patterns (relation, insert, delete-from, invocation, trigger ON-table, trigger EXECUTE FUNCTION), 0 import patterns (no in-language construct). Rejected emissions documented in header with reasons.
- rs/ll-open/ts/queries/bash/tags.scm (73 lines): 1 def pattern (function_definition, both spellings), 1 ref pattern (statically-named commands, source/. excluded — one node one fact), 2 import patterns (static word path, expansion-free double-quoted string path; first-argument anchored).
- refs.rs: extract_sql + extract_bash pure engine delegates, dispatch arms, EXTRACTION_EPOCH 2→3 with history entry (silent-empty shape — bump forces arena re-derivation).
- languages.rs: κ arms (sql: create_table/create_view/create_materialized_view→type, create_function→function, create_schema→module, program→module; bash: function_definition→function, program→module; bash `command` deliberately unmapped — it is the Import anchor kind, mapping it would collapse every command node), Tier1+2 block comment updated, Sql removed from tier12 negative probe.

TDD evidence — RED first, all confirmed failing before implementation:
- leyline-ts (12 RED): tier3_query_native_languages_map_emitted_def_kinds + sql_tests {extract_ddl_name_defs, extract_schema_qualified_defs_dual_emit, extract_relation_refs_from_join_update_insert_delete, extract_schema_qualified_relation_refs_dual_emit, extract_invocation_refs, extract_trigger_edges_not_trigger_name, rejected_emissions_stay_silent} + bash_tests {extract_function_defs_both_spellings, extract_command_refs_static_names_only, extract_source_imports_static_paths, variables_and_expansions_stay_silent}. (sql_has_no_import_algebra + dynamic_source_paths_do_not_import were vacuously green pre-impl, true negatives post-impl.)
- cli-lib (2 RED): sql_files_produce_def_ref_rows ("got 0. Symptom: extract_refs dispatcher has no Sql arm"), bash_files_produce_def_ref_rows (same shape).

GREEN: cargo test -p leyline-ts --features sql,bash --lib → 84 passed / 0 failed. cli-lib def_ref_extraction_fidelity_test → 14 passed (incl. both new arms). f6_extraction_epoch_invalidation → 4 passed (env-override design absorbs the constant bump). task ci → exit 0, 1590 tests across 114 suites, 0 failures, fmt + clippy + version-pin gates green.

title: schema-bridge json-schema output — first post-lift follow-up (rosary MCP inputSchema is the first consumer)
description:
First new output format for leyline-schema-bridge after the ADR-0036 Phase 2 lift (ley-line-open-0806dc). Rosary needs MCP inputSchema JSON generated from an annotated capnp tool registry (rosary ADR-0006 revival); the crate's README already declares JSON Schema as the planned next output (`outputs/json_schema.rs`).

Shape of the work (mirrors the Go emitter add, cloister-75f6d5):
- New `OutputFormat::JsonSchema` variant in src/lib.rs + `KNOWN` entry + `file_suffix()` (`schema.json` or similar)
- New `[[bin]] capnpc-schema-bridge-json-schema` in Cargo.toml (argv[0]-basename dispatch)
- New `src/outputs/json_schema.rs` emitter reading the existing IR — no IR changes expected for v1 (structs/scalars/enums/lists/unions already modeled)
- Fail-fast invariant holds: unmapped constructs stay hard errors, never a permissive `{}` schema
- Golden + fail-case tests in tests/integration.rs per the crate's paired-test discipline

Decide during implementation: draft dialect (draft-07 vs 2020-12 — MCP inputSchema consumers generally accept draft-07 subset), and how unions render (oneOf with discriminator vs flat oneOf matching the zod emitter's two union shapes).

comments.text:
GREEN reached in isolated worktree (branch feat/schema-bridge-jsonschema-6585aa, base main@30094c9). Strict TDD: 15 new tests written first, all failed for the expected reason (outputs::json_schema + OutputFormat::JsonSchema missing), then the emitter landed.

Design decisions:
- New emitter src/outputs/json_schema.rs, bin capnpc-schema-bridge-jsonschema (same argv[0] dispatch), file suffix `schema.json`, dispatched via OutputFormat::JsonSchema in the lib multiplexer.
- One draft 2020-12 document per schema file: $schema + $comment (do-not-edit header — JSON has no comment syntax) + $id (<basename>.schema.json) + $defs. Each capnp struct is an MCP inputSchema-compatible $defs entry (type/properties/required) — the rosary-08a278 consumer contract.
- Structs: all fields required (capnp has no optional), additionalProperties:false mirroring zod .strict() (cloister-cf2e6a).
- Named-group unions: discriminant property = oneOf of single-key strict branch objects; Void payloads = {"type":"null"} (capnp nested JSON convention). Anonymous-inline unions: whole def = oneOf over flat branches (base fields + one variant key), per cloister-77172d. oneOf not anyOf — wire discriminant selects exactly one.
- Data = {"type":"string","contentEncoding":"base64"} (wire-JSON view, matches Go encoding/json; zod validates the decoded Uint8Array).
- Consts = {"const": <value>} $defs entries; non-finite float consts are a hard UnmappedConstruct error (JSON has no Inf/NaN and no valid-JSON sentinel).
- No description fields: capnp CodeGeneratorRequest carries no doc comments; nothing to map.
- Determinism: direct string emission in IR declaration order (enums, structs, consts — zod/go order), no map-keyed serialization; full-text golden pin locks layout.

Cross-emitter gate: assert_cross_emitter_agreement() in tests/integration.rs holds zod/go/jsonschema to the same struct names, field wire names, union variants, enum values, and required-ness; jsonschema side compared structurally via parsed $defs. Applied to the identity.capnp Proof fixture (second-schema proof) and a Backend enum+named-union fixture.

Crate suite: 49 passed / 0 failed / 2 ignored (pre-existing aspirational stubs). serde_json added as dev-dependency only (workspace pin) for structural test assertions; runtime deps stay capnp + thiserror. task ci running; commit follows.
comments.author: rsry-mcp

title: Tier 3: author tags.scm for java/c/cpp (first query-native languages)
description:
Java, C, and C++ are registered Tier 1+2 (parse + validate, PR #220) but have NO extractor: canonical_kind returns None and extract_refs has no dispatch arm, so every .java/.c/.h/.cpp file writes zero node_defs/node_refs/_imports rows. Give them defs/refs/imports emission as .scm query data over the generic QueryEngine — the first languages authored as queries from day one (no imperative extractor ever existed for them).

Scope per language:
- java: class/interface/enum/record/method defs (+ ancestor-qualified Class.method), method_invocation call refs with receiver dual-emit obj.method+method, import_declaration imports (wildcard imports skipped — no addressable alias).
- c: function_declarator defs (definitions + prototypes), struct/union/enum/typedef type defs, call_expression refs, #include imports with quote AND angle-bracket stripping (engine @path trim rule extended as generic data-compatible behavior).
- cpp: the C set plus class defs, namespace defs, qualified `Ns::func` out-of-line method defs (query dual-emit, separator "::"), in-class method defs (field_identifier declarator + ancestor-qualified Class::method fixup), qualified `ns::func()` call refs.

Ancestor-qualified defs stay imperative (queries match downward only), following the documented rust_impl_receiver / python_enclosing_class / js_ts_context_fixups pattern. C preprocessor is taken at tree-sitter face value — macro/ifdef-hidden defs are out of scope, documented in the .scm headers. EXTRACTION_EPOCH bumps 1→2 so existing arenas re-derive facts and pick up the new emissions on binary upgrade.

comments.text:
RED→GREEN complete, task ci running.

RED (all observed failing on empty extraction before any implementation):
- java: refs.rs java_tests x4 (extract_type_defs, extract_method_defs_are_qualified, extract_call_refs_dual_emit_receiver, extract_imports_skip_wildcards) + fidelity java_files_produce_def_ref_rows
- c: refs.rs c_tests x4 (extract_function_defs_and_prototypes, extract_type_defs, extract_call_refs, extract_includes_strip_quotes_and_angle_brackets) + fidelity c_files_produce_def_ref_rows
- cpp: refs.rs cpp_tests x5 (extract_class_namespace_and_inclass_method_defs, extract_out_of_line_method_defs_qualified, extract_template_function_defs, extract_call_refs_bare_method_and_qualified, extract_includes_strip_quotes_and_angle_brackets) + fidelity cpp_files_produce_def_ref_rows
The new refs tests dispatch through extract_refs (not the extractor directly) so they also gate the factory arm.

GREEN:
- queries/java/tags.scm (68 lines), queries/c/tags.scm (62), queries/cpp/tags.scm (101). C is a pure engine delegate (zero imperative arms). Java keeps one imperative arm (java_enclosing_type: ancestor-qualified Type.method — class_body/interface_body + enum_body_declarations chains). Cpp keeps one (cpp_enclosing_class: in-class field_identifier members → Class::method); out-of-line Shape::area qualifies as pure query data via (#set! qualifier-separator "::") since the qualified_identifier is a CHILD of the declarator.
- Engine change (data-compatible, documented): @path trim rule extended from quotes/backticks to also strip <> — C/C++ system_lib_string carries the angle brackets in node text. Generic delimiter stripping; no language's path legitimately starts/ends with them.
- κ arms for java/c/cpp in languages.rs covering exactly the emitted def-kind anchors + import/module; tier12 negative test updated (java graduated), new positive pin tier3_query_native_languages_map_emitted_def_kinds.
- EXTRACTION_EPOCH 1→2 with doc-comment history (new emissions need the re-derivation gate on binary upgrade; f6 tests use LLO_EXTRACTION_EPOCH overrides so no test pin broke).
- Wildcard imports (import java.util.*) skipped via trailing-anchor pattern, same no-addressable-alias rule as Rust use foo::*. C/C++ preprocessor taken at tree-sitter face value, limitation documented in both .scm headers. Constructors (java + cpp destructors/operators) unmatched, documented.
