title: Emit qualifier on node_refs (the b9d1d5 leftover) — upgrades every qualifier-sensitive mache rule
description:
The original ley-line-open-b9d1d5 plan was "emit referrer_node_id + qualifier on node_refs"; container_node_id (→referrer) shipped in v0.7.4 but qualifier did not (verified: v0.7.8 node_refs columns = token, node_id, source_id, node_hash, container_node_id).

ASK: additive `qualifier` column on node_refs — the syntactic receiver/selector text for selector-expression call sites (log.Fatalf → "log", obj.Method() → "obj"; '' for bare calls). Same additive-column pattern as container_node_id; mache's v_refs already carries a qualifier column ('' for mention rows) so the probe-and-prefer wiring is one line on the mache side.

CONSUMERS: mache fatal_call rung-1 (qualifier JOIN _imports.alias → resolve stdlib packages through aliases — kills the custom-logger FP class), fan_out_skew qualifier-awareness on the mention arm (today only capnp binding rows carry it), and every future package-scoped rule. Companion mache bead filed for the rule-side work.

comments.text:
STARTED (isolated worktree, base 00593fb). Orientation done; design decision before implementation:

COLUMN SHAPE: additive `qualifier TEXT` (nullable) on node_refs. On the engine's dual-emit pair, the BARE-token row carries the qualifier text (fmt.Println → row(token='Println', qualifier='fmt'); std::process::exit → row(token='exit', qualifier='std::process'); analytics.events → row(token='events', qualifier='analytics')). The QUALIFIED-token row (token='fmt.Println') carries NULL — its token already embeds the qualifier, and NULLing it means exactly ONE row per qualified call site carries the structural (name, qualifier) pair, so GROUP BY/filter rules never double-count. Genuinely bare calls carry NULL.

DEVIATION FROM BEAD TEXT: bead says "'' for bare calls" — using NULL instead of '': ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN backfills NULL on every legacy row regardless, so '' on fresh rows would create TWO no-qualifier encodings; NULL matches the container_node_id/canonical_kind convention. mache's v_refs (TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '') wraps with COALESCE(qualifier,'') — still the predicted one-line probe-and-prefer change.

SCOPE: node_refs only. All imperative fixups (rust_impl_receiver, python_enclosing_class, js_ts_context_fixups, java_enclosing_type, cpp_enclosing_class) synthesize qualified DEF tokens, never refs — so the engine's @qualifier capture is the single population site. node_defs does NOT gain the column (pinned by test).

PLAN: EXTRACTION_EPOCH 3→4; ExtractedRef::Ref gains Option<String> qualifier; create_qualifier_column migration wired into cmd_parse (unlike container_node_id's unwired-but-pub migration — the epoch-4 re-derive INSERT names the column, so upgrading arenas need the ALTER); RefBatch → 6 cols; JSON emit path gains the field; TABLE_CONTRACT.md row updated. TDD: RED fixtures first (go/java/cpp/rust/sql in leyline-ts; python/js/ts in cli-lib fidelity; new qualifier_column_test.rs e2e + legacy-arena migration + defs-scope pin).
comments.author: rsry-mcp

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED — commit 4ac8d2a on branch worktree-agent-a10067410a46ff2ce (isolated worktree, base 00593fb). NOT pushed, no PR, bead left open per dispatch instructions.

EVIDENCE:
- TDD: 13 new tests written RED first (all failed with "no such column: qualifier"), then green after implementation:
  * leyline-ts refs.rs fixtures (6): go bare-row/dual-emit + chained-selector operand ('a.b'), java receiver invocations, cpp '::' namespace calls, rust '::' scoped calls ('std::process' on the exit row), sql schema-dot ('analytics' on the events row).
  * cli-lib def_ref_extraction_fidelity_test.rs (3): python/javascript/typescript through the full parse_into_conn pipeline.
  * cli-lib qualifier_column_test.rs (4, new file): e2e bare-row shape, qualifier JOIN _imports.alias resolves 'l'→'log' (the fatal_call rung-1 query), legacy-arena ALTER migration (pre-column node_refs table parses successfully and populates qualifier), node_defs-has-no-qualifier scope pin.
- f6_extraction_epoch_invalidation (4) + container_node_id_test (4) + full leyline-ts suite (130) all green.
- task ci: GREEN (exit 0). Two intermediate failures fixed en route: cargo fmt line width; smell gate unwrap_without_expect on the new test helper (switched to .expect, no rebaseline).
- EXTRACTION_EPOCH 3→4 with history entry; create_qualifier_column wired into cmd_parse (unlike container_node_id's unwired pub migration — the epoch-4 re-derive INSERT names the column, so upgrading arenas need the ALTER before the insert transaction).
- mache verified READ-ONLY: smell_refs_views.go consumers use explicit column lists (never SELECT *) and probe-and-prefer via tableHasColumn; v_refs currently emits '' AS qualifier for mention rows — flipping to the real column is the predicted one-line change on mache's side. Zero mache changes required for existing reads.

FILES: rs/ll-open/ts/src/{query_engine.rs,refs.rs,schema.rs}, rs/ll-open/cli-lib/src/cmd_parse.rs, rs/ll-open/cli-lib/tests/{def_ref_extraction_fidelity_test.rs,qualifier_column_test.rs}, docs/TABLE_CONTRACT.md.

B9D1D5 DISCHARGE: yes — b9d1d5 asked for referrer_node_id (shipped as container_node_id in v0.7.4, verified by mache 2026-07-13: "This fully satisfies the ask... Safe to close on your side") plus "optionally also surface qualifier". This lands the qualifier half, so b9d1d5 is fully discharged and both beads can close under the one PR that ships this commit.

title: Port extract_javascript + extract_typescript to .scm behind unchanged fixtures
description:
EXP0 follow-up (extract_go port, PR #216, bead ley-line-open-206d53): swap the hand-written extract_javascript and extract_typescript tree walks in rs/ll-open/ts/src/refs.rs for OnceLock-compiled QueryEngine delegation over queries/javascript/tags.scm and queries/typescript/tags.scm. One bead for both languages — they share the js_extract_var_bindings helper and the TSX grammar is a superset of the JS grammar, so the typescript query is expected to be the javascript query plus TS-specific patterns.

Behavior is pinned by the fixture tests in rs/ll-open/cli-lib/tests/def_ref_extraction_fidelity_test.rs — fixtures pass UNCHANGED.

Known engine-vocabulary boundaries to resolve or document as imperative leaks:
- qualified Class.method defs need the ANCESTOR class name; anchored query patterns match downward only
- js_extract_var_bindings pins κ = "function" on var-bound arrows/function expressions; the engine derives κ from the anchor node kind

Verification: cargo test -p leyline-ts --features javascript,typescript (plus cargo test -p leyline-cli-lib for the fidelity fixtures, and task ci from repo root).

comments.text:
RED recorded. Both extractor bodies swapped to OnceLock QueryEngine delegation over EMPTY query files (queries/javascript/tags.scm, queries/typescript/tags.scm contain only a header comment).

Two findings about the verification command:

1. `cargo test -p leyline-ts --features javascript,typescript` — 62 passed, 0 failed even with empty queries. leyline-ts carries no JS/TS fixture tests; the pinning fixtures live in rs/ll-open/cli-lib/tests/def_ref_extraction_fidelity_test.rs (leyline-cli-lib enables all leyline-ts language features). The RED signal comes from `cargo test -p leyline-cli-lib --test def_ref_extraction_fidelity_test`.

2. The stated command did not even compile before a one-line fix: tests/mache_compat.rs uses `TsLanguage::Go` without a `go` cfg gate, so any feature set omitting `go` failed with E0599 (preexisting on main, exposed by the non-default feature invocation). Gated the file with `#![cfg(feature = "go")]`, matching how refs.rs gates its go test mod.

RED failure list (cargo test -p leyline-cli-lib --test def_ref_extraction_fidelity_test — 5 passed, 4 failed):
- javascript_files_produce_def_ref_rows — "JS file must produce node_defs rows (foo, bar, C); got 0"
- javascript_arrow_and_function_expression_bindings_extract_as_defs — binding "foo" emitted no Def
- typescript_files_produce_def_ref_rows — "TS file must produce node_defs rows (foo, bar, C, method, Shape, Point); got 0"
- typescript_arrow_and_function_expression_bindings_extract_as_defs — binding "foo" emitted no Def

Go/Rust/Python fixtures stay green — the swap is isolated to the JS/TS arms.
comments.author: rsry-mcp

comments.text:
GREEN. Commit ab364992f612bd5fa0da440224f86f41c87a44a2 on branch worktree-agent-a0c2bce5276c78905 (isolated worktree; not pushed, no PR — per dispatch instructions).

What shipped:
- queries/javascript/tags.scm (95 lines): function/generator/class defs, bare method_definition def, lexical_declaration + variable_declaration arrow/function-expression bindings (anchored at the DECLARATION so node_id matches the pre-port emission point; name: (identifier) excludes destructuring), identifier + member_expression call refs (@qualifier dual-emit), four import patterns (default / namespace / named-without-as via !alias negation / named-with-as). @path captures the string_fragment inside the source string, so quote style never matters and empty/side-effect imports emit nothing.
- queries/typescript/tags.scm (122 lines): the javascript query plus four TS-only def patterns (interface_declaration, type_alias_declaration, enum_declaration, abstract_class_declaration). Shared sections byte-identical between the two files — deliberate duplication-as-data; a shared-include mechanism is a future decision, noted below.
- refs.rs: both extractors are now OnceLock QueryEngine delegation + js_ts_context_fixups. Deleted: both hand-written match bodies, js_extract_var_bindings (fully absorbed), js_enclosing_class + ts_enclosing_class (merged into one js_ts_enclosing_class), walk_js_import_specifiers + walk_ts_import_specifiers (absorbed). Net -578/+342 lines across the commit.

Imperative leaks (2), kept in js_ts_context_fixups with constraint comments:
1. Qualified Class.method defs — tree-sitter query patterns match downward from their root; a pattern anchored at method_definition cannot capture the ANCESTOR class name. The fixup walks method_definition → class_body → class(-ish) parent and prepends the qualified form of the engine's bare def (qualified-first order preserved).
2. κ = "function" on var-bound arrows/function expressions — the engine derives canonical_kind from the anchor node's kind; lexical_declaration/variable_declaration anchors would yield κ = None, but the definition recorded is the function the variable binds (load-bearing for mache dead_code/god_file filters).

Shared-include note: the two .scm files' def/ref/import sections are byte-identical except the TS-only block. If a third JS-family grammar lands, a shared-include mechanism becomes worth designing; not built now.

Evidence:
- Differential harness (temporary test, deleted after use): dumped every node_defs/node_refs/_imports row (token, node_id, source_id, container_node_id, canonical_kind) for a 69-row JS+TS corpus covering all four import forms, single/double quotes, import type, side-effect imports, generators, private/static/getter/computed methods, class expressions, object-literal methods, optional chaining, multi-declarator bindings, destructuring skips, new-expressions, and all TS-only defs. Old (git stash) vs new: byte-identical.
- cargo test -p leyline-ts --features javascript,typescript: 62 passed, 0 failed (compiles now only because of the mache_compat.rs go-gate fix noted in the RED comment).
- cargo test -p leyline-cli-lib: all green, 334 unit tests + integration suites including the 9 def_ref_extraction_fidelity_test fixtures (the 4 RED failures now pass; fixture files untouched).
- task ci from repo root: green (fmt, clippy -D warnings, full workspace tests, codegen freshness checks).

Bead left open for review/merge.

title: Port extract_rust to queries/rust/tags.scm behind unchanged fixtures
description:
EXP0 follow-up to bead ley-line-open-206d53 / PR #216 (a7ff5d1): extract_rust's hand-written tree walk becomes data in rs/ll-open/ts/queries/rust/tags.scm, delegated through the generic QueryEngine via OnceLock, same shape as extract_go.

Scope notes:
- Rust qualified call refs are pinned as `std::process::exit` (double-colon join); the engine's dual-emit rule joins `{qualifier}.{name}` with a dot. The join separator becomes per-pattern data via `(#set! qualifier-separator "::")` read from Query::property_settings — documented in the query_engine.rs emission vocabulary.
- Known upward-context arm: the `Receiver::method` qualified def dual-emit (bead ley-line-open-caf423) reads the receiver from an ANCESTOR impl_item/trait_item; tree-sitter patterns match downward only, so anchored matching at the function node cannot express it. Any such arm stays imperative and gets documented here.
- Use-tree flattening (prefix joining across scoped_use_list, unbounded nesting) may be inexpressible; if so that arm stays imperative too.

Verification command: cargo test -p leyline-ts --features rust

comments.text:
RED phase: extract_rust body swapped to QueryEngine delegation (OnceLock + include_str!, same shape as extract_go) with an empty queries/rust/tags.scm.

`cargo test -p leyline-ts --features rust,go` — 75 passed, 6 failed, all with `got []`:
- refs::rust_tests::extract_function_and_method_defs (missing add: [])
- refs::rust_tests::extract_type_kind_defs (missing S: [])

(extract_skips_wildcard_use passes vacuously against the empty query.)

Verification-command correction: `cargo test -p leyline-ts --features rust` alone does not compile on clean main either — tests/mache_compat.rs uses TsLanguage::Go unconditionally with no required-features gate, so the go feature is mandatory. Effective command: `cargo test -p leyline-ts --features rust,go`. Pre-existing gap, out of scope here.
comments.author: rsry-mcp

comments.text:
GREEN. Commit 4504b2b on branch worktree-agent-a304be320f6fc9cc8 (not pushed, no PR — per dispatch instructions).

Evidence:
- `cargo test -p leyline-ts --features rust,go`: lib 81 passed / 0 failed, mache_compat 1 passed. All six RED fixtures now pass; rust_tests fixtures byte-unchanged (diff touches only src/refs.rs non-test code, src/query_engine.rs, and the new queries/rust/tags.scm).
- `cargo clippy -p leyline-ts --features rust,go --all-targets`: clean.
- `task ci` from repo root: exit 0.

What moved to data (queries/rust/tags.scm, 82 lines):
- Defs: function_item, function_signature_item, struct_item, enum_item, union_item, trait_item, type_item, mod_item, const_item, static_item via `name: (_) @name`.
- Call refs: bare identifier; field_expression method (field name only); scoped_identifier dual-emit `mod::func` + `func` with `(#set! qualifier-separator "::")`.
- Macro refs: identifier macros full name; scoped macros bare name only (matches pre-port).
- Imports, single-leaf forms: bare `use foo;` (`@path @alias` on one node), `use a::b;` (alias = name field — the engine's `/`-split alias default never applies to Rust), `use a::b as c;`. Wildcards match no pattern.

Engine change (query_engine.rs): the dual-emit qualifier join separator is now per-pattern data read from Query::property_settings — `(#set! qualifier-separator "::")`, default `.`. Forced by the pinned `std::process::exit` fixture; documented in the emission vocabulary doc-comment. Go behavior untouched (its patterns set no property; go tests pass).

Expressibility leaks kept imperative in extract_rust (both documented in the fn doc-comment and the .scm header):
1. Qualified `Receiver::method` defs (bead ley-line-open-caf423): the receiver is the type/name field of an ANCESTOR impl_item/trait_item; tree-sitter patterns match downward only, so no pattern anchored at the visited function node can capture it. Rooting the pattern at impl_item instead would re-anchor the Def's node_id/canonical_kind to the impl block and double-emit the bare name — a behavior change. rust_impl_receiver stays.
2. Use-tree flattening: `use a::{b, c as d, e::{f}}` requires joining the shared path prefix onto each leaf (string concatenation across sibling captures — @path reads one node's text) and recursing to unbounded depth. collect_use_imports/collect_use_list_child stay, trimmed to list-only arms (single-leaf arms deleted as dead code).

Emission behavior is byte-identical for all fixture-pinned cases; no EXTRACTION_EPOCH bump.

  - python_files_produce_def_ref_rows — panicked at def_ref_extraction_fidelity_test.rs:90: "Python file must produce node_defs rows (foo, bar, C, method); got 0."

This is the fixture that must go GREEN behind an authored tags.scm with byte-identical emission behavior.

comments.text:
GREEN evidence (pending task ci, running now):

- queries/python/tags.scm authored (45 lines, 6 patterns): function/class defs, bare-identifier call refs, attribute-call refs with @qualifier dual-emit (`obj.method` + `method` — the engine's dot-join matches Python natively), plain dotted imports (@alias anchored to the LAST identifier because the engine's alias default splits on `/`, which never fires for dotted Python paths), and aliased imports.
- Two arms stay imperative in extract_python, each with a leak note in refs.rs:
  1. Qualified `Class.method` defs — the qualifier is an ANCESTOR of the anchored node (function_definition → block → class_definition); tree-sitter queries match downward only, and a class-rooted pattern would emit under the class's node_id/canonical_kind instead of the method's. python_enclosing_class helper retained for this arm.
  2. import_from_statement — the emitted path is a `{module}.{name}` JOIN of two captures; the engine's import vocabulary carries exactly one @path and has no join facility.
- Parity verified beyond the thin fixtures: a temporary walk-and-compare harness ran old vs new extractor over a rich fixture (nested defs, class methods, chained attribute calls `a.b.c.run(...)`, `import a.b.c`, `import numpy as np, x.y as z`, from-imports incl. aliased/relative/wildcard) with zero divergence, then was deleted along with the hand-written body (refs.rs: 44 insertions, 142 deletions).
- `cargo test -p leyline-ts --features python,go`: 76 passed, 0 failed. (Bare `--features python` fails to compile tests/mache_compat.rs on base main too — TsLanguage::Go is referenced unconditionally; pre-existing.)
- `cargo test -p leyline-cli-lib --test def_ref_extraction_fidelity_test`: 9 passed, 0 failed — python_files_produce_def_ref_rows flipped RED→GREEN with fixtures untouched.
- EXTRACTION_EPOCH not bumped: emission behavior is byte-identical (parity harness), so no rules change for the invalidation layer.

title: Canonicalize leyline-net wire frames in LLO — ToolCall/ToolResult/Manifest capnp so cloister + rosary vendor from upstream
description:
The leyline-net frame format (length-prefixed Manifest + ChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD envelope + ToolCall/ToolResult payloads, AD-bound manifest) is spec'd in cloister/wire/cloister.capnp (cloister ADR-0005) and VENDORED by rosary as schemas/cloister.capnp (rosary-6371e3, build.rs capnpc codegen for `rsry mcp --ipc-socket`). Verified 2026-07-15: LLO has no copy — schema-capnp holds ast/binding/cache/head/source/common, public-schema holds daemon/cache, schema-spec holds _traits + capability specs. A leyline-named substrate protocol currently has its canonical copy in a cloister subdirectory, and rosary re-vendors from there in lockstep by hand.

Per ADR-0035 ("leyline-* names belong in LLO") and the leyline-sign / leyline-schema-spec lift precedents:
- Split cloister/wire/cloister.capnp: the GENERIC wire frames (Manifest, ToolCall, ToolResult, frame layout + AEAD binding rules) move to LLO (rs/ll-core/schema-capnp/schemas/net.capnp or a new leyline-net spec dir under schema-spec with pinned test vectors, matching the VECTORS.sha256 discipline); cloister-deployment-specific structs stay in cloister/wire.
- Exact-pin capnp toolchain discipline applies (LLO ADR-0014 — canonical bytes must not move under consumers).
- Downstream (separate beads at PR time): cloister repoints wire/cloister.capnp to import/extend upstream; rosary re-vendors schemas/cloister.capnp from LLO instead of cloister (rosary follow-up bead depends on this landing).
- Ship cross-runtime test vectors so the Rust (rosary), TS (cloister), and companion sides prove byte-equality against the same pinned frames.

comments.text:
ORIENTATION COMPLETE (worktree agent-a6b424d3601dc1503). Findings that shape the implementation:

1. Current layout confirmed: canonical frames live in cloister/wire/cloister.capnp (@0xa1c0157e2a1e0001); rosary vendors a verbatim copy at schemas/cloister.capnp (header + Go annotations differ, struct bodies byte-identical). LLO has no copy.

2. Frame structs are ALL generic: Manifest, ToolCall, ToolResult, Content, BinaryContent — nothing cloister-deployment-specific is in wire/cloister.capnp beyond prose. The AEAD envelope (manifest-length/manifest/nonce/ciphertext) is spec prose in the header; per the ADR-0005 intra-cluster amendment, cloister-companion HTTP and rosary `--ipc-socket` UDS both exchange PLAIN canonical capnp ToolCall/ToolResult today (no Manifest envelope, no AEAD on those paths). The load-bearing cross-runtime bytes are the canonical struct encodings.

3. Cross-runtime vector anchor exists: cloister/wire/cross-check-fixtures.capnp defines 12 consts; test/wire/fixtures/canonical.ts pins their `capnp eval -b` bytes, validated by cloister's hand-rolled TS codec + Go bindings. I regenerated all 12 via `capnp eval -I .. --no-standard-import wire/cross-check-fixtures.capnp <const> -b` — byte-identical to canonical.ts. These are the golden frames.

4. Encoding-form finding (empirically verified with capnp =0.25.0 against the CURRENT cloister schema): `capnp eval -b` reference bytes are NOT strict canonical form for 9/12 values — set_root_canonical truncates trailing zero data/pointer words (e.g. tool-call-empty 56B→48B, tool-result-empty 40B→32B). Cloister's own cross-check tests state value-equivalence, not byte-equivalence, across encoders. Consequence: LLO pins BOTH forms per value — reference/ (byte-equal to cloister's pinned fixtures; proves compatibility with what TS+Go validate today) and canonical/ (set_root_canonical form, LLO's T8.10 fixture discipline; baseline digests captured from the cloister schema pre-split so the split is provably byte-neutral in both encodings).

5. Plan (follows build-cache/v1 + T8.10 precedent): (a) schemas/net.capnp in schema-capnp, same ordinals/types, new fileId 0xa25bb2a310446125 (new ID is REQUIRED — cloister will import this file downstream and two files cannot share an ID; fileId does not affect struct wire bytes); fileid_allowlist + lib.rs module + regen.sh Go bindings. (b) schema-spec/leyline-net/v1/ spec dir: README + wire docs + test-vectors (12 reference + 12 canonical .bin) + digests.json + VECTORS.sha256; VECTORS_FILES extended in verify_vectors_sha256.rs. (c) Tests in schema-capnp: digest-pinned consistency (hardcoded BLAKE3/SHA-256, fail-loud on frame edits), encode-canonical byte-equality, decode-direction field equality against reference bytes, capnp-eval byte-equality via an LLO fixtures.capnp, and a skip-if-missing cross-repo gate against the live cloister schema (build_cache_vectors_consistency pattern).

Downstream unchanged: cloister repoint + rosary re-vendor (rosary-086973) are separate beads; rosary untouched.
comments.author: rsry-mcp

comments.text:
SHIPPED (not pushed, no PR — per dispatch constraints). Branch worktree-agent-a6b424d3601dc1503, commit 9962c8cee58e3bc25291eb0a025b496b7ac2ce07. `task ci` green from repo root (full common gate: check, clippy, fmt, lint:blake3, smells, fs builds, tier:isolation, test, sign:host, compat:check, gen:server-json:check, readme:version-check). Go module: GOWORK=off go build+test ./... green incl. new net package.

WHAT LANDED
1. rs/ll-core/schema-capnp/schemas/net.capnp — canonical leyline-net frames (Manifest/ToolCall/ToolResult/Content/BinaryContent), identical ordinals+types to cloister/wire/cloister.capnp, new fileId 0xa25bb2a310446125 (registered in fileid_allowlist.rs with rationale; new ID is forced by capnp import mechanics — downstream cloister will import this file, two files can't share an ID; fileIds never reach struct wire bytes). lib.rs exports net_capnp; Go bindings generated (clients/go/leyline-schema/net, capnpc-go v3.1.0-alpha.2 = CI pin) + regen.sh row.
2. rs/ll-core/schema-spec/leyline-net/v1/ — README (spec proper + downstream table + version policy), wire/frames.md (struct ordinal tables, envelope + AEAD binding rules, canonical-encoding contract, the two byte-forms), test-vectors/ (fixtures.capnp + 12 values × 2 forms + digests.json + VECTORS.sha256).
3. Gates: tests/leyline_net_vectors.rs (7 tests: BLAKE3 pins hardcoded in test source; committed-file byte-equality; decode field-equality both forms; capnp-eval on LLO fixtures; skip-if-missing cross-repo eval against live cloister schema), tests/net_vector_values/mod.rs (shared builders, #[path]-included by generator + test so they can't disagree), examples/gen_leyline_net_vectors.rs (deterministic regen), verify_vectors_sha256.rs extension, capability-mapping §4 row (`n/a (substrate-internal)` | cloister/leyline-net/v1), version_bump gate satisfied (digests.json version = "cloister/leyline-net/v1"), README/CHANGELOG updates.

WIRE-COMPAT PROOF (TDD order followed)
- Step 1 (before any LLO schema existed): generated all 12 golden vectors from the CURRENT truth via `capnp eval -b` on cloister's cross-check-fixtures.capnp; verified byte-identical to cloister's committed test/wire/fixtures/canonical.ts (the bytes cloister's TS codec + Go bindings already validate). Also captured strict-canonical (set_root_canonical) baselines from the cloister schema via a scratch capnp=0.25.0 crate.
- Step 2: built net.capnp; all 24 vectors regenerated from the LLO schema are byte-equal to the cloister-schema baselines (verified by cmp, then mechanized as the BLAKE3 pins + the two capnp-eval gates). Zero wire bytes moved.
- Empirical finding worth keeping: capnp eval -b output is NOT strict canonical form for 9/12 values (canonicalization truncates trailing zero data/pointer words: tool-call-empty 56→48B, tool-result-empty 40→32B, manifest-zero-sequence 176→168B, etc.). Cloister's cross-check suite claims value-equivalence, not byte-equivalence, across encoders — consistent. Both forms are pinned (reference/ + canonical/); the spec text states decoders MUST accept both.
- Reference-form digests (sha256, cross-checkable against cloister's canonical.ts): manifest-canonical 9fa3eeb2…ebc2, tool-call-basic bdcd6632…607c, tool-result-mixed a8bfe3b2…d8cd (full set in digests.json + VECTORS.sha256).

SCOPE NOTES / DOWNSTREAM
- ADR-0005 amendment matters for consumers: cloister↔companion HTTP and rosary --ipc-socket UDS exchange PLAIN capnp ToolCall/ToolResult (no Manifest envelope, no AEAD). The envelope + AEAD binding rules are spec'd in net.capnp header + wire/frames.md; no AEAD implementation was added to LLO (no current in-repo consumer; adding chacha20poly1305/ed25519 vector machinery would pin bytes nothing produces — revisit when a network-crossing consumer lands).
- rosary-086973 re-vendors FROM rs/ll-core/schema-capnp/schemas/net.capnp (replacing schemas/cloister.capnp vendored from cloister). rosary's copy differs from cloister's only in header comments + Go annotations; struct bodies identical — re-vendor is mechanical. rosary untouched here.
- cloister repoint (separate bead at PR time): wire/cloister.capnp keeps its fileId and imports/extends these definitions, or re-vendors verbatim; its cross-check-fixtures.capnp can re-root on the vendored copy. The skip-if-missing cross-repo test in LLO will catch any divergence while both copies exist.

Bead left open per dispatch instructions (no push/PR yet).

title: ADR-0036 Phase 2 (cloister): lift schema-bridge → rs/ll-open/schema-bridge (overdue trigger — Phase 1 closed)
description:
Cloister ADR-0036 committed to a two-phase generalization of tools/schema-bridge (capnp → multi-output codegen). Phase 1 is DONE in cloister: output multiplexer (binary-name dispatch, capnpc-schema-bridge-{zod,go}), Go output, round-trip verify + drift gates (task cluster:{zod,go}:verify), and the second-schema proof (manifest/identity.capnp, cloister-77172d). The ADR says the Phase 2 bead is filed when Phase 1 closes — that never happened (verified 2026-07-15: no schema-bridge bead in ley-line-open; only ae89aa cache.capnp, unrelated).

Lift per the leyline-sign precedent (LLO PR #160 / cloister-8f4d3f pattern):
- Move cloister/tools/schema-bridge → rs/ll-open/schema-bridge (crate name per ADR-0035 naming: leyline-schema-bridge or schema-bridge under the ll-open workspace).
- Stays a build-time codegen binary: no daemon, no port, no SQLite sidecar (same posture as leyline-cas-ffi).
- License AGPL-3.0-or-later (already is).
- Keep the self-maintenance invariant: any unmapped capnp construct is a hard error, never silent z.unknown().
- Cloister-side follow-up bead (to file in cloister at PR time): consume upstream via git dep, delete tools/schema-bridge, repoint Taskfile cluster:{zod,go} targets.

First new consumer after lift: rosary needs a json-schema output (MCP inputSchema from an annotated capnp tool registry — rosary ADR-0006 revival). The bridge's own README already declares JSON Schema as the planned next output; scope it here as a follow-up bead or stretch goal, not a lift blocker.

comments.text:
Lift in progress on worktree branch worktree-agent-a61ca77bba74669ad (base = main @ 8233a34). Orientation findings + what landed:

**Source of truth**: cloister/docs/adr/0036-schema-bridge-multi-output.md — Phase 2 = move cloister/tools/schema-bridge → ley-line-open/rs/ll-open/schema-bridge, leyline-sign lift pattern (file move + license + workspace integration, no logic change). Phase 1 (A+B+C+D+E) confirmed shipped in cloister (multiplexer, Go emitter, void marshalers, round-trip gate, identity.capnp second-schema proof).

**What landed**:
- Crate at rs/ll-open/schema-bridge, package `leyline-schema-bridge` v0.7.9 edition 2024 (workspace convention — every ll-open crate is leyline-*), license AGPL-3.0-or-later (unchanged). Bin names `capnpc-schema-bridge-zod` / `capnpc-schema-bridge-go` kept verbatim — they are the capnp PATH-dispatch contract.
- Deps migrated to workspace inheritance: capnp `0.24` → workspace `=0.25.0` (compiles with zero code change), thiserror workspace. indoc stays a literal dev-dep (not workspace-declared, so no drift-gate conflict).
- `unwrap_without_expect` smell gate fired on the new crate (38 findings, zod.rs). Per the rule's drawdown discipline (convert truly-infallible unwraps to expect, don't grow the baseline), all 94 `write!`-to-String `.unwrap()`s in zod.rs + go.rs became `.expect("write! to String is infallible")`. Baseline untouched. Only other deltas vs cloister: this repo's rustfmt reflow + lib-path rename in main.rs/tests.
- README updated for the new home (provenance para, cloister-Taskfile framing, layout path, license follow-on item removed as resolved). CHANGELOG Unreleased entry added.
- verify-go.sh NOT lifted — it is cloister-repo orchestration (pnpm tsx dump of src/generated/cluster.ts + go test ./pkg/cluster), meaningless in LLO; it stays with cloister's Taskfile until the cloister-side follow-up repoints it.

**External consumers of the old path**: none. mache's only hit is a vendored rosary test snapshot; rosary's is an example string in a tool description; cloister itself consumes via its Taskfile (its follow-up bead — consume upstream git dep, delete tools/schema-bridge, repoint cluster:/identity: targets — is to be filed in cloister at PR time per this bead's description). No re-export shims needed.

**Evidence**: cargo test -p leyline-schema-bridge → 34 passed, 0 failed, 2 ignored (the two aspirational stubs, by design). clippy -D warnings clean (3 needless_borrows_for_generic_args in tests fixed), fmt clean. End-to-end verified through the real capnp CLI: `capnp compile -o…capnpc-schema-bridge-zod:gen tests/fixtures/with_const.capnp` emits with_const.zod.ts, `-go` variant emits with_const.go — argv[0] dispatch intact post-rename.

**Follow-up filed**: ley-line-open-6585aa — json-schema output (rosary MCP inputSchema, first post-lift consumer). Full `task ci` re-run in flight; commit lands after it is green.

title: CI: unpin nightly rustc when rustc-nightly ICE on #[serial(...)] rolls out
description:
## Watch bead

`rustc 1.99.0-nightly (daf2e5e18 2026-07-13)` ICEs on `#[test] + #[serial(<key>)]` combos (fires on `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/src/daemon/sheaf_ablation.rs:159`). Workaround: `.github/workflows/{ci,release,find-smells}.yml` pin to `nightly-2026-07-12` (last known-good). Applied in PR #198 (merged 539027a).

## When to close

- Run `rustup toolchain install nightly` locally + `task ci` — if green, remove the pin from all three workflows and confirm CI on a fresh PR.
- OR: someone else notices the upstream rustc issue was fixed and unpins.

## Files

- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
- `.github/workflows/release.yml`
- `.github/workflows/find-smells.yml`

## Verifiable close gate

`grep 'toolchain: nightly' .github/workflows/*.yml` returns exactly `toolchain: nightly` (no date suffix) AND CI on the unpin PR passes.

title: SAFETY-comment audit + gate — 143 unsafe sprawl resolved via 3 PRs (baseline reaches ZERO)
description:
## Goal

Every `unsafe` use in `rs/*/src/**/*.rs` documented with either `// SAFETY:` or `/// # Safety`, at the right layer of abstraction (typed helpers where the same invariant repeats, per-site otherwise). Regression gate to prevent future sprawl.

## Shipped (2026-07-14)

- **PR #199 (5dc2d7d)** — `unsafe_without_safety` JSON smell rule + projection in `cargo-toml-projector`, wired into `task smells`. Grandfathers 59 unannotated sites into `docs/smell-baseline.json`.
- **PR #200 (5e92620)** — 9 per-site annotations (libc syscalls + flock + sqlite-vec extension) + projector improvement to skip type-level `unsafe fn(...)` in transmute-target types. Baseline 59 → 50.
- **PR #201 (f03bda4)** — new `rs/ll-core/ffi-helpers/` (zero-dep, no_std) crate: `c_input`/`c_output`/`c_cstr`/`c_ref`. Refactored `sign/ffi.rs`, `cas-ffi/ffi.rs`, `fs/lib.rs`. Projector `doc_block_ending_at` walker so rustdoc `# Safety` sections are detected regardless of block length. Baseline 50 → 7.
- **PR #202 (5049d79)** — new `leyline_core::mmap::{mmap_read, mmap_write}` helpers. Refactored all 7 remaining `Mmap::map` / `MmapMut::map_mut` call sites. **Baseline 7 → 0.**

## Result

- `task smells` reports 0 unsafe_without_safety findings.
- Regression gate active: any NEW unannotated `unsafe` fails CI immediately.
- ~143 unsafe → ~35 production unsafe sites, every one annotated with `// SAFETY:` or `/// # Safety` docstring, or lives inside a documented helper (`c_input`, `c_output`, `c_cstr`, `c_ref`, `mmap_read`, `mmap_write`).
- 3 new unit tests on `mmap` module, 12 on `ffi-helpers`, 17 on projector tokenizer. Load-bearing tests pin every false-positive shape observed during bootstrapping.

## Verifiable close gate

`task smells` — 0 findings. All 4 PRs green on CI. Merged.

title: wal_pool_concurrent_readers_daemon_test::concurrent_readers_beat_pre_15b_mutex_shape flakes under workspace-parallel task ci load
description:
## Symptom

`concurrent_readers_beat_pre_15b_mutex_shape` (pin test for bead `ley-line-open-f0239d` WAL 15b — pool + writer split) intermittently fails during `task ci` with scale below the 3× floor:

Observed this session (2026-07-13):
- run 1: `scale=2.95×` (mutex=42107, pool=124076)
- run 2: `scale=2.06×` (mutex=60851, pool=125519)

Passes in isolation (`cargo test -p leyline-cli-lib --test wal_pool_concurrent_readers_daemon_test concurrent_readers_beat_pre_15b_mutex_shape` — debug OR release, hit rate 100%).

## Root cause

The assertion is `pool_reads / mutex_reads >= 3.0`. The **pool** number is stable (~124–125k across both fail runs — the pool shape works). The **mutex baseline** swings 40k → 60k → 90k depending on co-tenant CPU load during workspace-parallel test runs. When the mutex phase happens to hit a quiet moment, its throughput doubles and the ratio collapses below 3× — a pass/fail flip on pure co-tenant noise, not on any real change in the pool shape.

The test measures the wrong invariant: what the pool actually buys is "reads parallelize instead of serializing." A ratio against a wobbly baseline is contaminated by whatever else `cargo test --workspace` is scheduling.

## Fix (proposed)

`#[serial]` decorate the test — it's a wall-clock throughput comparison, and serial_test is already a workspace dev-dep. Excluding it from parallel workspace-test scheduling is legitimate (it's a perf test that needs isolation), not a band-aid. The 3-second wall-clock cost is a one-time hit in `task ci`.

Belt-and-suspenders: additionally add an absolute pool-floor assertion (`pool_reads >= 50_000` in 1.5s — well below the stable ~124k, above the ~40k mutex-shape a real regression would drop to) so a hypothetical pool regression to serialized behavior still fails loud even without the ratio.

## Verifiable close gate

`cargo test -p leyline-cli-lib --test wal_pool_concurrent_readers_daemon_test concurrent_readers_beat_pre_15b_mutex_shape` — must pass 5× in a row under `task ci` (workspace-parallel) load, not just in isolation. Loop:

```
for i in 1 2 3 4 5; do task ci || exit 1; done
```

## Files

- `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/tests/wal_pool_concurrent_readers_daemon_test.rs` (add `#[serial]` decoration + absolute floor)
- `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/Cargo.toml` (already has `serial_test` per bead `ley-line-open-*sheaf_ablation*`; confirm)

title: node_defs: add canonical_kind column — closes mache dead_code / god_file rule parity
description:
Cross-repo follow-up to `ley-line-open-6e798d` from mache's 2026-07-13 finding.

## Symptom

Mache observed `dead_code = 321` on the LLO projection vs tree-sitter's `5`. Root cause: LLO's `node_defs` emits defs for every module-level named binding in Rust (functions, methods, types, mods, consts, statics) — mache-schema only exports functions/methods/types. Mache-rule filter over LLO's projection would need a JOIN through `node_content.kind` to distinguish.

## Fix

Additive `canonical_kind TEXT` column on `node_defs` populated at parse time via `TsLanguage::canonical_kind(node.kind())`. One-column filter for consumers (`WHERE canonical_kind IN ('function', 'method', 'type')`) — no JOIN needed. Same additive-migration pattern as `container_node_id` (v0.7.4, bead 6e798d).

## Deliverable

- `rs/ll-open/ts/src/schema.rs` — DDL updates + `insert_def` signature + `create_canonical_kind_column` migration
- `rs/ll-open/ts/src/refs.rs` — `ExtractedRef::Def.canonical_kind: Option<&'static str>` + all extractors emit
- `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/src/cmd_parse.rs` — split `RefBatch` (5 cols) from new `DefBatch` (6 cols)
- Partial index `idx_defs_canonical_kind` for O(log n) filter

## Close condition

`cargo test -p leyline-cli-lib --test canonical_kind_defs_test` green (3 pin tests). `task ci` clean.

title: leyline-sign cert_chain: add the confinementDigest Interlace extension (lane-2 identity commit for confinement/v1 §7)
description:
## Why
cloister/confinement/v1 §7 commits the `confinementDigest` (BLAKE3-256 of the §6-canonical ConfinementManifest) to the bundle's **lane-2 workload identity** so a runner enforcing a different manifest than the identity-committed one fails at the trust boundary. `_capability-mapping.md` §4: "the bundle-side commit lives in lane-2 (workload identity)." That commit is a **cert extension**, and the Interlace cert extensions are canonically here — `rs/ll-open/sign/src/cert_chain.rs`, OID arc `1.3.6.1.4.1.99999.1.*`:
- `.4` epoch (DER INTEGER), `.5` peer_fp (UTF8String), `.6` scope (UTF8String).

There is **no confinementDigest extension yet**. cloister's a34edc (#129, merged) shipped the digest computation + conformance but can't complete §7 until this exists.

## Scope
1. Add OID `1.3.6.1.4.1.99999.1.7` = **interlace-confinement-digest**, encoded as a DER **OctetString** of the 32-byte BLAKE3-256 digest.
2. Parse it in `verify_cert_chain` → add `confinement_digest: Option<[u8; 32]>` to `CertClaims` (Optional — pre-existing certs without it stay valid; the field is None).
3. Emit it in `tests_helpers::mint_test_cert` (add an optional `confinement_digest` param) so dev/test minters + cloister's mint-dev-cert can commit it.
4. Document the extension in the cert_chain.rs header comment (alongside .4/.5/.6) + interlace-spec / _capability-mapping.

## Consumers (cross-repo)
- cloister `src/wire/signet-verify.ts` + `src/routes/lease-middleware.ts` — extract + compare to the bundle's declared confinement facet digest (cloister-side bead, depends on this).
- notme — mints the extension on bundle workload certs (production).

## Non-goals
- No enforcement runner (that's the compute-substrate, ADR-0044).
- Keep the wasm verify artifact byte-stable except the new optional extension parse.

comments.text:
Shipped as part of v0.7.6 (tag pushed).

title: Emit referrer_node_id (+ qualifier) on node_refs — enclosing-def linkage for ref-based consumer rules
description:
From mache's smell-gate parity investigation (mache-608a3c). node_refs currently emits (token, node_id, source_id, node_hash) where node_id = the FULL AST path of the call_expression SITE (leaf), e.g. `api/schema.go/method_declaration/block/.../call_expression`. Consumer rules that aggregate refs BY CALLER cannot work: mache's fan_out_skew (COUNT DISTINCT callee GROUP BY caller) and untested_function (was this def called from within a Test* function?) both need the ENCLOSING DEFINITION of each ref, but today every ref has a unique call-site node_id so GROUP BY yields n=1 -> 0 findings on the LLO projection (vs 33 and 9 on tree-sitter). Confirmed still absent in v0.7.3.

Ask: add a referrer_node_id column to node_refs = node_id of the nearest enclosing definition (function_declaration / method_declaration / function_item / etc.) of the ref site. Derivable from the AST already built (the enclosing def IS a real node_defs.node_id -- verified `api/schema.go/method_declaration` exists in node_defs). Makes ref-based rules backend-agnostic (matches the mache-schema projection semantics) instead of forcing consumers into brittle call-site-path string surgery. Optionally also surface qualifier (LHS of the selector expr; BindingRecord.qualifier / T8.7 already exists) as a column for fan_out's distinct-origin metric.

Wire impact: additive column, backward-compatible; no existing consumer breaks.

comments.text:
REFINED after inspecting v0.7.3 db. Two SEPARATE needs — don't conflate:

1. KIND normalization (LLO agent's canonical_kind proposal): NOT NEEDED to unblock mache. node_content already has a `kind` column that IS mostly-canonical (function_declaration/function_item/function_definition/function -> 'function'; method_declaration/method_definition -> 'method'), node_hash-joinable from node_defs/node_refs/_ast. mache can filter kind via JOIN today. Ship canonical_kind as a proper closed κ vocabulary if wanted for the decade (T3 taint, self-analysis; completes the tail: generic_function/function_signature_item/method_elem don't collapse) — clean but optional for smells.

2. CALLER/ENCLOSING-DEF linkage (THIS bead — the real blocker): node_refs.node_id = the call-SITE path (unique leaf). fan_out_skew needs to GROUP BY the enclosing caller def; untested_function's test-caller arm needs to know a ref fired from inside a Test* function. mache CANNOT derive this cleanly (only brittle call-site-path prefix surgery). This appears to be your existing `container_node_id` shape — if a `container_node_id` (= nearest enclosing def node_id, matching a node_defs.node_id) lands on node_refs, that fully satisfies this bead. Please align to that column name; PRIORITIZE over canonical_kind for smell parity.
comments.author: rsry-mcp

comments.text: SHIPPED in v0.7.4 ("container_node_id closes mache smell-rule parity") and VERIFIED by mache: node_refs now has a container_node_id column (+ idx_refs_container). On mache-on-mache: 81735 refs, 5439 distinct containers, 80957 (99%) with container_node_id matching a node_defs.node_id; the ~778 non-matching are top-level/file-scope refs. `SELECT container_node_id, COUNT(DISTINCT token) FROM node_refs GROUP BY container_node_id HAVING n>=5` reproduces the fan_out_skew shape (cmd/serve.go/function_declaration_1=94, cmd/cache.go/function_declaration_7=81, ...). This fully satisfies the ask — mache just aliases v_refs.referrer_node_id := container_node_id (mache-ba3dc6). Thanks! (canonical_kind, the separate/optional piece, is coming in v0.7.5.) Safe to close on your side.
comments.author: rsry-mcp

title: node_refs / node_defs: emit container_node_id (nearest κ function/method ancestor) — closes mache smell-rule parity gap
description:
## Problem

Mache's smell rules (`fan_out_skew`, `untested_function`, `dead_code`, `god_file`) were authored against mache's tree-sitter schema-inferred projection where `nodes.id` looks like `pkg/functions/Foo/source` — the containing function's identity. LLO's `cmd_parse` writes `node_refs.node_id` / `node_defs.node_id` as the AST-native path to the CALL SITE / DECLARATION SITE (e.g. `main.go/function_declaration_0/block/call_expression_0`).

The parity gap surfaces as:
- `fan_out_skew`: 33 → 0 (GROUP BY referrer_node_id groups by call site, not by function; every group has n=1 → filter `n >= 5` kills all rows)
- `untested_function`: 9 → 0 (rule filters `node_id LIKE '%/functions/%'`, LLO's shape never matches)
- `dead_code`: 5 → 321 (LLO writes defs at every declaration site; mache-schema only exports one def per function; explosion of FP noise)
- `god_file`: 2 → 25 (same shape as dead_code — wider def set per file)

## Deliverable

Add a `container_node_id TEXT` column to `node_refs` and `node_defs` — the node_id of the nearest ancestor whose canonical kind (`TsLanguage::canonical_kind`) is `function` or `method`. NULL when no such ancestor exists (top-level refs).

Computation: threaded through `cmd_parse::fold_children` where the AST walk already tracks the parent chain. Free from an ancestor stack maintained alongside the existing fold state.

Consumers can `GROUP BY container_node_id` (a stable, structural signal) instead of `referrer_node_id`, and mache's rules regain semantic parity without further changes.

## Downstream

- Mache's `fan_out_skew` / `untested_function` / `dead_code` / `god_file` rules regain the tree-sitter counts on LLO projection.
- T3 taint reachability (analysis-substrate decade) wants call-graph edges at containing-function granularity — this column is a load-bearing input.
- Cross-repo consumers reading LLO projections gain a structural "which function does this ref/def belong to?" signal without recomputing it.

## Non-goals (v1)

- Nested closures / lambda expressions: `container_node_id` reflects the nearest lexical function/method ancestor, not the enclosing lambda's lexical parent. Nested closure attribution can layer on top later.
- Backfill for legacy .dbs: additive column; older DBs read as NULL.

## Files

- `rs/ll-open/ts/src/schema.rs` — DDL for the new column on `node_refs` + `node_defs`
- `rs/ll-open/ts/src/refs.rs` — extraction signature carries the current container node_id
- `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/src/cmd_parse.rs` — fold state carries the ancestor stack; `insert_ref` / `insert_def` write the column
- `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/tests/container_node_id_test.rs` — unit test that a Go function containing 3 calls produces 3 `node_refs` rows all with the same `container_node_id` = function's node_id

## Close condition

`cargo test -p leyline-cli-lib --test container_node_id_test` green. Additionally, a mache-side smoke test (out of scope of this bead) reproduces `fan_out_skew` count parity between tree-sitter and LLO projections after mache's rules are updated to `GROUP BY container_node_id`.

## Priority

P2. Blocks mache's LLO-projection consumption story; unblocks the semantic-parity claim their contract test was designed to catch.

title: confinement/v1: the BLAKE3-256 confinementDigest is unpinned — §6/§8 reference a pin that VECTORS.sha256 holds as SHA-256
description:
## Finding (cloister-side validation, 2026-07-13)
Cloister validated confinement/v1 (v0.7.2) before consuming it (cloister-a34edc) and found the load-bearing content-addressed property is untestable as shipped.

- §6 and §8 state conformance = "reach the **BLAKE3-256** digest pinned in `VECTORS.sha256`." §7 makes `confinementDigest` (BLAKE3-256 of the canonical bytes) the value committed to the lane-2 Interlace identity — the whole "can't drift" cryptographic property.
- But `VECTORS.sha256` holds a **SHA-256** (`899f4f1449aa970c7c20f4bc2ddd7eb6905f1ad2c3c4985d8aa1dcc495f8169e` = `shasum -a 256` of `test-vectors/manifest-canonical.json` — a file-integrity hash). No BLAKE3 hex appears anywhere in the contract dir, and the restored cargo tests (`verify_vectors_sha256`, `version_bump_on_vector_change`, `capability_mapping_coverage`) don't test a BLAKE3 digest.
- So a second implementation following §8.2 has **no pinned BLAKE3 to reach**. The cross-impl agreement that makes confinementDigest trustworthy is unverified.

## The value (computed via cloister's substrate hash)
cloister computed BLAKE3-256 of the canonical vector via `leyline-cas-ffi::leyline_hash_bytes` (== `blake3::hash`, per cloister-cas's `hash_via_re_export_matches_blake3` test):

**confinementDigest(manifest-canonical.json) = `d9b5b7270bb6e5ec068aec92798dd76b0f71d1fe2640b3a09833b7742d51c617`**

(395 input bytes; the file is already §6-canonical — sorted keys, 2-space indent, last byte `}`.) This is cloister's half of the cross-impl check; if LLO's blake3 crate agrees, that's conformance proven.

## Fix
1. Pin the BLAKE3-256 confinementDigest — a `test-vectors/manifest-canonical.blake3` file, or a clearly-labeled BLAKE3 line in the vectors (distinct from the SHA-256 integrity pin).
2. Add a `verify_confinement_digest` cargo test: BLAKE3-256 of the canonical vector bytes == the pin (assert it equals `d9b5b727…`).
3. Clarify §6/§8 wording: `VECTORS.sha256` = SHA-256 *file integrity*; the *confinementDigest* is a separate BLAKE3-256 with its own pin.

## Blocks
cloister-a34edc's §8.2 cross-impl conformance step (the facet + Inv 10 + digest computation can proceed; the "prove cloister's digest matches LLO's" step waits on this).

title: Decade dataflow-substrate: staging-layer for reflow-accurate offsets (raised by T1.b3 F1 authoring)
description:
Decade-level open question surfaced while writing the F1_cfg_reflow_stable gate in T1.b3 (`ley-line-open-46f7d1`).

## The gap

`_cfg.node_hash` IS reflow-invariant (ADR-0027 merkle-AST content address; whitespace-invariant by construction). But `_cfg.entry_offset` / `_cfg.exit_offset` are body-*source*-relative — they measure position in the raw text of the body, which includes interior whitespace. Reflow shifts them.

The F1 gate had to be narrowed from "byte-identical row content" to "byte-identical row COUNT + node_hash + block_kinds", with offsets deliberately excluded from the invariant. That works for T3's differential-dataflow `arrange` (which keys on node_hash + block_id) but it means consumers reading offsets after dedup get **first-writer's source-relative positions**, which are only valid for that specific parse of that specific file.

## The right fix: staging layer

Offsets should be positions INTO A CANONICAL STAGED FORM of the body, not into raw source. Two candidates, both already in the substrate:

1. **Pre-serialized capnp `AstNodeList` blob** (ADR-0026 Phase 1 today, per-file). Phase 2 (`ley-line-open-ddfc1c`) explicitly names per-semantic-unit refinement — that's this staging layer. `_ast_pointer.offset_in_blob` is already exactly this shape for AST nodes.
2. **The linearized fold buffer** the merkle hash consumes (κ kind + tokens + child hashes) — content-addressed by construction, portable.

If `_cfg.entry_offset` / `_cfg.exit_offset` were offsets into a per-body staged blob (option 1) instead of body-source-relative, reflow would be fully byte-identical — offsets included — AND sub-file parsing fits by direct extension (staging IS the sub-file unit).

## Impact on already-shipped work

- T1.b2's schema doesn't need to change *shape* — `entry_offset INTEGER, exit_offset INTEGER` still works. Only the *semantics* flip. No migration needed; rows already merged in v0.7.x carry source-relative offsets and can be respected as "legacy source-relative" while new rows carry blob-relative.
- T1.b3's cfg.rs and F1 gate DON'T change — they're offset-agnostic under the narrowed invariant.
- T1.b4 (cyclomatic complexity, `ley-line-open-471195`) is offset-agnostic — safe to land unchanged.
- T2 (dfg-ssa) is offset-agnostic — safe to land unchanged.

## When to decide

**Before T3.b3 (Bootstrap-mode taint, `ley-line-open-485113`).** T3's differential-dataflow arrangement compounds on content-addressed keys; if downstream consumers start depending on the offset semantics before we lock them, migration cost climbs.

## Scope

1. Add a decade-doc section naming the staging layer explicitly (docs/decades/analysis-substrate.md §2 or §5).
2. Decide: canonical staged form = ADR-0026 pointer blob (per-body variant) OR fold buffer OR something else. Coordinate with `ley-line-open-ddfc1c` (ADR-0026 Phase 2) so we don't fork the pointer-store discipline.
3. Migrate cfg.rs to emit blob-relative offsets. Rewrite F1 to assert byte-identical row CONTENT (including offsets) under reflow.
4. Companion change in T2's `_dfg` and T3's `_taint` when they land.

## Close condition

`cargo test -p leyline-cli-lib --test f1_cfg_reflow_stable` passes with STRENGTHENED invariant (row bytes byte-identical, offsets included) under reflow. Task ci green.

## Priority

P2. Non-blocking for T1.b4 / T2 / T3.b1-b2, but blocking for T3.b3 shape decision.

title: schema-spec: add confinement/v1 vendor-neutral contract (kernel-confinement IDL, sibling to credential-isolation/v1)
description:
## Why
The confinement policy cloister-harness applies (a nono `CapabilityManifest`: fs allow-list + fail-closed network mode + port allowlist + credential source) has **no vendor-neutral wire contract**. Today it's hand-emitted in `cloister/scripts/harness-dev.mjs` (shipped S1: cloister #126/#128) and validated only at the enforcement point (the Rust binary refuses a non-`blocked`/`proxy` network — default-deny by refusal).

For the "one declaration drives BOTH isolation planes" unison (the S3 target), the confinement policy needs to be a declared substrate contract, exactly like `credential-isolation/v1` — so the cloister-side facet (a `cluster.capnp` Bundle field) can consume it, and the kernel-plane enforcement (nono manifest / libkrun mediator) derives from the same source.

## State (verified 2026-07-13)
LLO `rs/ll-core/schema-spec/` has THREE v1 contracts: `build-cache/v1`, `credential-isolation/v1`, `mcp-tool/v1`. **No confinement contract exists — this is greenfield.** The move of `cloister-spec` → LLO landed via #159 (bead ley-line-open-729a7e), so `schema-spec` is the canonical home for substrate IDL now.

## Scope (follow the credential-isolation/v1 pattern)
- `rs/ll-core/schema-spec/confinement/v1/README.md` — vendor-neutral spec: filesystem grants (allow-list, deny-precedence), **network mode with a DEFAULT-DENY / fail-closed rule** (`blocked` | `proxy` required; `unrestricted` is a declared opt-out, not a default), port allowlist (connect/bind/localhost), credential-source URI resolution (references credential-isolation/v1).
- `wire/` docs (shape, error-responses).
- `test-vectors/` + `VECTORS.sha256`; wire into the crate's `verify_vectors_sha256` test.
- `_capability-mapping.md` coverage entry.

## Relationship
- Sibling to `credential-isolation/v1` (the harness's *credential* half; this is its *confinement* half).
- Consumer: cloister's `cluster.capnp` confinement facet (cloister-side bead, depends on this).
- The nono `CapabilityManifest` (upstream nono crate) is the concrete kernel-plane encoding; this contract is the vendor-neutral shape it satisfies.

## Non-goals
- No libkrun/compute-substrate mechanism here (that's ADR-0044, separate).
- No cloister `cluster.capnp` changes (cloister-side bead).

title: T1.b3-followup — wire cfg::emit_cfg into cmd_parse's rayon-worker + batched inserts (dataflow-substrate/cfg-emission)
description:
Decade `dataflow-substrate`, thread `cfg-emission`. Follow-up to T1.b3 (`ley-line-open-46f7d1`) which shipped the CFG builder + F1 gate as a self-contained emission function (`leyline_ts::cfg::emit_cfg_for_source`).

## Deliverable

Wire CFG emission into `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/src/cmd_parse.rs`'s parse pipeline:

1. In `parse_file_pure` (the rayon worker), after `fold_children` returns, walk the tree to identify function-body nodes (κ `function`/`method`), look up their `node_hash` from `ast_entries`, call `cfg::build_cfg`, and collect `CfgBlock` + `CfgEdge` rows onto the `ParsedFile` struct.
2. Add `batch_table!` macro instances for `_cfg` and `_cfg_edge` (matches the existing pattern for `_ast`, `node_content`, etc.).
3. Add flush calls in the serial post-parse insert phase.
4. Add `create_cfg_tables` + `create_cfg_indexes` calls to the schema setup + deferred-index sequence.
5. When `_cfg` is populated by parse (not by `emit_cfg_for_source`), the F1 gate can be rewritten to invoke via the full pipeline (`cmd_parse` → serialize → deserialize → assert) which is stronger than the current test that goes through `emit_cfg_for_source` directly.

## Why this is a follow-up

T1.b3's PR (this scope) ships the CFG BUILDER + F1 falsifiability check. The rayon-worker plumbing + batched-insert macros + schema-setup wiring is delivery-vector, not correctness — it doubles the PR size without changing the falsifiability of the substrate thesis. Splitting keeps PRs focused per Fable's decade discipline.

## Close condition

`cargo test -p leyline-cli-lib --test f1_cfg_reflow_stable` passes end-to-end through `cmd_parse` (rewrite of the F1 test to consume from `_cfg` populated by the parse pipeline, not by direct `emit_cfg_for_source` calls). Plus: `task ci` green, no regression to existing parse tests.

## Files (predicted)

- `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/src/cmd_parse.rs` (~150 lines added: struct fields, walker call, batch macros, flush calls, schema setup)
- `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/tests/f1_cfg_reflow_stable.rs` (rewrite to go through cmd_parse)

## Priority

P2. Non-blocking for T1.b4 (cyclomatic complexity extends cfg.rs directly, doesn't require the wiring).

title: F5 — cross-cutting: HDC math gates survive the analysis-substrate pass (dataflow-substrate)
description:
Decade `dataflow-substrate`, cross-cutting. Guards against the new `_cfg` / `_dfg` / `_taint` emission pass interfering with HDC math (saturation, discriminability).

## Deliverable

Falsifiable gate that runs the existing HDC math checks (saturation, discriminability) AFTER `_cfg` / `_dfg` / `_taint` emission has run on the same fixture. Asserts the HDC math gates are byte-identical to a pre-decade baseline.

Rationale: analysis-substrate schema growth or index churn could plausibly regress HDC math via cache-eviction effects, wrong-order writes, or shared-connection contention. Pin the invariant loudly.

Files: new `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/tests/f5_no_regression_hdc.rs`.

## Close condition

F5 green throughout T1-T4 landing sequence: any thread that regresses HDC saturation or discriminability fails the F5 gate in that thread's PR CI.

title: T4.b4 — Retraction on file deletion (dd negative deltas) (dataflow-substrate/sheaf-cascade-integration)
description:
Decade `dataflow-substrate`, thread `sheaf-cascade-integration`. Blocks: T4.b2 (Update path). Same-file overlap with T4.b2 (incremental.rs) — serializes.

## Deliverable

File deleted → all its `_ast_pointer` rows go away → all its `_cfg` / `_dfg` / `_taint` rows must retract from dd's collections. dd's native retraction handles this if we correctly emit the negative deltas.

Files: extend `rs/ll-open/dataflow/src/incremental.rs`.

## F-gate: F4.b4_delete_purges_taint

Delete a file with a known planted sink; assert `_taint` rows referencing it retract to zero at the next event.

## Close condition

`cargo test -p leyline-cli-lib --test f4_b4_delete_purges_taint` — the F4.b4 gate.

title: T4.b3 — Bootstrap-vs-Update dispatcher (Soufflé elastic) (dataflow-substrate/sheaf-cascade-integration)
description:
Decade `dataflow-substrate`, thread `sheaf-cascade-integration`. Blocks: T4.b2 (incremental Update).

## Deliverable

Soufflé's elastic result: on high-impact changes, Bootstrap (full recompute) beats incremental Update. Dispatcher: if `|invalidated| / |total_regions| > θ` (start θ=0.3), fall back to Bootstrap; otherwise Update via dd delta.

Measure over N=100 real edits (from git log of mache); report the empirical crossover θ. Fine-tune the constant based on measurement, not assumption.

Files: new `rs/ll-open/dataflow/src/elastic.rs`.

## F-gate: F4.b3_elastic_never_worse

Over the measurement corpus, dispatcher-mode wall-time p99 ≤ 1.05 × min(pure-Bootstrap, pure-Update) — always as fast as the better of the two static strategies.

## Close condition

`cargo test -p leyline-dataflow --test f4_b3_elastic_never_worse` — the F4.b3 gate.

title: T4.b2 — daemon.sheaf.invalidate → dd epoch advance (edit-locality) (dataflow-substrate/sheaf-cascade-integration)
description:
Decade `dataflow-substrate`, thread `sheaf-cascade-integration`. Blocks: T4.b1 (persistent Worker). Core F4 gate for the whole decade.

## Deliverable

Wire the sheaf-invalidate event so each event:

1. Reads `invalidated: [region_id]` from payload.
2. Uses `region_labels` to map region → source_ids.
3. Reads changed `_cfg` / `_dfg` rows into dd input collections as `(+1 tuple, epoch=g)` and old ones as `(-1 tuple, epoch=g-1)` (retraction).
4. Advances the dd frontier to epoch `g`.

Files: new `rs/ll-open/dataflow/src/incremental.rs`.

## F-gate: F4_edit_locality (THE decade-level falsifiability check)

Edit one function body in a fixture, count `_taint` rows recomputed; assert ≤ 2 × |dfg edges touching changed function| + O(1). If it recomputes the whole corpus's `_taint`, the "sheaf as progress tracker" claim is refuted.

## Close condition

`cargo test -p leyline-cli-lib --test f4_edit_locality` — the F4 gate.

title: T4.b1 — Long-lived dd Worker on DaemonContext (dataflow-substrate/sheaf-cascade-integration)
description:
Decade `dataflow-substrate`, thread `sheaf-cascade-integration`. Blocks: T3.b3 (Bootstrap fixpoint). Substrate change that makes incremental *possible* — Bootstrap-mode still works, but with a persistent epoch counter that doesn't reset per call.

## Deliverable

Hold a `dataflow::Worker` on `DaemonContext` with input collections that stay attached across calls. Instead of `taint_bootstrap(conn)` reconstructing the dd computation every invocation, the Worker lives with the daemon and accepts new epochs.

Files: extend `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/src/daemon/mod.rs` (add Worker handle to DaemonContext), new `rs/ll-open/dataflow/src/worker.rs`.

## Close condition

`cargo test -p leyline-cli-lib worker_persists_across_calls` — two sequential `taint_bootstrap` calls reuse the same Worker; epoch counter monotonically increases; no re-construction cost on the second call.

title: T3.b5 — Barrier as filtered-edge pre-pass (avoid stratified negation) (dataflow-substrate/taint-fixpoint)
description:
Decade `dataflow-substrate`, thread `taint-fixpoint`. Blocks: T3.b3 (Bootstrap fixpoint).

## Deliverable

ADR-0024 open-question #4: barriers as filtered edges vs stratified negation. Push barrier filtering into the `_dfg_edge` VIEW that the projector consumes — need only positive recursion downstream.

Decides whether mache needs to depend on Mangle (Datalog-with-negation compiler) or can use plain `WITH RECURSIVE`.

Files: `rs/ll-open/dataflow/src/barrier.rs`.

## F-gate: F3.b5_no_stratified_neg

A taint rule with a sanitizer produces zero-count output when the sanitizer is on the path; positive recursion only, no negation operator used.

## Close condition

`cargo test -p leyline-dataflow --test f3_b5_no_stratified_neg` — the F3.b5 gate.

title: T3.b4 — _taint + _taint_step schema DDL + emission (dataflow-substrate/taint-fixpoint)
description:
Decade `dataflow-substrate`, thread `taint-fixpoint`. Blocks: T3.b3 (Bootstrap fixpoint). Same-file overlap with T1.b2 and T2.b4 (schema.rs) — serializes correctly.

## Deliverable

Add to `rs/ll-open/ts/src/schema.rs`:

```sql
CREATE TABLE _taint (
  source_node_hash BLOB NOT NULL,
  sink_node_hash BLOB NOT NULL,
  path_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
  config_name TEXT NOT NULL,
  path_edge_count INTEGER NOT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (path_id, config_name)
);
CREATE TABLE _taint_step (
  path_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
  step_idx INTEGER NOT NULL,
  from_node_hash BLOB NOT NULL,
  to_node_hash BLOB NOT NULL,
  edge_kind TEXT NOT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (path_id, step_idx)
);
```

Emission in `rs/ll-open/dataflow/src/emit.rs` — materializes dd output collection back to SQLite.

## Close condition

`cargo test -p leyline-dataflow taint_emit` — dd output collection round-trips to SQLite tables; path reconstruction matches source→sink chain.

title: T3.b3 — Bootstrap-mode taint via dd iterate (semi-naive fixpoint) (dataflow-substrate/taint-fixpoint)
description:
Decade `dataflow-substrate`, thread `taint-fixpoint`. Blocks: T3.b2 (config DSL), T2.b4 (_dfg schema).

## Deliverable

Semi-naive fixpoint via `differential_dataflow::operators::iterate::Iterate` over `_dfg_edge` restricted by TaintConfig predicates. Output collection: `_taint(source_node_hash, sink_node_hash, path_edge_count)`.

**Bootstrap only** — full recompute each call. Incremental Update lands in T4.

Files: `rs/ll-open/dataflow/src/bootstrap.rs`.

## F-gate: F3_taint_bootstrap_terminates

On mache/internal (Go, 207 files), with `HardcodedCredentialFlow`, the fixpoint completes in ≤ 2× the current cold-parse wall time AND produces a non-empty `_taint` set (fixture with a planted credential).

## Close condition

`cargo test -p leyline-dataflow --test f3_taint_bootstrap_terminates` — the F3 gate.

title: T3.b2 — Taint rule DSL (CodeQL-shaped four predicates) + HardcodedCredentialFlow (dataflow-substrate/taint-fixpoint)

title: leyline should honor .gitignore in collect_files (drops mache's git-archive workaround)
description:
Refiled 2026-07-10 after beads DB desync — original ID `a8e4ce` is not findable in the current DB (lost in the MCP-flap that already required the 10-bead refile pass on `docs/v0-7-1-touchups-and-beads-restore`).

## Problem

LLO's ingest walker (`rs/ll-open/cli-lib/src/cmd_parse.rs::collect_files` @ line 2469 + the duplicate walker `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/src/topology_pass.rs::collect_files` @ line 835) uses a hand-rolled `read_dir` recursion with an `is_bloat_dir` skiplist. Neither reads `.gitignore`.

Symptoms in production consumers:
- Users with `.gitignore`d `data/`, `.venv/`, model-checkpoint dirs get them indexed (blows up DB size + adds noise to smell rules).
- Mache maintains a git-archive tracked-tree workaround to feed LLO only tracked files — that workaround only exists because LLO doesn't respect gitignore.
- Rosary will inherit the same problem the moment it points at a real repo.

## Root fix

Replace the two `collect_files` recursions with `ignore::WalkBuilder` (from the `ignore` crate — same primitive ripgrep + mache use). It composes .gitignore/.ignore/.git/info/exclude/global git excludes with the existing `is_bloat_dir` skiplist as an add-on.

Escape hatch: `--respect-gitignore=false` flag (default true).

## Close condition

`cargo test -p leyline-cli-lib collect_files_respects_gitignore` — new test in cmd_parse.rs asserts a gitignored path is NOT collected. Plus `cargo test -p leyline-cli-lib -- --test-threads 1` passes end-to-end.

## Downstream

Mache drops the git-archive tracked-tree pre-pass. Rosary substrate ingest unblocks.

## Priority

P2. Non-blocking for v0.7.1 arc (already shipped).

title: Adopt mache's baselines.toml pattern for LLO F-tests

title: HDC value hypothesis — falsifiable ablation study (parallel to sheaf granularity)
description:
Refiled 2026-07-10 after beads DB desync — original ID was `ley-line-open-38e1cc`.

Does HDC add measurable value to LLO, or is it architectural bling we should rip out?

## The three testable value claims

### Claim 1 — Semantic locality beats syntactic locality

Labeled corpus of semantic queries with ground-truth relevance. Rank candidates by HDC vector distance vs syntactic distance (Levenshtein + AST subtree hash). Top-k precision at k=1, 5, 10.

**Pass**: HDC precision > baseline across ≥60% of query types, or average delta > 1.2× at k=5.

### Claim 2 — Composition is cheaper than SQL joins

Compose queries of increasing depth. Wall-time HDC-composed vs SQL-joined.

**Pass**: HDC scales flat with depth; SQL scales linearly-or-worse. Crossover N ≤ 4.

### Claim 3 — Noise / rename robustness

Rename identifier X → X'; run query that used X.

**Pass**: HDC precision degrades gracefully (< 20% loss); SQL falls off cliff.

## Corpus + labels — the actual blocker

Recommend **synthetic-first**: static analysis passes give exhaustive ground truth for callers/callees/type queries. Layer hand-labels for semantic where synthetic isn't possible.

## Three possible outcomes

- **Load-bearing** (≥ 2 of 3 claims): quantify moat
- **Marginal** (1 claim by < 1.2×): argue for simplification (HDC to sidecar only)
- **Adversarial** (baselines win all): rip out HDC crate, mark ADR-0024/0025 SUPERSEDED

## Related

- Parallel to sheaf granularity study (bead `debe39` — refiled)
- ADR-0024 (hdc-substrate-identity-rewrite) — theoretical
- ADR-0025 (hdc-compositional-validation) — theoretical
- HDC crate has own internal `sheaf.rs` (1,941 LoC) — potential audit target after value study

title: Sheaf-driven granularity dispatcher — measurement study (post ADR-0026 Phase 2)
description:
Refiled 2026-07-10 after beads DB desync — original ID was `ley-line-open-5b58ff`.

Router half shipped (PR #162, `daemon/granularity_router.rs::route_query`). This bead is the **measurement study half** — correlate sheaf-predicted granularity (PerNode / PerFile) with actual wall-time.

## Falsifiable claim

Sheaf topology (specifically δ⁰ distribution) predicts optimal storage layer better than a naive default. Under real workload:
- Sheaf-recommended granularity produces LOWER p99 read wall-time
- Beats "always PerNode" and "always PerFile" baselines

## Method

1. Wire router recommendations into ADR-0026 Phase 2 dual-read shadow mode
2. Log per-query: (a) sheaf recommendation (b) actual granularity used (c) wall-time
3. Analyze: does the sheaf recommendation correlate with wall-time win?

## Depends on

- ADR-0026 Phase 2.1 (op_query dual-read) — bead `ddfc1c` (refiled)
- Router already shipped in PR #162

## Also independent: precision reframing

The 91× ablation (PR #169) proved the LABELING SCHEME wins, not the router. This measurement study proves whether the ROUTER itself wins on wall-time — different question, different measurement path.

## Related

- ADR-0026 (`docs/adr/0026-content-addressed-pointer-store.md`)
- Post-v0.7.1 sheaf audit fixed the constants the router thresholds reference (`LOW_DELTA0_THRESHOLD` still documented heuristic per bead 5b58ff spec)

title: Producer/consumer contract test discipline — prevent caf423 class of gap
description:
Refiled 2026-07-10 after beads DB desync — original ID was `ley-line-open-e76959`.

Meta-lesson bead from `caf423` (PR #165, merged v0.7.0): mache found Python/JS extraction bugs that LLO's OWN tests missed because they verified SCHEMA shape, not the downstream SEMANTIC CONTRACT mache queries against.

## Four-layer defense

### Layer 1: Per-language non-empty gate

Table-driven test iterates `TsLanguage::supported()` — for every "supported" language, adversarial fixture must produce non-empty `node_defs` + `node_refs`. Adding a language to the enum without a fixture becomes a compile error.

### Layer 2: Downstream-consumer smoke in LLO CI

New `task ci:consumer-smoke`: parses mixed-language corpus + runs mache-shape queries + asserts semantic contract holds.

### Layer 3: Machine-executable contract tests

Extract prose invariants from `docs/audits/sheaf-invalidation-trace.md` etc. into runnable assertions. Every substrate contract gets a test.

### Layer 4: Extend `fd07d8` gate to producer layer

Any PR touching `refs.rs`, `hdc_enrich.rs`, `cmd_parse.rs` must include ≥ 1 downstream-consumer assertion (not just a schema check).

## Related

- Bead for original bug shipped in PR #165
- Bead `fd07d8` (adversarial testing sweep gate — closed v0.7.0)
- Follow-up: extend this discipline to `ts/refs.rs` extractor arms (still have `_ => Vec::new()` catchall for Elixir/HCL/Proto)

title: ADR-0029 CAS-backed workspace — Phase 1 mount driver + manifest impl
description:
Refiled 2026-07-10 after beads DB desync — original tracker was `ley-line-open-4b19f2`.

Implement Phase 1 of ADR-0029 (`docs/adr/0029-cas-backed-workspace.md`, merged v0.7.0): manifest data structure + local mount driver. Prove F1w-F5w on a single-file-scope bead type before rosary integration.

## What Phase 1 delivers

- Manifest data structure (`{path → blob_hash, mode, granularity}`), content-addressed
- Mount driver (extends mache's FUSE/NFS mount with manifest-mode)
- Copy-on-write write layer
- Bead-scope enforcement at mount layer (out-of-scope reads → ENOENT)
- Manifest → git commit translator
- **NOT in Phase 1**: sub-file (per-node) granularity, rosary dispatch, cloister capability tokens

## Depends on

- ADR-0028 Phase 1 (source_blobs shipped v0.7.0) ✓
- F-test measurement harness (bead `ddf147` — refiled below, was `5f9829`) — needs to land first

## Falsification gates (from ADR-0029 § 5)

- F1w: mount startup < 1s vs worktree 5-30s on 500MB repo
- F2w: N concurrent agents → O(1) disk
- F3w: agent literally can't read out-of-scope files (structural, not policy)
- F4w: 3-AST-subtree bead → manifest ~KB not MB (this is Phase 3 scope; Phase 1 is whole-file)
- F5w: manifest → git commit byte-identical diffs to worktree flow

## Related

- ADR-0029 (`docs/adr/0029-cas-backed-workspace.md`)
- Companion beads: ADR-0028 Phase 1 shipped in PR #153
- User's original insight: 2026-07-09 unified-CAS composition

title: ADR-0026 Phase 2 — op_query dual-read shadow + F2 wall-time gate
description:
Refiled 2026-07-10 after beads DB desync — original ID was `ley-line-open-8201de`.

Phase 2 execution plan for ADR-0026 (content-addressed pointer store). Consumer migration from row-projected `_ast` to pointer store, one op at a time, with dual-read discipline + F2 gate.

## Load-bearing choices (from ADR-0026 § 9.2, merged v0.7.0)

- **First op**: `op_query` — load-bearing consumer read path for mache + cloister
- **Discipline**: `LEYLINE_POINTER_STORE_READ={off|shadow|primary}` flag
  - shadow: execute BOTH paths, compare + log mismatches, return row-projected result
  - primary: pointer store first, fall back on error
- **F2 gate**: pointer-store p99 read time ≤ 50% of row-projected p99 before flipping to primary
- **Falsification path**: if F2 fails, either (a) per-file blob unit is wrong (switch to per-semantic-unit) OR (b) design bet falsified (kill Phase 2 per §7)
- **Rollback**: dual-write invariant (Phase 1) keeps row-projected populated through all of Phase 2; point of no return is Phase 3

## Sub-beads to file when this dispatches

- Phase 2.0: F2 read-side measurement infrastructure (blocking all subsequent) — **DONE** in PR #152 (`335d34`)
- Phase 2.1: `op_query` dual-read path + shadow-mode wire-up
- Phase 2.2: `op_query` F2 bench + primary flip if 2× gate passes
- Phase 2.3: `op_agreement` migration
- Phase 2.4: `op_reparse` migration
- Phase 2.5: LSP enrichment migration + resolve open question 5

## Related

- ADR-0026 (`docs/adr/0026-content-addressed-pointer-store.md` § 9.2)
- Bead for granularity dispatcher becomes actionable after Phase 2.1 ships (was `5b58ff` — refiled separately)

title: Doc: correct 91× ablation framing — it validates the labeling scheme, not the sheaf constants
description:
Math-friend audit (agent `a9d5e5a2`) 2026-07-10 found the ablation study (bead `2775a3`, PR #169) measures `emit_watcher_sheaf_invalidate`, which computes its region set via **label-prefix matching** — `SheafState::regions_touching_files` at `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/src/daemon/sheaf_ops.rs:216-262`. This path:

- Never calls `on_change`
- Never runs the BFS cascade
- Never evaluates a single δ⁰ threshold

Confirmed at `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/src/cmd_daemon.rs:1537-1560`: fine-grained diff or all-known fallback, then `bump_generation`, no cascade.

**The 91× precision win validates the LABELING SCHEME (2 labels/file), not the sheaf constants under audit** (cache.rs cascade, complex.rs violation thresholds, granularity_router.rs cutoffs).

## What ships

### Update `docs/research/sheaf-ablation-study.md`

- Section noting explicitly: measurement is on the label-prefix path, not the cascade/threshold path
- 91× validates: "for a labeled sheaf topology, region-precise invalidation touches ~1.1% of what naive would"
- 91× does NOT validate: any cascade depth, δ⁰ threshold, or router cutoff — those are downstream of `on_change` which the study never triggered

### Update marketing / release notes wording

If v0.7.0 release notes or CHANGELOG use the 91× number as evidence for the sheaf constants, add the qualifier: "labeling scheme validated; cascade + thresholds separately audited under [P1-P7 beads]."

### Cross-reference future work

Point at:
- Bead `5b58ff` — router measurement study (Phase-2-dependent, wall-time correlation)
- Beads `4e30d5` (P1), `4e8a8f` (P2), `4eef8d` (P3), etc. — the constants audit that DID identify concrete bugs

## What this bead does NOT deliver

- No code changes
- No re-running the study — the study measured what it measured; the reframing is honest, not corrective
- No withdrawal of the 91× number — it's still real, still load-bearing for what it actually proves

## Related

- `ley-line-open-2775a3` — the study

- All P1-P7 fix beads (4e30d5, 4e8a8f, 4eef8d, 4f3f6e, 4f9553, 4fece1, 504341) — the actual constants work

Audit `a9d5e5a2`, 2026-07-10, headline finding.
