title: L10 — agreement(token, payload_kind) op via CellComplex::detect_violations (ADR-0020 Gate 3)
description:
From `docs/problems/agent-first-semantic-surface.md` (L10). Depends on L8, L9.

## Problem

ADR-0020 falsifiability Gate 3. Implement the `agreement` daemon op per ADR-0020 §3. Computes `coherence_defect` via `CellComplex::detect_violations` against a degenerate 2-node complex (one node per source, identity restriction maps).

## Acceptance

- `BaseRequest::Agreement` variant + dispatch arm + registry entries; drift tests pass
- Fixture: insert two `code.symbol_def` observations on the same token from different sources with disagreeing fields
- Response `defects` array is non-empty; computation path went through `CellComplex::detect_violations` (verified via spy)

## Inputs

- `docs/adr/0020-entity-observation-lattice.md` §3
- `rs/ll-open/sheaf/src/cell_complex.rs::detect_violations`
- `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/src/daemon/{ops.rs, mcp.rs, wire.rs}`
- L8 + L9 beads
- `docs/problems/agent-first-semantic-surface.md` L10

## Output shape

PR; ~300 LOC.

## Scope

Just `agreement`. `neighborhood` and `co_changed_with` (ADR-0020 §3 also) are separate beads — NL6, NL7 in the decomposition doc.

## Failure mode

Spy fails (path doesn't reach `detect_violations`) or defects array doesn't reflect injected disagreement.

title: L9 — ComplexBuildPass invokes CellComplex + CoChangeTracker (ADR-0020 Gate 2)
description:
From `docs/problems/agent-first-semantic-surface.md` (L9). Depends on L8.

## Problem

ADR-0020 falsifiability Gate 2. New enrichment pass that scans recent `observation` rows, builds a `leyline_sheaf::CellComplex` from unique mention tokens + co-occurrence edges (edge weight = co-occurrence frequency × source diversity), and hands it to `CoChangeTracker::observe`. Test asserts the code path mechanically invokes `leyline-sheaf::CellComplex` — the gate exists specifically to prove the math is load-bearing, not decorative citation (per ADR-0020's own framing).

## Acceptance

- `ComplexBuildPass` registered in `enrichment.rs`
- Fixture: 5–10 observation rows referencing 3–4 distinct tokens
- Test asserts: complex constructed with expected node + edge count; `CoChangeTracker::observe` was called (verified via spy or invocation counter); internal edge weights updated
- Test FAILS if the code path doesn't actually call into `leyline-sheaf::CellComplex` (must be a real assertion — invocation spy, not "this typechecks")

## Inputs

- `docs/adr/0020-entity-observation-lattice.md` §2
- `rs/ll-open/sheaf/src/cell_complex.rs`, `co_change_tracker.rs`
- `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/src/daemon/enrichment.rs`
- L8 bead (`observation` table population)
- `docs/problems/agent-first-semantic-surface.md` L9

## Output shape

PR; ~350 LOC.

## Scope

Just pass construction + invocation. Three query ops (`neighborhood` / `agreement` / `co_changed_with`) are separate beads — L10 covers `agreement`.

## Failure mode

Spy/counter assertion fails (code path doesn't reach `CellComplex`).

title: L8 — observation table + Claude Code session enrichment pass (ADR-0020 Gate 1)
description:
From `docs/problems/agent-first-semantic-surface.md` (L8). First of three ADR-0020 falsifiability gates (1 → 2 → 3).

## Problem

ADR-0020 falsifiability Gate 1. Create the `observation` table per §1 schema:

```sql
CREATE TABLE observation (
    id            INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    source        TEXT NOT NULL,
    payload_kind  TEXT NOT NULL,
    payload_inline BLOB,
    payload_hash  BLOB,
    mentions      TEXT NOT NULL,
    observed_at   INTEGER NOT NULL
);
CREATE INDEX observation_by_kind     ON observation(payload_kind, observed_at DESC);
CREATE INDEX observation_by_mentions ON observation(mentions);
```

Implement one enrichment pass that walks Claude Code session JSONLs (the `agent-corpus` crate already provides the parser) and emits rows with `payload_kind = "agent.session_turn"` and `mentions` populated with cited paths/symbols/beads.

## Acceptance

- Schema migration creates `observation` table + indexes
- `EnrichmentPass` impl `SessionObservationPass` registered in `enrichment.rs`
- Fixture test: ingest a 5-turn fixture session; row count = 5; `mentions` arrays contain cited paths verified against fixture
- Inline-vs-hash threshold (4096 bytes) honored: small payloads in `payload_inline`, large in `BlobStore` + `payload_hash`

## Inputs

- `docs/adr/0020-entity-observation-lattice.md` §1
- `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/src/daemon/enrichment.rs` (EnrichmentPass trait)
- `rs/ll-open/agent-corpus/src/sources/claude_code.rs` (parser — shipped via `ley-line-open-79a37c`)
- `leyline-core::BlobStore` (hash-backed large payloads)
- `docs/problems/agent-first-semantic-surface.md` L8

## Output shape

PR; ~400 LOC.

## Scope

Just the table + one pass. Other observation sources (git, rosary) are follow-up beads. The three lattice query ops (`neighborhood`, `agreement`, `co_changed_with`) gate on L9 (ComplexBuildPass) first.

## Failure mode

Fixture test row-count or `mentions` mismatch.

title: L4 — implement search_symbols(pattern, limit, kind?) NDJSON streaming op (ADR-0016 §6)
description:
From `docs/problems/agent-first-semantic-surface.md` (L4).

## Problem

ADR-0016 §6. Streams results as newline-delimited JSON over MCP `tools/call` response body and over UDS. First result emitted as soon as SQL `LIMIT` yields first row (uses streaming cursor, not buffered result-set). Consumer can close the read side to cancel; server detects closed socket and aborts the query.

## Acceptance

- Variant + dispatch arm + registry entries; drift tests pass
- 10K-result search returns first 100 results within 100ms even when total takes 5s (ADR-0016 §6 falsifiability — instrument `time_to_first_result_ms` and `time_to_completion_ms` separately; gate the former)
- Cancel test: closing the read side mid-stream aborts the query (server-side log + query-not-finished assertion)

## Inputs

- `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/src/daemon/{ops.rs, mcp.rs, wire.rs}`
- `docs/adr/0016-ai-native-query-surface.md` §6
- rusqlite `Statement::query` (streaming, not `query_map` which collects)
- `docs/problems/agent-first-semantic-surface.md` L4

## Output shape

PR; ~250 LOC (streaming cursor + NDJSON framer + cancel handler).

## Scope

Just `search_symbols`. Pagination via cursor (ADR-0016 §6 alternative b) is a future bead if a consumer needs resume-after-disconnect.

## Failure mode

First-result latency > 100ms or close-cancel doesn't abort.

title: L3 — implement inspect_neighborhood(id, depth, edge_kinds, max_bytes) daemon op (ADR-0016 §5)
description:
From `docs/problems/agent-first-semantic-surface.md` (L3). Depends on L1 (`ley-line-open-c2c4d9`).

## Problem

ADR-0016 §5. Returns focal symbol's bundle plus the N-hop neighborhood. Bounded by `depth` (default 1, max 4), `max_bytes` (default 64KB, max 1MB), per-distance truncation (distant neighbors get `symbol_id` + `hover_typed.signature` only, not full callers/callees).

## Acceptance

- Variant + dispatch arm + registry entries; drift tests pass
- Fixture test: depth-2 query on a known graph returns expected neighbor count + correct truncation at hop-2
- Wire-counter assertion: `writes_for_neighborhood_query == 1` (ADR-0016 §5 falsifiability — single round-trip)
- Byte-cap test: max_bytes=4096 returns truncated response with `truncated: true`

## Inputs

- Same as L1 + `_lsp_refs` / `_lsp_defs` for edge expansion
- `docs/adr/0016-ai-native-query-surface.md` §5
- `docs/problems/agent-first-semantic-surface.md` L3

## Output shape

PR; ~400 LOC (graph walk + truncation).

## Scope

Bounded single-response shape only. Bidirectional streaming (ADR-0016 §5 alternative c) is out-of-scope.

## Failure mode

Drift test fails, wire-counter > 1, or byte-cap not enforced.

comments.text:
Shipping in this PR. `inspect_neighborhood(symbol_id, depth, max_neighbors_per_hop)` returns focal symbol + BFS neighborhood in one round-trip.

## Shape

```
inspect_neighborhood(symbol_id, depth=1, max_neighbors_per_hop=20) -> {
  ok, symbol_id, depth,
  focal: { symbol_id, kind, definitions: [...], provenance, certainty },
  neighbors: [
    { symbol_id, kind, hop, definitions: [...], provenance, certainty },
    ...
  ],
  provenance, certainty
}
```

Neighbors are reachable via callers + callees relation. BFS frontier visits each symbol at the FIRST hop reached (deduped via `visited` HashSet).

## v1 simplifications (documented inline)

- **No `max_bytes` byte-cap.** ADR-0016 §5 spec'd one; v1 bounds via per-hop count + depth ceiling instead. Byte-cap is a follow-up if response-size budgeting becomes load-bearing.
- **No `edge_kinds` filter.** Always traverses both callers AND callees. Filtering one direction or specific edge types is a follow-up bead.
- **Single round-trip** (ADR-0016 §5 falsifiability gate `writes_for_neighborhood_query == 1`): the entire neighborhood is built inside one `with_live_db` closure and serialized once. Pinned implicitly by the handler architecture (no nested daemon calls).
- **Depth ceiling 4** per ADR-0016 §5; clamped at handler entry.
- **L7 provenance/certainty** carried through on every neighbor row.

## Tests (3 new)

- `inspect_neighborhood_empty_db_returns_empty_neighbors`
- `inspect_neighborhood_depth_1_returns_callers_and_callees` — A calls B, C calls A → neighbors = {B, C}
- `inspect_neighborhood_depth_is_capped_at_max` — depth=999 → response says depth=4

All drift tests pass; `task ci` clean.
comments.author: rsry-mcp

title: Add license = "AGPL-3.0-or-later" to leyline-core + leyline-cas-ffi (cloister cargo-deny block)
description:
## Problem

Cloister's `task verify` runs `cargo deny check` over the full Rust closure including LLO crates pulled in via the wasm32 BLAKE3 FFI (cloister-713b4e). Two LLO crates lack `license` fields, so cargo-deny's `licenses` check fails:

```
error[unlicensed]: leyline-core = 0.4.5 is unlicensed
error[unlicensed]: leyline-cas-ffi = 0.4.5 is unlicensed
```

LLO is AGPL-3.0-or-later per the root LICENSE; `leyline-text-search/Cargo.toml` already declares this. Other LLO crates inherited the unlicensed default — fine for in-repo use, breaks downstream cargo-deny.

## Fix

Add `license = "AGPL-3.0-or-later"` to:
- `rs/ll-core/core/Cargo.toml` (leyline-core)
- `rs/ll-open/cas-ffi/Cargo.toml` (leyline-cas-ffi)

Both crates `cargo check` clean after the addition.

## Coordinated with

- Cloister bead: `cloister-eefd45` (the cargo-deny failure surface)
- Cloister consumer: `cloister-713b4e` (CAS FFI consumption — closed)
- `leyline-text-search/Cargo.toml` (already declares this license — pattern)

## Followup

After this lands + LLO ships a new commit, cloister bumps the git-rev pin in `rs/crates/cas/Cargo.toml:21` to point at the new SHA. Tracked under `cloister-eefd45`.

## Acceptance

- Both manifests declare `license = "AGPL-3.0-or-later"`
- `cargo check` clean
- Downstream cargo-deny stops emitting `unlicensed` errors for these crates

title: witchcraft git pin: advance to upstream master + revalidate candle API
description:
From dep audit 2026-06-17. `rs/ll-open/text-search/Cargo.toml` pins witchcraft via git with no explicit rev — `cargo update -p witchcraft` will advance freely. Current lockfile pins `02788a46`; upstream `dropbox/witchcraft` master is at `b7a2a3ae` (192 commits ahead).

## Acceptance gates

- `cargo update -p witchcraft --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml`
- `cargo test -p leyline-text-search --features engine-witchcraft --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml`
- candle API drift smoke check: the `WitchcraftEngine` impl uses candle-via-witchcraft; if witchcraft's master bumped candle, our consumer code may need adjustments.
- `task ci` green (Witchcraft only activates under the `engine-witchcraft` feature flag, so default CI doesn't exercise it — but the optional-feature build matrix does).

## Scope

- `rs/Cargo.lock` — `cargo update -p witchcraft`
- `rs/ll-open/text-search/src/*.rs` — only if the candle API changed

## Risk

Unknown. Witchcraft is an upstream project we don't control. The 192-commit drift could be benign (refactors only) or could include candle pin advances that affect us. Validation IS the deliverable.

title: leyline-sign X.509/CMS crypto stack bump (der/spki/const-oid/cms/x509-cert)
description:
From dep audit 2026-06-17. The X.509/CMS crypto stack in `leyline-sign` moves as a unit. Current vs latest:

| Crate | Current | Latest |
|---|---|---|
| `der` | 0.7 | 0.8.0 |
| `spki` | 0.7 | 0.8.0 |
| `const-oid` | 0.9 | 0.10.2 |
| `cms` | 0.2 | **0.3.0-pre.2** (pre-release) |
| `x509-cert` | 0.2 | **0.3.0-rc.4** (release candidate) |

`cms 0.3` and `x509-cert 0.3` haven't reached stable releases yet (only pre-release / RC tags). Bumping just der/spki/const-oid would break cms 0.2 + x509-cert 0.2 which depend on der/spki 0.7. So the bump can't be partial — it has to wait for cms 0.3 / x509-cert 0.3 stable.

## Acceptance gates

- `cms` and `x509-cert` have stable 0.3 releases on crates.io.
- Bump all five crates in one PR.
- `cargo test -p leyline-sign --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml` passes.
- Cross-runtime PKCS#7 fixture (if any) round-trips.
- `task ci` green.

## Scope

- `rs/ll-open/sign/Cargo.toml` — five-line version bumps
- `rs/ll-open/sign/src/*.rs` — likely some API-shape changes (cms/x509-cert 0.2 → 0.3 may rename or restructure types)

## Trigger to start

`cargo search cms` returns a stable 0.3.x (not pre.X) AND `cargo search x509-cert` returns a stable 0.3.x (not rc.X). Until then, blocked on upstream.

title: fuser 0.15 → 0.17 in leyline-fs (Linux-only validation)
description:
From dep audit 2026-06-17. `fuser` is 2 minor releases behind. Bumping to 0.17 doesn't surface any obvious source breaks but local macOS validation fails for an environment reason:

- The system's `osxfuse.pc` reports v1.2.1 (provided by `fuse-t` on this machine).
- `fuser 0.15` and `fuser 0.17` both require osxfuse ≥ 2.6.0.
- GitHub Actions `task ci` is the actual validation surface — Linux libfuse provides the required version.

## Acceptance gates

- `task ci` green on the dep-bump PR (GitHub Actions Linux).
- No source changes required (verify; if any, scope in this bead).
- `leyline-fs` lib + integration tests pass.

## Scope

- `rs/ll-open/fs/Cargo.toml` — single-line version bump
- `rs/ll-open/fs/src/*.rs` — only if fuser 0.17 changed an API. Likely zero source touch.

## Not in scope

- Fixing local macOS fuser support — that's a separate dev-env issue, not a workspace-substrate issue.

title: jj-lib 0.38 → 0.42 in leyline-vcs (async-API migration)
description:
From dep audit 2026-06-17. `cargo check -p leyline-vcs` with jj-lib 0.42 surfaces 6 async-related breaking changes:

- `Workspace::load_at_head` now returns `impl Future<Output = Result<(Workspace, Arc<ReadonlyRepo>), ...>>`. Callers must `.await` before `.map_err()`.
- `Workspace::load_at` same shape — async.
- `Repo::commit_loader().load_at`, working-copy read methods, transaction commit — all moved to async.
- `tokio::io::AsyncReadExt` import needed; `read` on `Box<dyn AsyncRead + Send>` now requires the trait in scope.

## Acceptance gates

- `cargo check --workspace --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml` passes
- `cargo test -p leyline-vcs --manifest-path rs/Cargo.toml` passes
- `task ci` green
- No `.unwrap()` on `Future`s; all error-mapping flows through `.await.map_err(...)`

## Scope

- `rs/ll-open/vcs/Cargo.toml` — bump version
- `rs/ll-open/vcs/src/*.rs` — async migration; likely concentrated in the load + working-copy paths
- `rs/ll-open/vcs/tests/*.rs` — may need #[tokio::test] / pollster::block_on adjustments

## Not in scope

- Bumping `pollster` to a newer major (currently 0.4).
- Any consumer of leyline-vcs (the daemon doesn't currently use vcs paths that touch the changed APIs, but verify).

title: ADR-0014 §3: update capnp pin language (=0.20.0 → =0.25.0) + Go alpha-pin section
description:
Per coupling audit F4 (`docs/audits/2026-06-17-coupling-audit.md`).

## Problem

ADR-0014 §3 ("Per-language generators / Per-language runtimes") declares an exact pin:

> Rust: `capnpc = "=0.20.0"` exact (currently `"0.20"` semver-range — TIGHTEN). Go (mache side): `capnp.org/go/capnp/v3/capnpc-go@v3.0.X` exact tag, NOT `@latest`. Per-language runtimes. Rust: `capnp = "=0.20.0"` exact. Go: `require capnproto.org/go/capnp/v3 v3.0.X` exact.

Actual workspace pin (2026-06-17):

| Crate | `capnp` | `capnpc` | `capnp-json` |
|---|---|---|---|
| `rs/ll-core/schema-capnp` | =0.25.0 | =0.25.0 | — |
| `rs/ll-core/public-schema` | =0.25.0 | =0.25.0 | =0.1.0 |
| `rs/ll-open/cli-lib` | =0.25.0 | (via deps) | =0.1.0 |
| `rs/ll-open/lsp` | =0.25.0 | (via deps) | — |

Rust intra-workspace is *consistent at 0.25.0*. The ADR doc is just stale by ~5 releases.

## Go side question

`clients/go/leyline-schema/go.mod` and `mache`'s go.mod both pull `capnproto.org/go/capnp/v3 v3.1.0-alpha.2` — an **alpha** version. The cross-runtime fixture tests (T8.10 + cache.capnp extension in PR #53) pass against this alpha, but alpha-pin violates the deterministic-cross-runtime goal the ADR sets.

Two paths:
1. Bump Go to the stable `v3.0.x` line if one exists that matches 0.25.0 Rust (probably none — Go capnpgen hasn't released stable since v3.0.0).
2. Document the alpha as the deliberate cross-runtime pin and explain *why* it's load-bearing.

## Scope of fix

Two file changes:

1. `docs/adr/0014-capnp-as-protocol.md` §3 — update version strings throughout (s/=0.20.0/=0.25.0/g where it's a pin language, not historical record). Add a paragraph documenting the Go-side alpha pin decision.
2. (Optional) `docs/adr/0014-capnp-as-protocol.md` add a "history" footnote noting the 0.20→0.25 bump landed in PR #33 (rusqlite-era dep audit).

## Out of scope

- The deferred 0.25.4 triplet bump (per handoff bead `ley-line-open-5f92fa`). This bead is *language-only*; the actual triplet revision lives in the deferred work.
- Any actual `Cargo.toml` change. The pins are correct; only the doc is wrong.

## Acceptance

- Diff is doc-only (no Cargo.toml or go.mod changes).
- ADR §3 quotes match the actual pin in code.
- Go alpha decision is explicit in the ADR (deliberate or "revisit at 0.25.4 triplet").

title: CacheLockfile schema as substrate primitive: ship cache.capnp + Go/TS bindings (ADR-0021)
description:
## What

Ship `rs/ll-core/public-schema/capnp/cache.capnp` alongside `daemon.capnp` — the canonical lockfile schema for any substrate consumer (mache, me-bundle, agent-corpus, future tools) that needs content-addressed manifests over the LLO BlobStore.

ADR-0021 (`docs/adr/0021-cache-lockfile-schema.md`) lays out the design: capnp source, TOML on-disk via cloister's bidi pipeline (cloister ADR-0025), OCI JSON wire form.

## User correction (2026-05-21)

Originally mache landed the schema in its own repo (mache ADR-0020). User caught the architectural mistake: "LLO already has cache right? Wouldn't it make sense for it to live there? If I want it repeatable?"

Same correction shape as ring-io (impl moved from cloister to LLO, cloister-c2a7c3 → ley-line-open-cb3453): **substrate stuff lives in the substrate.** Consumers (mache, me-bundle, agent-corpus) require the substrate's primitives; the substrate provides one schema, all consumers compose.

## Scope

1. **`rs/ll-core/public-schema/capnp/cache.capnp`** — the schema (CacheLockfile, Meta, ProcessorVersion, SourceEntry, TopologyEdge, Hash).
2. **`common.capnp` Hash type** — if it doesn't exist yet, add it (verify against `rs/ll-core/public-schema/capnp/` before authoring). Hash is reused across daemon, cache, and likely future schemas.
3. **Capnp triplet bump** per ADR-0014 §3 — bump schema-capnp + public-schema + downstream pins as one atomic move.
4. **Go bindings** via `clients/go/leyline-schema/cache/` (mirrors how daemon bindings ship).
5. **TS bindings** via cloister's schema-bridge codegen (ADR-0022 / ADR-0025 pipeline).
6. **Round-trip tests** — TOML→capnp→TOML and capnp→OCI-JSON→capnp test gates.

## What this bead does NOT do

- The actual consumer code (`mache push` / `me-bundle pack` / etc.) — those are per-consumer beads.
- The `build-cache/v1` capability spec — that's cloister-side, filed when Phase 3 of mache-aeb262 hits.
- Cryptographic signing — consumer concern (mache may not sign; me-bundle MUST via signet).

## Dependencies

- Substrate: ley-line-open-783d72 (BlobStore trait), ley-line-open-bb0316 (FsBlobStore — chunk addressing).
- Bidi pipeline: cloister ADR-0025 (already shipped).
- Schema codegen: cloister ADR-0022 (already shipped).

## Consumers (downstream, separate beads)

- mache-aeb262 — portable mache db
- ley-line-open-dffb77 — me-bundle (consumes for the manifest-over-chunks shape)
- ley-line-open-79a37c — agent-corpus (potential future consumer for observation chunks)

## Files

- `rs/ll-core/public-schema/capnp/cache.capnp` (new)
- `rs/ll-core/public-schema/capnp/common.capnp` (audit/extend — add Hash if missing)
- `clients/go/leyline-schema/cache/` (new package)
- `tests/cache_roundtrip.rs` (new) — TOML↔capnp; capnp↔OCI-JSON round trips
- `docs/adr/0021-cache-lockfile-schema.md` (landed)

comments.text:
## 2026-05-22 — Implementation pass on feat/cache-schema-ae89aa

Schema landed end-to-end with comprehensive round-trip coverage on both runtimes. Two ADR-0021 corrections caught during impl (now reflected in the ADR):

### Architectural corrections vs ADR-0021's initial draft

1. **Location**: cache.capnp moved from `rs/ll-core/public-schema/capnp/` (initial ADR placement) to `rs/ll-core/schema-capnp/schemas/`. Reason: schema-capnp/schemas/ is the *structural substrate* directory (common, ast, binding, head, source); public-schema/capnp/ is for *protocol* schemas (daemon RPC). Cache is data-shape, not RPC — belongs with the structural substrate. Bonus: lets cache.capnp `using Common = import "common.capnp";` cleanly via the existing -I path.

2. **Hash shape**: `common.Hash` already exists (`{ bytes :Data }`, 32-byte BLAKE3 by substrate decision per Σ §3.4). Initial ADR proposed `Hash { algo, bytes }` to future-proof for SHA-256/512 — that fights the substrate's intentional BLAKE3-lock. cache.capnp now imports `common.Hash` directly; no algo field.

### What landed

- **`rs/ll-core/schema-capnp/schemas/cache.capnp`** (~80 lines, fileId `0xca7eca7eca7eca7e`):
  - `CacheLockfile { meta, sources, topology, root }`
  - `Meta { producer, producerVersion, schemaVersion, inputProcessors, generatedAtMs }`
  - `ProcessorVersion { kind, version }`
  - `SourceEntry { path, inputHash, chunkHash, kind }`
  - `TopologyEdge { from, toSource }`
  - All hashes are `Common.Hash` imports
  - Comments document ordinal discipline (ADR-0014 §3), Σ §3.4 BLAKE3 lock, consumer-defined semantics for kind/path/topology

- **`rs/ll-core/schema-capnp/src/lib.rs`**: `pub mod cache_capnp` declaration + 6 new Rust round-trip tests pinning every struct (SourceEntry, Meta with nested processors list, full CacheLockfile assembly, empty lockfile edge case, ProcessorVersion, TopologyEdge). All 8 lib tests pass (2 existing common tests + 6 new cache tests).

- **`rs/ll-core/schema-capnp/tests/fileid_allowlist.rs`**: cache.capnp added to `SCHEMA_FILEID_ALLOWLIST` with ADR reference + mnemonic-fileId rationale (`0xca7eca7eca7eca7e` is leetspeak `cafecafecafecafe` — visually obvious drift detection per ADR-0014 §2). Allowlist gate passes.

- **`clients/go/leyline-schema/regen.sh`**: cache entry added to SCHEMAS list, regen runs clean, `go build ./...` passes (cross-schema imports resolve).

- **`clients/go/leyline-schema/cache/cache.capnp.go`** (generated, 4 pointer struct + 5 nested types via capnpc-go).

- **`clients/go/leyline-schema/cache/cache_test.go`** (~280 lines): 4 Go round-trip tests mirroring the Rust ones — SourceEntry, Meta with processors, full lockfile assembly, empty lockfile edge case. All pass with `GOWORK=off go test ./cache/`.

### Open work (this bead still tracks)

- Cross-runtime fixture: drop a canonical CacheLockfile capnp segment file into `rs/ll-core/schema-capnp/tests/fixtures/` so both Rust and Go decode it byte-equal (extends T8.10 cross-runtime fixture pattern that exists for binding.capnp).
- TOML↔capnp round-trip (gated on cloister ADR-0025 bidi pipeline being available cross-repo; today the test would live as a fixture in cloister's schema-bridge or in the mache consumer crate).
- OCI JSON wire form is fully specced in ADR-0021 but the wire-side fixture is consumer-side (mache push test).

### Verification commands run (per /evolve protocol)

- `cargo test -p leyline-schema-capnp` → 8 lib tests + 4 cross-runtime fixtures + 1 fileid_allowlist gate = 13 passed
- `bash clients/go/leyline-schema/regen.sh` → all 7 schemas regen + `go build ./...` clean
- `GOWORK=off go test ./cache/` → 4 Go tests pass
- `task test` (full workspace) → running in background

### Branch

`feat/cache-schema-ae89aa` (uncommitted). Ready for human review of the architectural corrections (cache.capnp location + Hash shape) before commit.

title: Ship GETTING-STARTED.md + audit homebrew-tap formula (leyline.rb vs ley-line-open)
description:
## What

ley-line-open has no `GETTING-STARTED.md`. Add one matching the rosary/mache/cloister convention (root, hyphen-named). Constraint from user (2026-05-21): "zero local deps needed ideally" — install path should be one `brew install` and nothing else.

Open question that goes with this: `~/remotes/art/homebrew-tap/Formula/leyline.rb` exists but the public repo is `ley-line-open`. Investigate whether `leyline.rb` corresponds to the PRIVATE `ley-line` repo or to LLO. If private: file a new `ley-line-open.rb` formula in homebrew-tap. If LLO: rename the formula to `ley-line-open.rb` (or add an alias) to match the repo and binary name.

## Phase 1 (this bead)

1. Audit `homebrew-tap/Formula/leyline.rb` — what binary does it install? Which repo's releases does it pull from? Update README / formula name to match reality.
2. Author `GETTING-STARTED.md` at LLO root. Skeleton:
   - **Use**: `brew tap agentic-research/tap && brew install <correct-formula-name>`. Then `leyline --help` or `ll --help` (confirm binary name from formula).
   - **Develop**: only here mention rustup + capnp + task. Behind a "Working on LLO" header so first-timers skip it.
   - **What LLO does**: 5 lines linking to the README's deeper sections.
3. README quick-start block — same 3-line shape as rosary-d01972 proposes for rosary.

## Zero-deps audit

LLO's runtime deps (from `Cargo.toml` + the daemon code): SQLite (linked statically via rusqlite), FUSE (fuse-t on macOS — runtime system dep). Document whether `brew install ley-line-open` brings fuse-t in via `depends_on` or if the user needs a separate step. Goal: one command, no fallout.

## Pairs with

- rosary-d01972 — the parent setup-story bead (shape this one mirrors)
- ley-line-open-dffb77 — me-repo bundle bead — depends on LLO being installable cleanly; "zero deps for users of the me-bundle" is an L5-consumer constraint

## Out of scope

- Public/private decision on homebrew-tap (separate bead if filed)
- Cross-platform install (Linux for now is `cargo install` from source; macOS is the priority)
- Formula coverage for crumb / signet (out of scope per user 2026-05-21)

## Files

- `GETTING-STARTED.md` (new, root)
- `README.md` (quick-start block)
- `../homebrew-tap/Formula/leyline.rb` (rename or audit)
- `../homebrew-tap/README.md` (formula-name reference if changed)

title: ring-io/v1 reference impl: host drain loop + descriptor types in rs/ll-open/ring/ (LLO ships the substrate; cloister consumes as CSI-shape capability)
description:
## Framing (user, 2026-05-21)

> "LLO prob handle the uring stuff? Not cloister. Cloister consumes it like a CSI?"

Apply the cred-iso/v1 pattern: cloister defines the capability interface (`cloister-spec/ring-io/v1/`, see cloister-baac45 reframed); LLO ships the reference implementation. cloister consumes the impl when hosting units, the same way it consumes the vault-DO impl for cred-iso/v1.

## Why this lives in LLO

- LLO already owns the substrate I/O surface (arena, BlobStore, ContentAddressed trait — see ley-line-open-082202).
- The "schema/scope check" in §3.1 of the Ring Seam spec — host validating writes against the content-addressed store — is *literally* an LLO BlobStore operation.
- Cross-arena (cluster-spanning) units consume the same impl regardless of which cloister bundle hosts them. Substrate logic ≠ composition logic.

## Scope (for this bead)

1. **Crate** at `rs/ll-open/ring/` — `Cargo.toml`, public API in `lib.rs`.
2. **Descriptor types** — capnp schema for `Submission { op, handle, buf_ptr, buf_len, offset, user_data, flags }` and `Completion { user_data, res, flags }`. Lives under `rs/ll-core/public-schema/capnp/ring.capnp` so it's reusable like daemon.capnp (cloister can `use ring/v1` from Go bindings the same way it uses leyline-schema/daemon).
3. **Host drain loop** — async Rust task: read submission → capability check → schema/scope check (for writes) against the BlobStore substrate → dispatch to op handler → post completion.
4. **Op dispatch table** — v1 surface: `read`, `write`, `open-at`, `readdir`, `stat`. Each op handler is a small Rust function with a uniform signature; substrate ops go through the BlobStore trait so any LLO-resident store can back the ring.
5. **Test harness** — Rust test that pretends to be a WASI guest (Vec-as-SQ, Vec-as-CQ), validates capability bounds, POSIX `res` codes for denied ops.

## What this bead does NOT do

- Doesn't wire the crate into workerd / wasmtime. That's the cloister-side adapter (separate bead, filed later in cloister).
- Doesn't implement zero-copy via `MemoryCreator`. Defer to v1.1.
- Doesn't define the Unit Format manifest (which handles get dispensed at instantiation) — that's a cloister responsibility (cloister-c26528, capability matchmaker layer per ADR-0027).
- Doesn't define the capability interface spec. That's the cloister-side bead (cloister-baac45 reframed).

## Files

- `rs/ll-open/ring/Cargo.toml`
- `rs/ll-open/ring/src/lib.rs`
- `rs/ll-open/ring/src/descriptor.rs`
- `rs/ll-open/ring/src/drain.rs`
- `rs/ll-open/ring/src/ops/` — per-op handlers
- `rs/ll-open/ring/tests/host_integration.rs`
- `rs/ll-core/public-schema/capnp/ring.capnp`

## Dependencies

- Depends on `cloister-baac45` (capability interface declaration — being reframed to `cloister-spec/ring-io/v1/`)
- Adjacent to ley-line-open-783d72 (BlobStore crate + SqliteBlobStore default) — the ring's writes flow through BlobStore; if that bead's trait shape changes, ring's op handlers track it
- Adjacent to ley-line-open-dffb77 (me-repo) — me-bundle as a live arena reads/writes through this ring

title: Versioned wire-format handshake on connect (replace empirical drift detection)
description:
## Surfaced by

Mache PR #384 + PR #388 audits (2026-05-18). The campaign hit version-drift seams multiple times:

1. **v0.4.2 daemon silently dropped sheaf.invalidate events** (`ley-line-open-5caa59`). Mache had no way to detect this from the client side — the subscribe op succeeded, just no events ever arrived. Diagnosed by writing `tools/sheaf-subscribe-probe/main.go` and waiting for a timeout.

2. **Auto-download URL drift** (`mache-9051f0`). Mache's `downloadLeyline` fetched `/releases/latest/` against a private repo. PR #388 pinned to `v0.4.1` mirroring `leyline-schema v0.4.1` in `go.mod`. But `leyline-schema` versions and daemon-binary versions are separate streams — the schema-client at `v0.4.2` and daemon binary at `v0.4.3` can drift apart silently.

3. **`parseUint64` silent coercion** (`mache-5159a2`). Mache parses generation field from the daemon's JSON wire. If LLO ever emits a different numeric encoding (hex string, capnp-quoted int, raw uint64 vs string), mache silently coerces to 0 — the generation reads as "fresh daemon, never advanced" forever.

The common pattern: **the client cannot tell at connect/subscribe time whether it's talking to a wire-format-compatible daemon.** All three issues above would have been a single loud error if a handshake existed.

## What to ship

A `leyline_version` (or `hello` / `handshake`) op that:

- Accepts the client's reported version (mache passes its `leylineBinaryVersion` const, or its embedded schema-client version)
- Returns the daemon's binary version + the capnp schema version + the JSON wire-format major
- Includes a `compat` field: `{compatible: bool, since: "v0.4.1", note: "..."}`
- Clients call this immediately after `DialSocket`, BEFORE any `SendOp` or `Subscribe`
- Mismatch = clear error: "mache built against schema v0.4.1; daemon is v0.4.3 — upgrade mache or downgrade daemon; see <docs URL>"

Could be a Subscribe-time check too (every Subscribe op replies with the wire-version) so subscribers don't get stale-shape events.

## Why this beats the alternatives

- **Beats "guess and pray"**: today's `parseUint64` returns 0 on malformed input; consumers see a value but not the version skew.
- **Beats "pin everywhere"**: PR #388 pins one URL to one version. The pin will rot the next time LLO releases. A handshake lets the daemon advertise compatibility live; clients don't need hardcoded pins.
- **Beats "log and hope"**: a log line doesn't stop processing. A handshake-mismatch error fails fast.

## Paired with

- `ley-line-open-503971` — typed JSON payload structs. The handshake's `wire-format major` is exactly the right gate for those types: "schema-client v0.4.3 requires daemon wire-format major X."
- `mache-8kif` — mache-side startup leyline-version check. Already filed on mache; the handshake's daemon-side response is what mache-8kif consumes.

## Fix shape

- Daemon side: `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/src/daemon/socket.rs` — add `op: "leyline_version"` handler that returns:
  ```json
  {
    "ok": true,
    "binary_version": "0.4.3",
    "schema_version": "0.4.3",
    "wire_format_major": 1,
    "compat_min": "0.4.1",
    "build_date": "2026-05-18"
  }
  ```
- Client side (mache, future): `internal/leyline/socket.go::DiscoverOrStart` or `DialSocket` calls `leyline_version` and either logs (warning) or errors (incompatible) on mismatch.
- Wire-format major bumps EVERY time the JSON shape on the wire changes incompatibly. Capnp schema version bumps independently for capnp-only consumers.

## Test plan (LLO side)

`rs/ll-open/cli-lib/tests/version_handshake_blackbox_test.rs`:

1. `version_op_returns_known_shape` — call `leyline_version`, assert all fields present + types correct.
2. `version_op_is_idempotent` — call twice, get identical responses.
3. `version_op_works_before_subscribe` — call before any other op, no daemon initialization required.

## References

- `ley-line-open-5caa59` (closed) — the bug that motivated this; would have been caught by a handshake
- `ley-line-open-503971` — typed payloads (mache uses these once published); paired with this
- `mache-8kif` — mache-side consumer
- `mache-9051f0` (closed) — version-pinned URL that this handshake makes redundant
- `mache-5159a2` — parseUint64 silent coercion; handshake catches wire-format drift before it hits parseUint64

## Out of scope

- Cross-version migration (auto-upgrade daemon binary) — separate problem, separate bead.
- Capnp RPC interface — `ley-line-open-a4e860` covers the JSON → capnp migration arc; this handshake works equally well in either framing.

title: Daemon-side topic filtering on Subscribe (currently emits ALL events to every subscriber)
description:
## Surfaced by

Mache PR #384 + the evolve-coverage-trunk campaign (2026-05-18). Observed empirically via `tools/sheaf-subscribe-probe/main.go`:

> "the daemon's Subscribe op publishes ALL events, regardless of which topics the client passed. (Confirmed empirically: a `daemon.files.changed` from the git-watcher poll comes through even when we subscribed to sheaf.invalidate only.)"

Mache's `internal/leyline/sheaf_subscriber.go::dispatch` confirms this with a client-side filter:

```go
topic, _ := ev["topic"].(string)
if topic != "sheaf.invalidate" {
    // Only sheaf.invalidate is subscribed; silently ignore the
    // impossible case rather than emit log noise per event.
    return
}
```

Comment says "impossible case" but in fact the daemon DOES push other-topic events; the filter is load-bearing.

## What's wrong with the current behavior

1. **Wasted UDS bandwidth.** Every subscriber on the bus receives every published event, even those it doesn't care about. With `daemon.snapshot` firing every ~5s during normal operation, a subscriber interested only in `sheaf.invalidate` pays the JSON serialize + UDS write + JSON deserialize + topic-check cost on every irrelevant event.

2. **Every consumer reimplements the filter.** Mache filters in `dispatch`. Future consumers (cloister, third-party agents) will all do the same. One filter belongs upstream, not N filters scattered downstream.

3. **Subscribe op contract is misleading.** The op accepts `topics []string` — a typed surface that promises filtering. Today that promise is broken; the topics array is effectively documentation-only.

4. **Glob-pattern semantics implied.** `internal/leyline/socket.go::Subscribe` docs:
   > Topics use dot-separated hierarchical matching:
   >   - "daemon.snapshot" — exact match
   >   - "daemon.*" — one segment wildcard
   >   - "daemon.**" — any segments wildcard
   
   The wildcard semantics suggest a topic-pattern matcher was intended at the daemon. The implementation never landed.

## Fix shape

Daemon-side: in `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/src/daemon/socket.rs` (the `handle_subscribe` path that PR #390 of LLO recently fixed for `ley-line-open-5caa59`), record the per-subscriber topic list, and BEFORE publishing each event to that subscriber's `mpsc::Sender`, match the event's topic against the subscriber's list. Drop non-matching events at the publish site, not on the wire.

Glob semantics:
- `topic` exact match
- `topic.*` matches `topic.foo` but not `topic.foo.bar`
- `topic.**` matches `topic.foo`, `topic.foo.bar`, etc.
- empty list = subscribe to everything (current effective behavior; preserve as a fallback for debugging — e.g. the probe's `**` use case)

## Regression guards

Tests in `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/tests/event_push_blackbox_test.rs` (the file PR #390 added for ley-line-open-5caa59):

1. `subscribe_exact_topic_filters_other_topics` — subscribe to `["sheaf.invalidate"]`, emit `daemon.snapshot` and `sheaf.invalidate`, assert subscriber sees ONLY the latter.
2. `subscribe_glob_one_segment` — `["daemon.*"]` matches `daemon.snapshot` but not `daemon.files.changed.deep` (or similar — depending on `*` semantics).
3. `subscribe_glob_recursive` — `["daemon.**"]` matches everything under `daemon.*`.
4. `subscribe_empty_topics_receives_all` — preserve the current "no filter" behavior for the probe / debugging.

## Mache-side impact

Once the daemon filter lands, mache can:
- Drop the `if topic != "sheaf.invalidate"` filter in `dispatch` (becomes truly impossible from the daemon side).
- Stop subscribing to `**` in `tools/sheaf-subscribe-probe/main.go` (no longer needs the workaround).
- Reduce subscriber CPU / GC by ~Nx where N is the daemon's other-topic emit rate.

Paired mache bead: filed separately after this lands. Title sketch: `chore(leyline): drop client-side topic filter once daemon implements topic-aware Subscribe`.

## References

- `tools/sheaf-subscribe-probe/main.go` (mache) — the observable
- `internal/leyline/sheaf_subscriber.go::dispatch` (mache) — the current client-side filter
- `internal/leyline/socket.go::Subscribe` (mache) — the docstring promising glob semantics
- `ley-line-open-5caa59` (LLO) — the recent v0.4.3 fix that re-established event delivery; this bead is the natural next step
- `ley-line-open-503971` (LLO) — paired typed payload structs; both improve subscriber ergonomics

title: chat-embed: refactor to use agent-corpus parsers + lib/bin split
description:
## Why

Today `rs/ll-open/chat-embed/main.rs` is 574 LOC, single file, single binary. It does Claude Code session indexing via MiniLM but:

- Its parser is ~30 LOC of `derive_embed_text` — structurally weaker than what `agent-corpus::sources::claude_code` will ship
- It's bin-only, not importable as a library — no way for a future `rsry pickbrain` MCP tool or `chat-embed` integration test to consume it programmatically
- It hardcodes the MiniLM-via-fastembed path, can't pick up the `text_search` engine surface (PR #31)

## Refactor

1. `chat-embed/main.rs` → `chat-embed/src/lib.rs` + `chat-embed/src/main.rs` (thin CLI wrapper)
2. Replace `derive_embed_text` with a call into `agent-corpus::sources::claude_code` (parser is now the source of truth)
3. Keep MiniLM as the engine via fastembed-rs; expose the engine as `Arc<dyn TextSearchEngine>` so callers can swap to witchcraft or future engines without rewriting chat-embed
4. README updated to frame chat-embed clearly: "first-party CLI for indexing/querying Claude Code session via MiniLM + sqlite-vec. Uses LLO's substrate, isn't part of it."

## Falsifiability gates

- `chat-embed index` against a tempdir of fixture JSONLs produces the same row count as today's impl
- `chat-embed query foo` returns at least the same hits as today (regression: no quality drop from the parser swap)
- The `chat-embed` lib is importable from a small smoke test — `use chat_embed::index_sessions; index_sessions(...)` compiles and runs
- The substrate-non-leak gate sees `chat-embed`'s storage path is outside any arena (it's a sidecar `.db` per session corpus)

## Blocked on

- agent-corpus seed bead (`AgentSourceParser` + `ClaudeCode` impl) merged

## Risk

The chat-embed binary at HEAD was just shipped (PR #29 — `ab1170f`). Touching it within weeks of ship is fine but worth a regression test: ensure `cargo test -p leyline-chat-embed` and the existing CLI surface stay stable across the refactor.

comments.text:
**2026-06-17 close — superseded by lectio.**

Decision is **delete** rather than refactor. Lectio's MCP exposes the same Claude-session retrieval surface (`agent_memory.rs` + `claude_session.rs` adapters + the `text_index.rs` sink). The `chat-embed` binary in LLO is now redundant: anyone wanting "search claude sessions semantically" should call lectio's MCP rather than run a parallel MiniLM indexer.

The actual deletion can happen lazily — chat-embed has zero importers in the LLO workspace (confirmed in coupling audit F5, `docs/audits/2026-06-17-coupling-audit.md`). It's a standalone binary with no consumers. Deletion is a single PR ("delete `rs/ll-open/chat-embed/`") whenever someone gets around to it. Not gating anything; not closing the file scope down (lectio integration would happen on consumer side, not LLO side).

Closing here as "decision made; cleanup is mechanical."
comments.author: rsry-mcp

title: agent-corpus: add Codex + Slack source impls
description:
## Why

Follow-up to the agent-corpus seed bead. Once `AgentSourceParser` trait + `ClaudeCode` impl land, add the next two source modules distilled from pickbrain:

- `agent-corpus/src/sources/codex.rs` — OpenAI Codex session JSON parser (pickbrain's `codex.rs` is 631 LOC)
- `agent-corpus/src/sources/slack.rs` — Slack export ingestion (pickbrain's `slack.rs` is 1111 LOC)

Both are feature-gated so a deployment that only cares about Claude Code doesn't carry Slack-parsing weight.

## Cargo features

```toml
[features]
default = ["source-claude-code"]
source-claude-code = []
source-codex = []
source-slack = []
```

## Falsifiability gates

For each new source:
- `pending(fresh_watermark)` returns true when fresh source files exist; false otherwise
- `chunks_since` yields stable `node_id`s across re-ingest
- Round-trip test: parse a fixture session/export, assert chunk count + first/last node_id

## Blocked on

- agent-corpus seed bead (trait + ClaudeCode) merged

## Open question

Whether the Slack module should index public-channel exports only or also DMs (the pickbrain CLI exposes both via `--dm`). Defer to consumer policy — the parser yields all chunks; the consumer filters via metadata.

comments.text:
**2026-06-17 close — superseded by lectio.**

- **Codex source**: already shipped in lectio (`crates/memory-core/src/adapters/agent_memory.rs` walks `~/.codex/sessions/**/*.jsonl` + memories, URI scheme `codex://session/<stem>/entry/<n>`).
- **Slack source**: not in lectio yet. Filed as `lectio-60ee9c`.

Closing here per the same direction as `ley-line-open-79a37c`: lectio owns the agent-source ingest layer.
