title: 5f7100-1b: stress test for op_reparse + concurrent UDS queries (5fea4e follow-up)
description:
Follow-up to ley-line-open-5fea4e (closed at commit f5a0a45). The core fix is in — periodic snapshot timer now uses try_lock + skip; std::sync::Mutex<Connection> safety documented. This bead pins the regression-prevention test that the original 5fea4e acceptance listed.

**Test contract:**

- 10 concurrent `lsp_hover` UDS queries against different files
- 1 concurrent `reparse` op that takes ~500ms (load a fixture that's slow enough)
- Assert: all 10 hover queries complete within 2× the reparse duration (no head-of-line blocking)
- Assert: snapshot ticks during the reparse window log "live_db contended, skipping" but daemon stays Ready
- Assert: post-reparse, snapshots resume and complete normally

Prior commits this regression-pins:
- f5a0a45 — try_snapshot_or_log + safety doc
- 294fd6b — mutex poison recovery in embed drain (related — same recovery pattern)

**Files**: rs/ll-open/cli-lib/tests/integration.rs (new test), maybe a fixture under rs/ll-open/cli-lib/tests/fixtures/.

Once this stress test is in CI, the 5f7100-1 deadlock class is fully sealed. Until then, the fix is *correct* but not regression-pinned beyond the unit-level tests in cmd_daemon::tests::try_snapshot_or_log_*.

title: T7.6 F6: TLA+ canonicality counterexample search
description:
Bead 7.6 of T7 under decade ley-line-open-9d30ac. Channel: analysis.

**SCOPE UPDATED 2026-05-01** after §3 red-team. F6 now searches for canonicality counterexamples (a system meeting R1–R7 *outside* the vector-commitment family), not necessity counterexamples (any system meeting R1–R7 without Σ — too broad, since Verkle/accumulator/skip-list members trivially break it).

Falsifies the canonicality claim itself.

Concrete deliverables:
- docs/decades/spec/MerkleCAS_Canonicality.tla
- Encode R1–R7 (operationalized per §2) as TLA+ properties
- Encode Σ as the spec's state machine
- Encode "vector-commitment family" as a parameterized abstraction (any C : 𝓥* → 𝓒 with sublinear-update + CR-root)
- Apalache bounded model check at depth ≥ 7
- Search for a state-machine satisfying R1–R7 *outside* the vector-commitment family — i.e., one that achieves O(log n) authenticated updates without a vector-commitment root structure
- If found: canonicality has a gap; revisit decade
- If not found at depth ≥7: canonicality established within bounded scope (subject to bound-depth caveats)

A counterexample using Verkle, accumulator, or auth-skip-list is *not* a falsification — those are explicitly admitted in §3.4 as Σ-isomorphic substitutes.

Depends on T1.2 (TLA+ spec exists with operationalized R1–R7).

title: T7.5 F5: end-to-end composition test
description:
Bead 7.5 of T7 under decade ley-line-open-9d30ac. Channel: final.

Falsifies R7 — hash-only composition seams.

Concrete deliverables:
- tests/substrate/f5_composition.rs
- Stand up: ley-line-open daemon, mock signet (signs roots), mock workerd (verifies sig + executes), mock rosary (dispatches against root)
- Workflow: write → root advances → signet signs → rosary dispatches workerd against root → workerd verifies sig + fetches ρ(root) + executes → result lands back as new bead
- Assert: every cross-system message contains ONLY a hash + signature, no shared mutable state
- Negative: corrupt the root in transit → workerd refuses to execute

Depends on T5.1, T5.2, T5.3.

title: T7.4 F4: O(log n) diff-complexity benchmark
description:
Bead 7.4 of T7 under decade ley-line-open-9d30ac. Channel: final.

Falsifies R6 — registry-scale partial updates.

Concrete deliverables:
- benches/substrate/f4_diff_complexity.rs
- Build a 50,000-leaf Merkle tree
- Edit k=1 leaf; count rehashes during snapshot
- Predicted: O(log₂ 50000) ≈ 16 rehashes
- Pin: actual ≤ 20 rehashes (tolerates k-ary fan-out)
- Variant: k=10, k=100 changes — confirm O(log n + k)

Depends on T4.3 + T4.4.

title: T7.3 F3: MITM gossip integrity test
description:
Bead 7.3 of T7 under decade ley-line-open-9d30ac. Channel: final.

Falsifies (CR) — content addressing prevents tampering.

Concrete deliverables:
- tests/substrate/f3_mitm_gossip.rs
- Sender hosts blob B with hash σ(B) = h
- Receiver pulls h via mock-Pollen channel
- MITM substitutes B' with σ(B') ≠ h
- Receiver MUST reject; assert it does
- Variant: receiver pulls h, MITM passes σ(B) header but B' body — reject
- Variant: signature verification under T5.1 — root with valid sig but wrong content rejected

Depends on T3.1 (blob store) + T5.1 (sig).

title: T7.2 F2: N-writer throughput benchmark
description:
Bead 7.2 of T7 under decade ley-line-open-9d30ac. Channel: final.

Falsifies R1 — non-blocking concurrent advance.

Concrete deliverables:
- benches/substrate/f2_concurrent_writers.rs (Criterion bench)
- Measures committed-tx/sec at N ∈ {1, 2, 4, 8, 16} writer threads
- Workload: each writer touches disjoint files (ideal case for optimistic CCC)
- Adversarial workload: same file (forces full serialization via rebase)
- Pin: throughput at N=10 disjoint ≥ 4× N=1; throughput at N=10 same-file ≤ 1.5× N=1 (rebases)

Depends on T6.1 (CAS protocol) + T6.2 (rebase).

title: T7.1 F1: crash-mid-write integration test
description:
Bead 7.1 of T7 (falsification gauntlet) under decade ley-line-open-9d30ac. Channel: final.

Falsifies (IM) — partial writes invisible.

Concrete deliverables:
- tests/substrate/f1_crash_mid_write.rs
- Forks a writer; SIGKILL at random points during snapshot_to_arena
- Parent process opens arena read-only; verifies for every observed current_root: ρ(root) is non-empty AND σ(ρ(root)) == root
- Run the loop 1000× with random kill timing
- CI gate: any single failure breaks the build

Depends on T2.3 (verify on read).

title: T6.3 Conflict-resolution policy — CRDT-shaped per-table or app-level
description:
Bead 6.3 of T6 under decade ley-line-open-9d30ac. Channel: analysis.

Decision document: how do we resolve conflicts when 3-way merge fails? Per-table policy registry vs app-level callback vs CRDT-typed columns?

Concrete deliverables:
- Decision ADR at docs/decades/spec/conflict-resolution-policy.md
- Survey of options:
  a) Per-table policy: declare "last-writer-wins" / "max" / "set-union" per table
  b) App-level callback: caller provides resolve(base, ours, theirs) -> resolved
  c) CRDT-typed columns: column-level CRDT semantics (LWW, OR-Set, counter)
- Recommendation: hybrid — option (a) as default, (b) for app-specific tables, (c) for explicit CRDT columns
- Connects to ADR-A's restriction-map weights for structurally-aware resolution

Channel: analysis (decision doc, not code).

title: T6.2 Loser rebase: 3-way merge over Merkle subtrees
description:
Bead 6.2 of T6 under decade ley-line-open-9d30ac. Channel: final.

When CAS fails (someone else advanced root), the loser doesn't blindly retry — it rebases its delta onto the new root via 3-way merge.

Concrete deliverables:
- ll-core/core/src/merge.rs: pub fn three_way_merge(base: Hash, ours: Hash, theirs: Hash) -> Result<Hash, Conflict>
- Walks Merkle subtrees in parallel; resolves cleanly where only one side touched a subtree
- Returns Conflict on subtrees both sides modified incompatibly
- Per-table conflict resolution policy plugged in via T6.3
- Pin: trivially-disjoint deltas (different tables) merge without conflict

Depends on T6.1 (CAS protocol) + T4.4 (diff API).

title: T6.1 Writer protocol: read R; build R'; CAS(R, R')
T5.4 mache projects ρ(R_n) into FUSE view; root-aware hot-swap
description:
Bead 5.4 of T5 under decade ley-line-open-9d30ac. Channel: commentary.

Cross-repo: mache (../mache). FUSE view is keyed by current_root; hot-swap when root advances. Optionally allow pinning to a historical root for time-travel queries.

Concrete deliverables:
- mache: subscribe to ll-open daemon's root-advance events
- FUSE root inode tagged with current_root; refreshes on advance
- New CLI: mache mount --pin-root <hash> for time-travel
- Pin: F5 — mache view at root R is byte-identical regardless of when the mount is opened

Already partially aligned: mache has HotSwapGraph integration. T5.4 generalizes to root-aware (vs gen-aware).

title: T5.3 rosary bead body references R_n; dispatch fetches ρ(R_n)
description:
Bead 5.3 of T5 under decade ley-line-open-9d30ac. Channel: commentary.

Cross-repo: rosary (../rosary). Bead descriptions/comments can reference an arena root by hash; dispatch sets up the workspace by fetching ρ(R_n) into the agent's view.

Concrete deliverables:
- rosary: add bead.arena_root: Option<Hash> field
- rsry_dispatch: when arena_root is set, fetch ρ(root) into workspace before agent invocation
- Agent receives a "frozen" view at the bead's authored generation
- Pin: F5 — bead authored at root R is reproducible at root R regardless of when agent runs

SCOPING SPIKE FIRST: rosary already has scope-aware dispatch via file paths; this extends it to versioned snapshots.

title: T5.2 workerd manifest = Cap'n Proto + content-addressed root reference
description:
Bead 5.2 of T5 under decade ley-line-open-9d30ac. Channel: commentary.

Cross-repo: cloister (../cloister). Workerd workload manifest carries a content-addressed reference to the arena root the workload should execute against.

Concrete deliverables:
- cloister/cloister.capnp: add ArenaRef { root: Data; signature: Data; }
- Workload spec includes ArenaRef instead of arena path
- workerd init: fetch ρ(root) via local FsBlobStore (or remote via Pollen-shaped gossip later)
- Verify signature with signet pubkey before exec
- Pin: F5 — workerd execution against a known-bad root MUST fail at verification, not silently execute

SCOPING SPIKE FIRST: evaluate cloister's manifest format extensibility.

title: T5.1 signet signs R_n advance; cert binds key to advancement authority
description:
Bead 5.1 of T5 (composition seams) under decade ley-line-open-9d30ac. Channel: commentary.

Cross-repo: signet (../signet). Issue an Ed25519 signature over each new current_root before set_arena commits. Cert binds the signing key to "authorized to advance arena <arena_id>".

Concrete deliverables:
- signet: new cert claim type "arena-advancer:<arena_id>"
- ll-open/cli-lib/src/cmd_daemon.rs::snapshot_to_arena: call signet to sign current_root before atomic flip
- ll-core/core/src/control.rs: add root_signature: [u8;64] to Controller
- Reader (HotSwapGraph) verifies signature against trusted pubkey before swap
- Pin: F3 protocol — corrupt root → signature verification fails → reader rejects swap

SCOPING SPIKE FIRST: before commitment, evaluate signet's API for cert-binding-to-arena-id. May spawn signet-side beads.

title: T4.4 Diff API: (R_a, R_b) → changed-subtree set in O(log n + k)
description:
Bead 4.4 of T4 under decade ley-line-open-9d30ac. Channel: final.

Given two roots, return the set of changed subtree hashes. Walk both Merkle trees in parallel; descend only where parent hashes differ.

Concrete deliverables:
- ll-core/core/src/diff.rs: pub fn diff(a: Hash, b: Hash, store: &dyn BlobStore) -> ChangedSubtrees
- ChangedSubtrees enumerates: changed-table set + per-table changed-row-set
- Used by ADR-B (per-community sync) and rosary scope-aware dispatch
- Pin: F4 protocol — n=50000 leaves, k=1 changed; diff visits ≤ ⌈log₂ n⌉ + 1 nodes

Depends on T4.3 (reduce structure) + T3.1 (BlobStore for fetching subtrees).

title: T4.3 Top-level Merkle reduce: R = σ(∥ subtree hashes)
description:
Bead 4.3 of T4 under decade ley-line-open-9d30ac. Channel: final.

current_root computed from concatenation of subtree hashes (per-file via T4.1, per-table via T4.2) instead of σ(whole arena bytes).

Concrete deliverables:
- snapshot_to_arena: read all <table>_root values from _meta, sort by table name, concatenate, σ(concat) → current_root
- Replaces "hash whole arena" approach from T2.2
- Property: changing one file changes its hash → changes nodes/_ast/_source roots → changes top-level root, but other table-roots unchanged (subtree-localized propagation)
- Pin: F4 protocol — k=1 leaf change recomputes O(log n) hashes, not n

Depends on T4.1 + T4.2.

title: T4.2 Per-table Merkle hash in _meta
description:
Bead 4.2 of T4 under decade ley-line-open-9d30ac. Channel: final.

Compute σ(canonical-serialization(table_rows)) per derived table, store in _meta as <table>_root.

Concrete deliverables:
- Canonical serialization: rows ordered by primary key; field order fixed; NULL distinguished from missing
- ll-open/ts/src/schema.rs: helper canonical_table_hash(conn, table) -> Hash
- Tables to hash: nodes, _ast, _source, _file_index, node_refs, node_defs, _imports, _lsp* (when present)
- Update _meta on each table modification (snapshot time, not per-row)
- Pin: stable across hosts given same row set (DET axiom)

title: T4.1 Per-file Merkle hash in _file_index (hash-of-content)
description:
Bead 4.1 of T4 (subtree Merkle) under decade ley-line-open-9d30ac. Channel: final.

Add content_hash column to _file_index. cmd_parse computes σ(file_bytes) on parse and stores it. Becomes a Merkle leaf for the per-file subtree.

Concrete deliverables:
- ll-open/ts/src/schema.rs: ALTER TABLE _file_index ADD COLUMN content_hash BLOB
- Migration: backfill from existing rows by re-hashing on first reparse
- ll-open/cli-lib/src/cmd_parse.rs::parse_into_conn: blake3::hash(content) per file
- Pin: same content → same hash; bit-flip → different hash

title: T3.3 GC: retain N most recent roots + reachable subtree closure
description:
Bead 3.3 of T3 under decade ley-line-open-9d30ac. Channel: final.

Garbage collection over the objects/ store. Default retention: last 100 roots + reachability closure from each retained root.

Concrete deliverables:
- ll-core/core/src/blob_store.rs: gc(retained_roots: &[Hash]) -> GcStats
- Mark phase: walk Merkle subtree from each retained root, collect all reachable hashes
- Sweep phase: for each blob in objects/, delete if not in marked set
- Configurable retention via DAEMON_OBJECT_RETENTION env var or config
- Pin: dropping a root + GC removes its unique blobs but preserves shared subtrees

Depends on T4.4 (need diff API to walk subtrees) — actually loosely depends; can ship a coarse "retain N roots, walk linearly" version first.

title: T3.2 Atomic blob write — tmpfile + fsync + rename
description:
Bead 3.2 of T3 under decade ley-line-open-9d30ac. Channel: final.

FsBlobStore::put crashes-safely. Partial writes never become visible under their final hash-named path.

Concrete deliverables:
- Write to objects/.tmp/<random>; fsync data; fsync parent dir; rename atomically to objects/<aa>/<bb...>
- Linux: rename() is atomic per POSIX
- macOS: rename() is atomic via APFS; non-cross-volume only (assert)
- Pin: F1 protocol — kill writer between fsync and rename; reader must NOT see the partial blob under the final name

Depends on T3.1 (layout must exist).

title: T3.1 objects/<hash[0..2]>/<hash[2..]> layout in arena dir
description:
Bead 3.1 of T3 (CAS blob store) under decade ley-line-open-9d30ac. Channel: final.

git-shaped object directory next to the controller and arena files.

Concrete deliverables:
- ll-core/core/src/blob_store.rs: BlobStore trait + FsBlobStore impl
- Layout: <arena_dir>/objects/<hex(hash[0])>/<hex(hash[1..])>
- API: put(bytes) -> Hash, get(Hash) -> Option<Vec<u8>>, contains(Hash) -> bool
- Tests: round-trip, idempotent put (same content → same hash → existing file kept), get-of-missing returns None

comments.text:
## 2026-05-22 — Implementation pass on feat/cache-schema-ae89aa (executed in same branch as ae89aa per thoroughness directive)

### What landed

- **`rs/ll-core/core/src/blob_store.rs`** (~580 lines including tests) — FsBlobStore + MemBlobStore impls of the existing BlobStore trait
- **`rs/ll-core/core/src/lib.rs`** — module declaration + re-exports

### Beyond the bead's stated minimum

The bead specified put/get/contains + git-shaped layout + basic tests. Shipped additionally per the user thoroughness directive:

1. **Atomic write** — temp file in same dir + fsync + rename. POSIX rename atomicity guarantees no torn reads; failure modes documented in module rustdoc.
2. **Verify-on-read enforcement** — `get()` recomputes σ over retrieved bytes; mismatch = hard error naming the path. Honors the BlobStore trait doc's promise + ley-line-open-bad8f1 pattern. Tested with disk-corruption simulation.
3. **MemBlobStore** — companion in-memory impl with same axiom honoring (idempotent put, verify-on-read). Future tests across crates don't need TempDir-shaped fs setup; substrate consumer crates can ship trait-tests using MemBlobStore as the canonical fixture.
4. **`FsBlobStore::open` vs `FsBlobStore::new`** — open() auto-creates the root recursively; new() refuses missing root (for callers asserting external init). Both documented.
5. **`path_for()` public** — diagnostic accessor; useful for "where would this blob land?" tooling without breaking the abstraction.

### Test coverage (17 unit tests)

- **Round-trip:** fs + mem
- **Idempotency (IM axiom):** fs + mem, including a hardened "preserves original bytes under double-put" variant
- **Absence:** fs + mem (get of missing → None; contains → false)
- **Verify-on-read corruption:** fs (filesystem tamper) + mem (inner-map tamper) — both must refuse to vouch for mismatched bytes, error names path/key
- **Layout pin:** asserts the exact git-shape (2-char prefix dir, 62-char remainder file, both hex-only)
- **Prefix collision coexistence:** brute-forces two contents sharing byte[0]; both readable, neither overwritten
- **Edge cases:** empty blob (canonical BLAKE3-of-empty), 1 MiB large blob
- **Persistence:** close store, reopen at same root, contents readable
- **Constructor discipline:** new() refuses missing, open() creates parents recursively
- **`contains()` reflects state** — pre/post put

### Verification

- `cargo test -p leyline-core blob_store` → 17 / 17 pass
- `cargo test -p leyline-core` → 51 / 51 pass (full crate, including the 34 existing tests; nothing regressed)

### Open follow-ups (NOT in this bead's scope)

- GC of unreachable blobs — explicit `gc()` API per substrate.rs:152's (IM) axiom note (`get` may return None after explicit GC). Separate bead when prioritized.
- Concurrent-writer fuzz testing — two processes putting same content simultaneously. The atomic-rename design SHOULD handle it; would want a stress test before claiming so.
- Network-backed BlobStore impl (R2/S3) — needed for me-bundle remote distribution. Different bead, consumes the trait.

Status: ready to close once committed.

title: T4: Subtree Merkle structure
description:
Thread under decade ley-line-open-9d30ac. Per-file / per-table / per-community subtree hashes. Enables F4 (O(log n) diff complexity).

4 beads:
- 4.1 Per-file Merkle hash in _file_index (hash-of-content) (final)
- 4.2 Per-table Merkle hash in _meta (final)
- 4.3 Top-level Merkle reduce: R = σ(∥ subtree hashes) (final)
- 4.4 Diff API: (R_a, R_b) → changed-subtree set in O(log n + k) (final)

Aligns with ADR-A (sheaf cache stalks) and ADR-B (per-community sync) — both assume per-region Merkle hashes; this thread implements them.

Depends on T3 (need objects/ to address subtrees).

title: T3: Content-addressed blob store
description:
Thread under decade ley-line-open-9d30ac. git-shaped object directory. Multiple roots coexist. GC by reachability + retention policy.

3 beads:
- 3.1 objects/<hash[0..2]>/<hash[2..]> layout in arena dir (final)
- 3.2 Atomic blob write (tmpfile + rename) with fsync (final)
- 3.3 GC: retain N most recent roots + reachable subtree closure (final)

Depends on T2 (current_root must exist before objects/ keyed by it).

title: sheaf: structural_root XOR-fold property only tested for single-layer change; multi-layer delta unverified
description:
File: rs/ll-open/hdc/src/sheaf.rs, test 'structural_root_xor_folds_layers' (line 721).

The module doc and structural_root comments state: 'XOR fold is order-independent across layers' and the XOR-fold property means delta(structural) == XOR of deltas of each changed layer. The test at line 721-762 only verifies the single-layer case: only AST changes, Semantic stays the same. It asserts delta_struct == delta_ast.

The two-layer case is NOT tested: if both AST AND Semantic change simultaneously, the test does not verify that delta_struct == delta_ast XOR delta_sem. While this property is mathematically guaranteed by XOR associativity/commutativity (given that per-layer merkle roots are independent), the test explicitly disclaims it: 'We can't reconstruct without computing them' (line 735). This is avoidable — the test CAN compute both delta_ast and delta_sem and verify their XOR equals delta_struct.

The current test would NOT catch a hypothetical implementation bug where structural_root reused a per-layer intermediate rather than re-hashing each layer independently.

Fix sketch: extend the existing test or add 'structural_root_delta_multi_layer': set up cx and cx2 where both AST and Semantic stalks differ, compute delta_ast, delta_sem, and delta_struct, then assert delta_struct == delta_ast XOR delta_sem (byte-wise). This pins the additive property claimed in the doc.

comments.text: Fixed in commit 162dfc1. New test structural_root_xor_fold_holds_for_multi_layer_change mutates AST + Semantic simultaneously and asserts delta(structural) == delta(AST) XOR delta(Semantic) — catches a refactor that would cache an intermediate per layer.
comments.author: rsry-cli

title: sheaf: propagate_sections centroid-recovery claim only tested with identical stalks — noisy cluster unverified
description:
File: rs/ll-open/hdc/src/sheaf.rs, test 'propagate_sections_returns_centroid_per_component' (line 920) and bundle_majority_recovers_centroid_of_consistent_group (line 881).

The module docstring for propagate_sections states it 'recover[s] the centroid of a consistent group'. The only test for this (line 920-951) uses THREE IDENTICAL stalks — every bit in every stalk is the same. For identical inputs, majority-rule trivially returns the input; this test does not exercise any noise-tolerance property at all.

The doc claim is that the bundle lands 'near' a cluster of similar-but-not-identical stalks. A more meaningful test scenario: create 3 stalks that differ at ~5% of bits (Hamming ~410), compute bundle_majority, and verify the result has Hamming distance < 410 from each member. Under the theoretical bound for D=8192 and N=3 the bundle should be considerably closer to each member than a random vector. NO test covers this.

Specifically: if you flip 20% of bits in one stalk of a 3-stalk cluster and leave the other two identical, the tie structure shifts. The bundle may no longer equal the majority stalk exactly, and the claim 'the bundle is recoverable' is untested for the practical noisy case.

Fix sketch: add a test 'bundle_majority_cluster_with_noise' that:
1. Generates a base stalk.
2. Creates 3 stalks with small random perturbations (e.g. XOR with an expand_seed HV then AND with a sparse mask).
3. Verifies bundle_majority of the 3 is closer to each member than to a random unrelated stalk (i.e., Hamming < D/2 - margin to each member).
This is the test that would catch if bundle_majority was replaced with XOR-bundle (which satisfies the current identical-stalk test but fails the near-cluster case).

comments.text: Fixed in commit 162dfc1. New test bundle_majority_recovers_noisy_cluster_centroid: 5 copies of a base each with ~5% independent bit-flip noise; bundle must land within Hamming 50 of base AND be ≤ Hamming-distance to each individual copy (real denoising property, not tautological identity check). First attempt failed because per-copy PRNG seeds overlapped — fixed with blake3_seed mixing.
comments.author: rsry-cli

title: hdc: ModuleCodebook::base_vector and encode_module are two different encoding paths for same type

hdc-9: integration tests — per-layer + combined + radius-calibration
description:
## Scope

End-to-end integration tests under `--features hdc`. Drives the full pipeline: cold-parse → run HdcPass(s) → calibrate baselines → query ops.

## Tests to land

### `test_hdc_pass_populates_layers_for_fixture`

Cold-parse a small Go/Rust/Python fixture, run all 4 HdcPass instances, verify `_hdc` has rows for each scope at each layer.

### `test_hdc_search_finds_clone_within_radius`

Plant two structurally-identical functions with renamed variables. Encode. Query `op_hdc_search` with one as the query. Assert the other is in results within calibrated radius.

### `test_hdc_density_detects_hotspot_cluster`

Plant 10 near-identical functions (e.g., 10 try/catch wrappers). Query `op_hdc_density` against one of them. Assert count returned >= 9.

### `test_hdc_unbind_recovers_kind_at_known_position`

Encode a fixture function whose root child is a known kind. Call `op_hdc_unbind` for `child_0` role. Assert recovered kind matches.

### `test_hdc_combined_prefilter_top_k_correctness`

Populate 1000 scopes; one is structurally identical to a query at AST + Semantic layers. Combined-view top-50 prefilter contains it. Per-layer reranking surfaces it as #1.

### `test_hdc_radius_calibration_persists`

Cold-start daemon with populated `_hdc`. Verify `_hdc_baseline` has 4 rows, one per layer, with non-default median + mad values.

### `test_cross_language_clone_detection`

Plant the same `if-then-return` fixture in Rust + Go + Python (using canonical-kind alphabet from hdc-2). Query AST-layer with the Rust version. Assert all three are within calibrated radius. **This is the cross-language hotspot detection demonstration.**

### `test_hdc_disabled_no_schema_changes`

`cargo test -p leyline-cli-lib --tests` (without `hdc` feature): no `_hdc*` tables created in any test fixture. Existing schema unchanged.

## Files

- `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/tests/integration.rs` — append the 8 tests under `#[cfg(feature = "hdc")]`

## Definition of done

8 integration tests pass under `cargo test -p leyline-cli-lib --features hdc --tests`. Cross-language test demonstrates the cross-grammar invariance the canonical-kind alphabet was designed for.

## Parent

title: hdc-7: combined-view BLOB — unweighted XOR-bind prefilter
hdc-6: empirical radius calibration — daemon-startup baseline sampling
description:
## Scope

The fix for math-friend review B. Real codebases aren't iid — `self`/`if`/`return` co-occur far above chance, so the theoretical D/2 ± √D/2 prediction is the wrong null. Need empirical calibration of the radius threshold per layer.

## Algorithm

```
on_daemon_start():
    for each layer in {ast, semantic, temporal, module}:
        sample 10000 random pairs (a, b) of scopes from _hdc WHERE layer_kind = layer
        compute d_a_b = popcount(a.hv XOR b.hv) for each pair
        d_med = median(d_a_b values)
        mad = median(|d_a_b - d_med|)
        recommended_r = d_med - 3 * mad
        write to _hdc_baseline(layer_kind, median_distance, mad, sample_size, computed_at_ms)
```

The query ops in `hdc-5` use `_hdc_baseline.median_distance - 3 * mad` as their default `r` if the caller doesn't override.

## Why this matters

Without this calibration, every density/radius query is uncalibrated noise. With it:
- Radius queries produce *meaningful* equivalence-class membership tests
- Hotspot detection has principled thresholds
- Per-codebase tuning is automatic (a Python-monorepo and a Rust-microservice cluster will get different baselines, both correct for their domain)

## Recompute trigger

The baseline is invalidated when:
- Schema changes (rare)
- The corpus grows by >10% (`_hdc.scope_id` count increases significantly)
- Manual recalibration ops call

Otherwise sticky — baseline computed once, reused.

## Implementation

```rust
pub fn calibrate_radius(conn: &Connection, layer: LayerKind, sample_size: usize) -> Result<RadiusBaseline> {
    let pairs: Vec<(Vec<u8>, Vec<u8>)> = sample_random_pairs(conn, layer, sample_size)?;
    let distances: Vec<i64> = pairs.iter().map(|(a, b)| popcount_xor(a, b) as i64).collect();
    let median = quickselect_median(&distances);
    let mad = quickselect_median(&distances.iter().map(|d| (d - median).abs()).collect::<Vec<_>>());
    Ok(RadiusBaseline { median, mad, sample_size, computed_at_ms: now_ms() })
}
```

## Tests

- `calibrate_radius_uniform_baseline` — populate _hdc with i.i.d. random vectors, verify median ≈ D/2 and mad ≈ √D/4
- `calibrate_radius_skewed_baseline` — populate with vectors that share boilerplate prefix, verify median < D/2 (the "self/if/return" effect)
- `calibrate_radius_persists_in_baseline_table` — call calibrate, query _hdc_baseline, assert row present

## Files

- `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/src/daemon/hdc/calibrate.rs` (NEW)
- `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/src/cmd_daemon.rs` — call calibrate_radius for each layer at end of run_daemon init

## Definition of done

3 unit tests pass. Daemon startup runs calibration for each populated layer, records baseline, and `op_hdc_search` defaults to using calibrated r when client omits it.

## Parent

ley-line-open-96b1a9

title: hdc-3d: Temporal codebook — time-decayed co-edit matrix → simhash
description:
## Scope

Temporal-layer `BaseCodebook` from git history. Per math-friend review E, the right primitive is a **time-decayed co-edit matrix** simhashed per row.

## Recipe

```
1. For each pair (i, j) of scopes (functions, files), accumulate weight:
   w_ij = Σ_{commits c containing both i and j} exp(-(t_now - t_c)/τ)
   τ ≈ 90 days

2. The temporal vector for scope i is the row W[i, :] (real-valued).

3. Hash via Charikar simhash (same as semantic layer):
   H_temporal(i) = sign(W[i, :] · R)
   where R is a per-repo seeded random matrix.
```

## Why this works (per review)

- **Append-only updates**: a new commit nudges weights for the affected pairs; ~1% bit churn per new commit (per Charikar's stability properties)
- **Fully deterministic** given seed: any daemon with the same git history + same seed produces identical temporal HVs
- **τ knob**: default 90 days. Older commits decay exponentially. Effectively makes the temporal HV a "recent edit pattern" fingerprint.

## Implementation considerations

- The co-edit matrix W can be large (N × N where N is scope count). Most entries are zero — store sparsely.
- Recompute incrementally on each new commit: only weights involving newly-touched scopes change.
- `head_sha` is the basis for `_hdc_baseline.basis` for this layer — when HEAD advances, the temporal HVs may need refresh.

```rust
pub struct TemporalCodebook {
    coedit: SparseMatrix,           // scope × scope, time-decayed
    hyperplanes: Box<[[f32; ?]; D]>, // simhash projection
    tau_seconds: u64,                // 90 days default
}

impl TemporalCodebook {
    pub fn refresh_from_git_log(&mut self, source_dir: &Path) -> Result<()> { ... }
    pub fn project(&self, scope_id: &str) -> [u8; D/8] { ... }
}
```

## Tests

- `temporal_codebook_decays_old_commits` — synthetic git log; verify weights at t=10·τ are ~exp(-10) of fresh weights
- `temporal_codebook_simhash_charikar` — same Charikar collision-probability test as semantic codebook
- `temporal_codebook_incremental_stability` — adding one commit causes <2% bit changes for unaffected scopes

## Files

- `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/src/daemon/hdc/codebook/temporal.rs` (NEW)

## Definition of done

3 unit tests pass. Temporal HVs incrementally stable under realistic commit cadence.

## Parent

ley-line-open-96b1a9

title: hdc-3c: Semantic codebook — Charikar signed-projection from dense embeddings
hdc-3b: Module codebook — structural module-hash from existing CAS primitive
description:
## Scope

Module-layer `BaseCodebook` for code-organization topology. Reuses the CAS primitive ley-line already has (content-addressed module hashes).

## Recipe

For each module/class/file scope, compute hypervector encoding the structural skeleton at that level:

```
H_module(scope) =
    base[scope_kind: module|class|file]
    ⊕ ρ_for_each_child · H_child_canonical_skeleton
    ⊕ ρ_dependencies · H_set_of_dependency_module_ids
```

Where:
- `child_canonical_skeleton` is a hash of the canonical-kind sequence at the child's signature level (function: parameter kinds + return kind; class: method canonical-kind set)
- `dependency_module_ids` is the set of imported/depended-on module IDs (using ley-line's existing CAS hashes for stable cross-instance addressing)

## Why this is cheap

The CAS layer already gives us deterministic module hashes. We just *bind them as roles* into a hypervector instead of using them as Merkle leaves. The XOR-bind elevates equality-only Merkle to continuous Hamming similarity at the module level.

## Tests

- `module_codebook_deterministic` — same module structure → same HV across runs
- `module_codebook_method_reorder_invariant` — same class with methods in different order → same HV (use sorted multiset)
- `module_codebook_dependency_isomorphism` — two classes that depend on isomorphic dependency sets produce nearby HVs

## Files

- `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/src/daemon/hdc/codebook/module.rs` (NEW)

## Definition of done

The 3 unit tests pass. Module HVs deterministic and method-reorder-invariant.

## Parent

ley-line-open-96b1a9

title: 5f7100-12: edge-case test sweep — 10 untested paths from review (concurrency, crashes, overflow)
description:
Parent: 5f7100. The hardening review surfaced 10 specific edge cases the integration suite doesn't cover. One bead because they're all in tests/integration.rs and dispatching 10 separately would just add overhead.

Tests to add, in risk order:

1. test_concurrent_uds_load — N=20 parallel clients, mix of lsp_hover + query, assert no deadlock and bounded p99 latency (< 2s with mocks)
2. test_lsp_server_crash_mid_enrichment — kill the spawned LSP server while op_lsp_hover triggers try_enrich_file. Daemon must recover, return error, no panic, no half-populated _lsp.
3. test_arena_buffer_overflow — parse a synthetic source tree large enough that db_bytes > buf_capacity. Daemon must bail cleanly; verify auto-resize was attempted; assert state is recoverable on restart.
4. test_git_watcher_no_repo — start daemon with --source pointing at a non-git directory. Verify no CPU burn, no infinite log spam, ideally auto-disable after N consecutive failures.
5. test_op_reparse_broken_symlink — pass --source <broken symlink> AND a deleted file path. Clean error; phase doesn't stick at Parsing forever.
6. test_vec_search_dim_mismatch — register a 768d embedder against a 384d VectorIndex. Assert clean error, no panic.
7. test_op_query_destructive — DROP/DELETE/UPDATE via op_query. Behavior should match the resolution of 5f7100-11 (gate or document).
8. test_op_reparse_snapshot_race — fire reparse + force snapshot in parallel. Assert generation strictly monotonic, no torn snapshot in arena.
9. test_embed_queue_overflow — promote 1M node_ids to embed_queue. Assert RSS bounded; if the queue is bounded, assert eviction policy.
10. test_warm_start_corrupted_controller — write garbage bytes into .ctrl. Assert daemon either logs a warn before cold start (post-5f7100-6) or refuses to start.

Each test maps 1:1 to a fix bead it validates. Some can be `#[ignore]` until the upstream fix lands so we don't have failing CI.

title: 5f7100-11: MCP HTTP trust boundary — document + plan token handshake (op_query is a SQL foot-gun)
description:
Parent: 5f7100. Severity: documentation gap with security impact.

daemon/mcp.rs:18-20 explicitly defers auth to the cloister gateway: "Per-tool authorization, mTLS termination, and identity scoping are intentionally out of scope here". Anyone on localhost can `POST /mcp tools/call query "DROP TABLE nodes"` — op_query (ops.rs:336ish) runs raw SQL against the live db.

Local-process boundary is the only thing protecting the database today. That's defensible if and only if the LLO daemon is run by the same user that owns the data; in any shared-machine scenario this is broken.

Acceptance:
- Doc note in mcp.rs + README: "MCP HTTP transport binds 127.0.0.1 with no auth; trust boundary is the local user. Use cloister + notme-proxy for any multi-tenant deployment."
- Plan a token handshake: optional --mcp-token <hex> flag; require it as Authorization: Bearer header; deny by default if not set in non-dev mode.
- Consider gating op_query behind a feature flag or restricting to SELECT-only via parser check (`prepare()` then check the kind).
- Test: tools/call query with DROP/DELETE/UPDATE either errors cleanly or is gated.

comments.text: Mostly superseded by ADR-0022 (shipped via bead `b885d1`, PR #66). The "document + plan token handshake" work was the bead's scope; ADR-0022 did both — token gate is implemented at `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/src/daemon/mcp.rs:694` (`auth::require_token` middleware on `/mcp` routes). The remaining loose end is the module docstring at `mcp.rs:18-20` still says "Per-tool authorization, mTLS termination, and identity scoping are intentionally out of scope here" — that's now stale since the token gate ships in-tree. Worth a tiny follow-up to refresh the docstring; not blocking. Closing as superseded.
comments.author: rsry-mcp

comments.text: Reopen-and-complete: the prior close left a loose end (stale mcp.rs:18-20 docstring). PR #109 ships the docstring refresh — names the in-tree token gate (`auth::require_token` middleware, ADR-0022) AND keeps "out of scope, lives in cloister" framing for what genuinely still belongs there (per-tool authorization / vault slice grants per ADR-0013, mTLS, cross-machine identity scoping, OIDC/CF Access — ADR-0022 Mode B). This commit completes the close per the "never partial close" discipline.
comments.author: rsry-mcp

title: 5f7100-10: small-lint sweep — silent fs errors, SQL dup, URI helper, monotonic priority, configurable skips
description:
Parent: 5f7100. Bundles six small mechanical cleanups into one bead — the gain from splitting them is less than the dispatch overhead.

1. socket.rs:28-41 — five `let _ = std::fs::...` calls swallow filesystem errors silently. remove_file on stale socket is fine; create_dir_all + symlink failures should at least log::warn.
2. socket.rs:82-101 — six `let _ = writer.write_all(...)` on the response path. Add log::trace! so dropped responses are diagnosable.
3. ops.rs — `LIKE ?1 || '%'` repeated 5× (file-prefix queries, lines around 567/683/736/805/775). Extract one helper `file_prefix_clause(file: &str) -> (String, Vec<String>)`.
4. ops.rs:766-771, 798-801 — file:// URI strip duplicated. One helper `normalize_file_uri(s: &str) -> &str`.
5. lsp_pass.rs:38-49 + lsp_pass.rs:16-29 — two parallel hardcoded language maps (ext→id and id→server) that can drift. Single map keyed by enum variant.
6. cmd_parse.rs:656-659 — hardcoded skip list `[".", "node_modules", "vendor", "target"]`. Make configurable via Daemon CLI flag (`--ignore <pattern>`) and/or a `.leylineignore` file.
7. embed.rs:170-172 — embed-task priority is wall-clock micros; clock skew (NTP step) reorders tasks unpredictably. Use a monotonic `AtomicU64` counter shared across the queue.

Acceptance: each item has either a test demonstrating the new behavior (configurable skip, monotonic priority) or is a non-behavioral refactor (helpers, log adds). Clippy clean.

title: 5f7100-9: single source of truth for op names — mcp.rs / ops.rs / socket.rs drift risk
description:
Parent: 5f7100.

The same canonical set of op names appears in three independent string lists:
- daemon/socket.rs:15 — STATE_CHANGING_OPS = ["load", "reparse", "flush", "snapshot", "enrich"]
- daemon/ops.rs:21-44 — match table that dispatches each op name
- daemon/mcp.rs:50-184 — tool registry advertising names

Adding a new op means touching all three; missing one silently breaks something (event emission, MCP advertisement, or dispatch).

Acceptance: define `enum OpName { Status, Snapshot, Reparse, ... }` (or `&'static str` constants in one module) and reference it from all three sites. Optionally derive `IntoEnumIterator` so the registry can be auto-generated.

Acceptance test: a compile-time assertion (or unit test) that asserts every variant has a registry entry, dispatch arm, and is or isn't in STATE_CHANGING_OPS as documented.

Verified 2026-06-25 at `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/src/daemon/wire.rs:21,36,67` — `BASE_OP_NAMES` is the canonical SoT; `socket.rs:13` references `daemon::ops::is_state_changing` which reads `BASE_OP_NAMES`; `mcp.rs` tool_registry has drift test enforcing `tool_registry ⊆ BASE_OP_NAMES`. Closing.

title: 5f7100-8: extract magic numbers to named constants (one bead, batched)
description:
Parent: 5f7100. Pure cleanup.

title: 5f7100-6: warm-start silently swallows controller/arena open errors → invisible cold start
description:
Parent: 5f7100. Severity: observability gap.

cmd_daemon.rs:374-377 and 384-387 (try_warm_start): both `Controller::open_or_create` and `File::open(arena_path)` fall through with `Err(_) => return Ok(None)`. A corrupt controller, permissions failure, or unreadable arena file produces a cold start with zero indication anything went wrong — the user just sees `cold start` in the log and assumes it's intended.

Acceptance:
- log::warn! the underlying error before returning Ok(None) — distinguish "no arena yet" (true cold start) from "arena exists but unreadable" (likely a real bug)
- consider returning Result<Option<Connection>, anyhow::Error> instead of Result<Option<...>> so the caller can decide policy
- test: corrupt the controller, restart daemon, assert warn-log present (or that cold start is gated behind a flag)

comments.text: Stale — fix already landed. Verified 2026-06-25 at `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/src/cmd_daemon.rs:580-627`. `try_warm_start` now explicitly distinguishes FRESH state (silent `Ok(None)`) from REAL ERROR (`log::warn!` with arena path + error context before `Ok(None)`). Both `Controller::open_or_create` failures (line 594) and `File::open(arena_path)` failures (line 611) emit warn-logs. Header doc-comment at line 584-590 documents the two-class discipline explicitly. Closing.
comments.author: rsry-mcp

title: a46586-3: priority queue for MCP-driven re-embedding
description: Parent: a46586. Depends on a46586-2. Add embed_queue: BinaryHeap<EmbedTask> to DaemonContext. Query ops promote node_ids on access. Background drain task processes batches every 1s, embeds via current Embedder, writes to VectorIndex, emits daemon.embed.complete.

title: a46586-2: EmbeddingPass with no-op default embedder
description: Parent: a46586. Depends on a46586-1. Define Embedder trait + ZeroEmbedder default + EmbeddingPass scaffold registered via DaemonExt. Track staleness via _meta.vec_parse_basis. Add op_vec_search. Private repo overrides Embedder via DaemonExt::enrichment_passes().

title: a46586-1: move vector.rs from leyline-fs to cli-lib
description: Parent: a46586. vector.rs has zero production callers in leyline-fs (only test refs). With FUSE optional, vec0 sidecar belongs near EnrichmentPass framework. Move to cli-lib::daemon::vec_index, flip sqlite-vec dep, drop FUSE vector_search adapter and graph::vectors accessors.

title: Move VectorIndex from leyline-fs to enrichment layer + priority queue reingestion
description:
## Problem

`VectorIndex` (sqlite-vec `vec0` virtual table) lives in `leyline-fs` which is the FUSE/NFS filesystem crate. Vectors have nothing to do with mounts. The sqlite-vec dependency is open (not private), so the index infrastructure belongs in LLO proper — near the enrichment pipeline.

## What to do

1. **Move `vector.rs`** from `rs/ll-open/fs/src/vector.rs` to `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/src/daemon/` or a new `rs/ll-open/vec/` crate. Keep the sidecar db design (vec0 can't survive sqlite3_serialize).

2. **Create `EmbeddingPass`** as an EnrichmentPass. It reads `nodes` from the living db, generates embeddings, writes to the sidecar VectorIndex. Tracks staleness via `_meta.vec_parse_basis`.

3. **Priority queue FIFO for reingestion**: When a file is accessed/queried via MCP tools, its embedding should get priority for re-computation if stale. Pattern:
   - Maintain a priority queue of node_ids needing re-embedding
   - MCP access (find_callers, get_node, etc.) promotes accessed nodes
   - Background embedding pass drains the queue, highest-priority first
   - Similar to the sheaf cache invalidation pattern but for vectors

## Notes

- The embedding MODEL is private (ley-line), but the INDEX is open infrastructure
- vec0 virtual tables can't survive sqlite3_serialize/deserialize (arena round-trip), so sidecar db is correct
- The EmbeddingPass would be registered via DaemonExt.enrichment_passes() from ley-line private

title: ADR: arena as shared memory — mache reads SQLite from arena buffer, not file
description:
## Problem

Mache copies the arena buffer to a temp file, then opens it with sql.Open(). This defeats the zero-copy design. The arena IS shared memory — both processes should read from the same buffer.

## Context

ADR-001 describes POSIX shared memory (shm_open) as the arena substrate. Cloudflare's workerd/DO model shows the pattern: SQLite embedded in a V8 isolate, no file I/O. The .db is a data structure in memory, not a file.

## Design options

1. **Go VFS shim** — custom SQLite VFS for modernc.org/sqlite that reads from a byte slice (mmap'd arena buffer). Mache reads in place.

2. **Daemon as query proxy** — mache sends SQL queries over UDS socket, daemon runs them via sqlite3_deserialize and returns results. Mache never opens SQLite. The daemon IS the SQLite process.

3. **POSIX shared memory** — full ADR-001 vision. Arena lives in /dev/shm (Linux) or shm_open (macOS). Both processes mmap the same object. Go's sql.Open can open /dev/shm/leyline-default on Linux.

Option 2 is closest to workerd's model (the DO runtime owns storage, the app sends queries). The daemon's `query` op already does this.

## The .db file is a checkpoint, not the live path

The on-disk .db should be a portable snapshot (DELETE journal, single file, cp-able). The live path is always through the arena buffer or UDS socket. Remote: send the serialized bytes over the network (ley-line's existing transport).

title: perf: in-memory SQLite during parse, serialize to disk at end
description:
Currently cmd_parse opens a file-based SQLite DB and writes throughout parsing. Each INSERT hits the WAL, fsync, and disk I/O.

Fix: open `:memory:` SQLite during parse. After all files are parsed, use sqlite3_serialize() to dump the entire DB to a byte buffer, then write to disk in one shot. Or use ATTACH to copy from memory to disk.

codebase-memory-mcp does this — builds in-memory, dumps when done.

Expected: significant I/O reduction during parse. The bulk of time is tree-sitter CPU, but eliminating WAL overhead helps.

title: Binary file detection: skip files with null bytes in first 8KB
description: Before parsing a file, check the first 8KB for null bytes. If present, skip (it's binary). Same heuristic git uses. Prevents binary blobs (images, compiled output, .wasm) from polluting the .db.

title: Support extensionless files (Dockerfile, Makefile) via sentinel detection
description: leyline parse skips files without recognized extensions. Needs sentinel-file detection: if a file is named "Dockerfile", parse it as dockerfile syntax. If "Makefile", parse as make. Mache's internal/lang/lang.go already has SentinelFiles per language — LLO needs the same concept in its language detection.

title: Priority queue: user MCP request promotes files to front of parse queue
description:
When mache receives find_callers("NormalizeID") during initial parse, it should tell the daemon "parse graph/graph.go next" so the user gets results faster.

Flow:
1. MCP tool request → mache can't resolve → missing file
2. mache sends {"op": "prioritize", "files": ["graph/graph.go"]} over UDS
3. daemon moves those files to front of parse queue
4. parse → flip → mache hot-swaps → retries the tool request

Requires: daemon parse queue (not just sequential walk), UDS prioritize op, mache retry-after-swap logic.

comments.text: Subsumed by decade ley-line-open-eb0252 (living daemon). Priority queue depends on the file watcher + reparse loop.
comments.author: rsry-mcp

title: Progressive/streaming parse: incremental arena flips as files are parsed
description:
Instead of parsing all files → single arena flip, flip incrementally:
parse batch of N files → load partial .db → arena flip → mache hot-swaps → continue

mache's graph grows progressively. MCP tools return partial results immediately. Users don't wait 10s for the first response.

Design:
- Batch size: 10-20 files per flip (balance latency vs overhead)
- Each flip is a complete valid .db (just with fewer files)
- Sheaf topology can inform batch order (high-fan-in files first)
- node_refs/node_defs populated incrementally as files are parsed

comments.text: Subsumed by decade ley-line-open-eb0252 (living daemon). Progressive parse is step 4 of the daemon lifecycle.
comments.author: rsry-mcp

title: composition interface — LL enrichment hook on LLO base .db
description:
Define how ley-line (private) layers enrichments onto an LLO-produced .db without LLO needing to know about LL. The .db file is the contract — LL opens it, adds tables (embeddings, sheaf cache), closes it. LLO never imports LL. LL imports LLO as a dependency.

The answer is probably: no code needed in LLO. The .db schema IS the interface. Document the table contracts and let LL do whatever it wants. This bead is about writing that documentation and validating the approach with a round-trip test (LLO parse → LL enrich → mache mount).

**Why:** Without this, LL vs LLO boundary stays undefined and features drift apart.

comments.text: The composition interface is now mechanically solved: DaemonExt::enrichment_passes() lets LL register passes with disjoint write sets. EnrichmentPass trait enforces reads()/writes() declaration. The framework is committed (557e8a4). What remains: write the TABLE_CONTRACT.md documenting which tables each layer owns.
comments.author: rsry-mcp

title: cross-ref extraction — populate node_refs/node_defs from _ast
description:
Add a pass (either in `ll-open parse` or as a separate `ll-open enrich-refs` subcommand) that queries the _ast table for call_expression, selector_expression, import_spec etc. and populates node_refs (token, node_id) and node_defs (token, dir_id) tables.

These tables power mache's callers/callees graph (GetCallers/GetCallees in sqlite_graph.go). Currently only populated by mache's SitterWalker (CGO path) or `mache build`. Having ll-open produce them means mache can skip CGO entirely for the refs path too.

**Why:** mache's fast path already checks for node_refs in the main DB (sqlite_graph.go:149). If present, it skips the sidecar .refs.db entirely.

title: ll-open enrich-lsp subcommand — LSP enrichment into existing .db
description:
Add `ll-open enrich-lsp` subcommand that takes an existing .db (produced by `ll-open parse`) and enriches it with _lsp, _lsp_hover, _lsp_defs, _lsp_refs tables by spawning language servers via the lsp/ crate.

The lsp/ crate (client.rs, protocol.rs) already supports: document_symbols, definition, references, hover, completion, diagnostics. This subcommand wires those into the .db enrichment pipeline.

**Why:** mache's serve_lsp.go and serve_handlers.go fall back to _lsp_defs/_lsp_refs tables when present. Pre-baking these avoids live LSP at mount time.

comments.text: The enrichment framework is in place (557e8a4): EnrichmentPass trait, op_enrich UDS op, dependency resolution. What remains: implement the actual LspEnrichmentPass that spawns language servers and populates _lsp* tables. This is a ley-line private pass registered via DaemonExt::enrichment_passes().
comments.author: rsry-mcp

title: Second review family: public-API review keyed on node_defs (own restriction + false-skip) [was f463aa]
description:
ADR-0031 next move: prove the per-family pattern generalizes beyond call-target. Second family = PUBLIC-API review of a function/type, keyed on node_defs (def token + qualifier + canonical_kind + signature-relevant rows), not node_refs. Build its restriction (sound superset of what a public-API review observes) via the reusable restriction_cache module, a distinct expensive review-result stand-in, the oracle, and the policy table with its OWN false-skip measurement. Then roadmap: import review → _imports; unsafe/unwrap → token/operator restriction. Recreated after beads.db reset lost id f463aa. Depends on the re-key bead (module); serialize after it (both touch restriction_review_real.rs / the module).

Verification: cargo test -p leyline-sheaf public-API restriction fixtures; Restriction false_skip_rate == 0.

comments.text:
SECOND REVIEW FAMILY SHIPPED — public-API review keyed on node_defs (ADR-0031 f463aa generalization proof). Branch worktree-agent-a750bf1ba988ce4aa, commit 4b1273d. task ci EXIT 0 (green). Not pushed, no PR; bead left open.

WHAT WAS BUILT (extends the reusable restriction_cache module + a mirror driver):
- restriction_cache.rs: ApiDefRow (node_defs row enriched with visibility, sig_identity, sig_type_tokens), restriction_for_public_api(), review_public_api()->PublicSurface. Parser-independent; ships in production.
- tests/common/mod.rs: build_api_defs() parser-dependent extraction — signature stable identity (signature_node_hash: declaration subtree MINUS body), param/return TYPE tokens (type_identifier + primitive_type leaves under parameters+return_type), visibility (visibility_modifier child starts-with "pub"). Body content never enters.
- tests/restriction_review_public_api.rs: 6 real Rust before→after fixtures over a two-file corpus (score names corpus type Widget in its signature, calls private helper in body).

PUBLIC-API RESTRICTION DEFINITION (cheap, sound superset, DISTINCT from result):
hash{ token, qualifier, canonical_kind, is_public(visibility), sig_identity(decl-subtree-minus-body node_hash), sorted sig_type_tokens, AND the node_defs those type tokens resolve to across the whole corpus via token-indexed point lookup }. The cross-corpus typedef arm is the multi-object span.
review_public_api = the expensive DISTINCT stand-in: visibility-gated, unindexed cross-corpus JOIN of each signature type against ALL node_defs -> resolved PublicSurface descriptor (NOT a hash, != restriction).

POLICY TABLE (verdict test, own false-skip measurement):
policy       false_skip  true_skip   recompute_saved
WholeObject   0/3         0/3         0
AstShape      2/3         2/3         2 (unsafe)
Restriction   0/3         3/3         3
=> Restriction false_skip_rate == 0 (sound superset). true_skip 3/3 > WholeObject 0/3. AstShape false-skips 2/3 — the ADR-0030 blind spot on SIGNATURE identifiers (i64->i32 and Widget->Panel are identifier-text changes invisible to the kind-shape hash).

BODY-CHANGE-SKIPS + SIGNATURE-CHANGE-RECOMPUTES (the specific asks):
- whitespace          review_unchanged  Restriction SKIP  (WholeObject recompute)
- body-change (base+w.id -> base*w.id+7, signature identical)  review_unchanged  Restriction SKIP  <- THE KEY WIN
- helper-rename (rename the private helper score CALLS, body-only)  review_unchanged  Restriction SKIP  <- family distinction: call-target restriction WOULD invalidate here, public-API does NOT (asserted in body_only_edit_leaves_public_api_restriction_unchanged)
- sig-return-type (i64 -> i32)  review_changed  Restriction RECOMPUTE  (AstShape false-skips: both primitive_type)
- visibility (pub fn -> fn)  review_changed  Restriction RECOMPUTE
- sig-typedef-rename (Widget->Panel in math.rs ONLY; score's file byte-identical)  review_changed  Restriction RECOMPUTE  <- multi-object span across node_defs

HONESTY (tighter / cheaper / sound — none failed):
- TIGHTER than object: body/whitespace/helper-rename skip while whole-object recomputes (measured).
- CHEAPER than review: rows touched 1 vs 406/4006/20006 at 203/2003/10003 defs; wall time 7.6us vs 144.4us at 10k defs = 18.9x faster. Small-scale inversion printed not hidden (0.5x at 203 defs: SHA-256 constant beats a tiny in-memory join) — same crossover as call-target.
- SOUND superset: false_skip 0/3 by oracle + by-construction (restriction hashes a superset of every review input).

Measured vs inferred: policy table, oracle outcomes, and cost sweep are MEASURED (4 tests pass). The "real public-API review (docs/ABI/semver-diff) widens the cost gap" framing is INFERRED — review_public_api is a deliberate lower-bound stand-in, same as the call-target family.

Tests: public_api_extraction_sanity, body_only_edit_leaves_public_api_restriction_unchanged, public_api_review_verdict, public_api_restriction_is_cheaper_than_review — all pass. cargo fmt --all clean.
comments.author: rsry-mcp

title: T2.b5 — HDC ↔ DFG bridge: per-DFG-connected-component stalks (optional) (dataflow-substrate/dfg-ssa)
description:
Decade `dataflow-substrate`, thread `dfg-ssa`. Blocks: T2.b4 (dfg emission).

**OPTIONAL.** Kill this bead if it competes with T3 for review budget.

## Deliverable

For every DFG-connected component in a function, populate an HDC stalk at scope `dfg://{node_hash}::cc{idx}`. Enables "does this dataflow-connected-component look like a known-vulnerable shape?" queries via existing HDC ops.

Files: extend `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/src/daemon/hdc_enrich.rs`.

## Reconciliation with HDC

HDC stalks are function-level today; this makes them per-DFG-CC. A strictly finer stalk, not a replacement. Join key with `_dfg`: `node_hash` + cc_idx.

## Close condition

`cargo test -p leyline-cli-lib hdc_dfg_bridge` — planted fixture with 2 disjoint DFG components produces 2 distinct HDC stalks under the same function's node_hash.

title: Design: signingTime attribute alignment between leyline-sign::cms (Rust) and go-cms (Go)
description:
Refiled 2026-07-10 after beads DB desync — original ID was `ley-line-open-dc8b85`.

Cross-runtime spec-drift risk from overlap detector 2026-07-09.

## The finding

- LLO's `leyline-sign::cms` (Rust) — OMITS `signingTime` attribute for wasm32 portability
- `go-cms` (Go, at `~/remotes/art/go-cms/pkg/cms/`) — INCLUDES it with TimeSource interface

Both implement RFC 5652 + RFC 8419 CMS/PKCS#7 with Ed25519. Not a fork; parallel implementations. But signature attribute set diverges.

## Why this matters

Cloister-issued signatures (Rust path) vs signet-issued (Go path) have different signed-attribute sets. Downstream verifiers see semantically different envelopes.

## Options

- **A**: Add optional `signing-time` feature to LLO; wasm builds skip, host builds enable
- **B**: Drop signingTime from go-cms
- **C**: Document the split as intentional

Recommend A — feature-gate in LLO. Preserves signet behavior + keeps wasm portable.

## Related

- Signet consolidation (PR #133) accepted Path B (drop) since no signet CMS in production
- Bead for signet leyline-sign fork consolidation shipped v0.7.0
- Cross-repo overlap agent 2026-07-09

title: P7: MAX_DENSE_ELEMENTS silent-skip on axiom check + NEIGHBORHOOD_MAX_DEPTH bounds wrong quantity
description:
Math-friend audit (agent `a9d5e5a2`) 2026-07-10 found two guard-quantity issues.

## P7a — MAX_DENSE_ELEMENTS silent-skip

`rs/ll-open/sheaf/src/complex.rs:1102-1104` — `assert_cochain_complex` silently `return`s above `MAX_DENSE_ELEMENTS = 10_000_000`. The δ¹∘δ⁰ = 0 axiom simply stops being checked for large complexes. **This is worse than a limit — it's silent verification disappearance.**

## P7b — NEIGHBORHOOD_MAX_DEPTH bounds wrong quantity

`rs/ll-open/cli-lib/src/daemon/ops.rs:2437` — `NEIGHBORHOOD_MAX_DEPTH = 4`, clamped at `:2445`. Also incoherent with the cascade's `3` (bead `4eef8d` — a consumer can see a radius-4 neighborhood that the cascade will never invalidate). But the deeper problem: depth caps don't bound response size on co-occurrence graphs — one observation with k mentions creates a k-clique (`complex_build_pass.rs:424-430`), so a radius-1 ball can already be O(V). The guard guards the wrong quantity.

## Fix

- **P7a**: log at warn (or fail-loud in debug builds) when skipping, OR subsample-verify a random subset. Something ≠ silently returning.
- **P7b**: bound VISITED-NODE COUNT (e.g. `MAX_NEIGHBORHOOD_CELLS`) instead of or in addition to hops. Reconcile the 4-vs-3 with the cascade radius once bead `4eef8d` (P3) lands.

## Related

- Router constants (`granularity_router.rs:69,76`): `LOW_DELTA0_THRESHOLD = 0.1` and `MANY_CELLS_THRESHOLD = 8` — Fable explicitly recommends KEEPING these as documented heuristics + the existing measurement plan (bead `5b58ff`). NOT part of P7 scope.

## Files

- `rs/ll-open/sheaf/src/complex.rs` (P7a)
- `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/src/daemon/ops.rs` (P7b)

## Rules

- No cascade-depth changes here (that's P3)
- No router-constant changes here (documented heuristic, tracked in `5b58ff`)

comments.text: PR open: https://github.com/agentic-research/ley-line-open/pull/174 (commit 6b1e133, batch PR fix/sheaf-audit-followups). P7a: assert_cochain_complex skip now logs at warn with exact matrix sizes ("absence of a panic here is not evidence the axiom holds"); log dep added to leyline-sheaf via workspace. P7b: NEIGHBORHOOD_MAX_CELLS = 1_000 hard cap on emitted cells + truncated flag in the response set by both the total-cell cap and the pre-existing per-hop soft cap. The 4-vs-3 cascade-radius incoherence dissolves with P3/#172 (δ⁰ cascade has no depth cap anymore). Router constants untouched per scope rule (bead 5b58ff). task ci green.
comments.author: rsry-mcp

acceptance_criteria: assert_cochain_complex logs/errors instead of silently returning above cap; NEIGHBORHOOD guard bounds visited-node count not just hops; task ci green

title: Design: signingTime attribute alignment between leyline-sign::cms (Rust) and go-cms (Go)
description:
Cross-runtime spec-drift risk surfaced by overlap detector 2026-07-09.

## The finding

LLO's `leyline-sign::cms` (Rust) and `go-cms` (Go, at `~/remotes/art/go-cms/pkg/cms/`) both implement RFC 5652 + RFC 8419 CMS/PKCS#7 SignedData with Ed25519. Same OIDs, same OpenSSL interop target. **Written independently** — different ASN.1 libraries, different error taxonomies. Not a fork.

**Divergence**: `signingTime` signed attribute (OID 1.2.840.113549.1.9.5):

- **LLO's cms.rs OMITS it** — deliberately, for wasm32 portability (comment lines 8-22: no cross-host time-source contract yet)
- **go-cms INCLUDES it** — with a `TimeSource` interface (operators point at NTP)

## Why this matters

If cloister (Rust, LLO's leyline-sign) and signet (Go, go-cms) both attest to the same content:
- Their CMS blobs will have different signed-attribute sets
- Downstream verifiers seeing "signature from cloister" vs "signature from signet" get semantically different envelopes
- Cross-runtime signature compat is not just algorithmic (both Ed25519) but attribute-shape too

## Options

- **A — Add `signingTime` to LLO behind a `signing-time` feature.** Wasm builds skip it (no time source); host builds enable it (`libc::time` or the caller passes a timestamp). Rust and Go converge on the same signed-attribute set when both are configured for host execution.

- **B — Drop `signingTime` from go-cms.** Signet would lose the attribute. Simpler but a regression for signet consumers who want temporal binding.

- **C — Document the split as intentional.** Cloister-issued signatures don't carry signingTime; signet-issued signatures do. Downstream verifiers accept both. Wire spec docs the divergence.

Recommend **A** — feature-gate the attribute in LLO. Preserves signet's behavior (host cargo build enables the feature) while keeping wasm portable. Small code addition; the signingTime encoder already exists in git history (removed for wasm; can be revived under cfg).

## What this bead does NOT deliver

- **NO changes to go-cms** — signet's own concern
- **NO immediate implementation** — this is a design bead; implementation follows a decision on A/B/C
- **NO changes to cloister-side signatures already in the wild** — this is forward-looking

## Related

- `ley-line-open-d4fdf4` — signet leyline-sign consolidation (LLO-side tracker)
- `signet-d583bd` — signet consolidation PR (accepts drop under path B by default)
- `ley-line-open-a2099a` — LLO wasm publish infra

## Provenance

- Overlap detector agent `aacef19e779782f9c`, 2026-07-09
- User directive: "i think that is all the repos that could overlap [including go-cms]"
- Followup discussion notes go-cms is likely fine as parallel implementation, but the signingTime attribute drift is real
acceptance_criteria: Design decision A/B/C recorded; implementation follows if A; cross-runtime signature attribute set documented in wire spec

title: WAL adversarial: subprocess-isolated ENOSPC/RLIMIT_FSIZE test
description:
Deferred half of bead `ley-line-open-0cdf2d`. Filed 2026-07-08 during that bead's implementation.

## Why this couldn't ship inline

`RLIMIT_FSIZE` is process-scoped. `cargo test` runs integration tests as threads in one process. `tokio::test` with `flavor = "multi_thread"` spawns concurrent runtimes. Any in-process cap on `RLIMIT_FSIZE` leaks to sibling tests that spawn daemons concurrently, causing them to fail against the artificially low quota.

Attempted RAII Drop guard restores original limit at test end, but siblings running IN PARALLEL still see the cap during their execution.

## What this bead delivers

A subprocess-isolated ENOSPC test. Approaches, from cheapest to most robust:

**A. Fork + exec self via `std::process::Command::new(env::current_exe())`** with an env var flag to select just the ENOSPC test. Standard Rust integration-test pattern. Zero new deps. Only downside: re-invokes cargo's test binary, so the child re-runs the runtime init.

**B. Ship a dedicated helper binary** in `rs/ll-open/cli-lib/tests/bin/wal_enospc_helper.rs` (with `[[bin]]` entry in Cargo.toml). Parent uses `Command::spawn`. Child does the cap + boot, exits with a status code. Parent asserts. Cleanest isolation; small `Cargo.toml` change.

**C. `libc::fork` + minimal child that boots the daemon.** Matches F1 crash-test pattern. Complicated by tokio needing to init in the child.

Recommend **A** (subprocess re-invocation with an env flag).

## What the test proves

**Claim**: under an aggressive `RLIMIT_FSIZE` cap (e.g., 4 KiB), a fresh daemon boot in a fresh dir returns Err rather than panicking or silently succeeding. After lifting the cap, boots recover cleanly.

**Assertions**:
- Constrained boot returns Err (or exits with non-zero if the harness is subprocess-based)
- Post-cap boot in fresh dir succeeds

## Provenance

- Parent bead `0cdf2d` — WAL adversarial (ENOSPC + bloat)
- Parent's WAL bloat test shipped inline; the ENOSPC half punted here
- Gate bead `fd07d8` — adversarial testing sweep (closed 2026-07-08 with this as documented deferral)
acceptance_criteria: Subprocess-isolated ENOSPC test lands; test suite doesn't leak RLIMIT to sibling tests; assertion proves daemon returns Err under quota + recovers cleanly post-cap

title: W4/W6: adopt the mache find-smells gate in LLO (commit baseline; blake3 stays local)
description:
Analysis-substrate decade, W4+W6. LLO produces the substrate; now it consumes the gate too. Adopt the mache find-smells GHA + `task smells` local target over LLO's Rust tree (unblocked — extract_rust means node_defs is populated for Rust, so structural rules run), commit a smell baseline (grandfather current debt). Depends on mache W6.1 (mache-4dbad9) + W5.3.
